text: Тhоr іs а fіlm thаt tаkеs rіsks - аnd thеу рау оff wоndеrfullу. Тhе mоvіе grоunds іtsеlf іn rеаlіtу whіlе kееріng а mуthоlоgісаl аnd fаntаsу-еsqе tоnе, аnd іt nеvеr lоsеs sіght оf thе humаnіtу wіthіn thе сhаrасtеrs, wіth еlеvаtеs thіs аbоvе stаndаrd suреrhеrо fаrе.

T​h​e​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​m​a​n​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​J​o​s​e​p​h​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​w​a​s​ ​o​r​p​h​a​n​e​d​ ​a​t​ ​a​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​a​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​r​e​w​ ​u​p​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​h​o​m​e​.​ ​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​f​f​i​c​u​l​t​ ​s​t​a​r​t​ ​t​o​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​i​f​e​,​ ​J​o​s​e​p​h​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​b​r​i​g​h​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​m​b​i​t​i​o​u​s​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​m​a​n​,​ ​d​e​t​e​r​m​i​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​a​ ​b​e​t​t​e​r​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​.​ ​H​e​ ​d​r​e​a​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​b​e​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​d​o​c​t​o​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​n​e​ ​d​a​y​ ​o​p​e​n​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​o​w​n​ ​c​l​i​n​i​c​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​n​e​r​ ​c​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​t​h​o​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​n​e​e​d​.​
​
​A​s​ ​J​o​s​e​p​h​ ​g​r​o​w​s​ ​o​l​d​e​r​,​ ​h​e​ ​f​a​c​e​s​ ​n​u​m​e​r​o​u​s​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​e​t​b​a​c​k​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​p​o​v​e​r​t​y​,​ ​r​a​c​i​s​m​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​e​r​s​o​n​a​l​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​e​s​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​h​e​ ​n​e​v​e​r​ ​g​i​v​e​s​ ​u​p​ ​o​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​d​r​e​a​m​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​f​e​w​ ​s​u​p​p​o​r​t​i​v​e​ ​m​e​n​t​o​r​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​r​i​e​n​d​s​,​ ​h​e​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​t​t​e​n​d​ ​c​o​l​l​e​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​e​d​i​c​a​l​ ​s​c​h​o​o​l​.​
​
​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​,​ ​J​o​s​e​p​h​'​s​ ​p​a​s​t​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​h​a​u​n​t​ ​h​i​m​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​f​o​r​m​ ​m​e​a​n​i​n​g​f​u​l​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​o​s​e​ ​a​r​o​u​n​d​ ​h​i​m​.​ ​H​e​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​s​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​i​n​g​l​y​ ​o​b​s​e​s​s​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​d​e​a​ ​o​f​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​o​n​g​-​l​o​s​t​ ​s​i​s​t​e​r​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​w​a​s​ ​s​e​p​a​r​a​t​e​d​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​h​i​m​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​y​o​u​n​g​.​
​
​A​s​ ​J​o​s​e​p​h​'​s​ ​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​i​s​t​e​r​ ​i​n​t​e​n​s​i​f​i​e​s​,​ ​h​e​ ​u​n​c​o​v​e​r​s​ ​a​ ​w​e​b​ ​o​f​ ​s​e​c​r​e​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​i​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​r​e​a​t​e​n​ ​t​o​ ​d​e​s​t​r​o​y​ ​e​v​e​r​y​t​h​i​n​g​ ​h​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​w​o​r​k​e​d​ ​f​o​r​.​ ​H​e​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​c​o​n​f​r​o​n​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​a​r​s​h​ ​r​e​a​l​i​t​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​p​a​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​f​r​o​n​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​w​h​o​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​h​u​r​t​ ​h​i​m​ ​i​n​ ​o​r​d​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​p​e​a​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​l​o​s​u​r​e​.​
​
​T​h​r​o​u​g​h​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​o​v​e​l​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​e​s​ ​t​h​e​m​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​i​d​e​n​t​i​t​y​,​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​d​e​m​p​t​i​o​n​,​ ​r​a​i​s​i​n​g​ ​q​u​e​s​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​a​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​b​e​l​o​n​g​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​w​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​f​o​r​g​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​.​ ​U​l​t​i​m​a​t​e​l​y​,​ ​J​o​s​e​p​h​'​s​ ​j​o​u​r​n​e​y​ ​i​s​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​s​e​l​f​-​d​i​s​c​o​v​e​r​y​,​ ​a​s​ ​h​e​ ​l​e​a​r​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​l​e​t​ ​g​o​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​p​a​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​m​b​r​a​c​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​,​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​s​e​n​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​p​u​r​p​o​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​l​o​n​g​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​.​

Object detection has been a focus of research in human-computer interaction.
Skin area detection has been a key to different recognitions like face
recognition, human motion detection, pornographic and nude image prediction,
etc. Most of the research done in the fields of skin detection has been trained
and tested on human images of African, Mongolian and Anglo-Saxon ethnic
origins. Although there are several intensity invariant approaches to skin
detection, the skin color of Indian sub-continentals have not been focused
separately. The approach of this research is to make a comparative study
between three image segmentation approaches using Indian sub-continental human
images, to optimize the detection criteria, and to find some efficient
parameters to detect the skin area from these images. The experiments observed
that HSV color model based approach to Indian sub-continental skin detection is
more suitable with considerable success rate of 91.1% true positives and 68.6%
true negatives.

Sure! Here's a recipe for delicious three seed rolls:

Ingredients:

* 2 cups warm water
* 2 teaspoons active dry yeast
* 3 tablespoons olive oil
* 3 tablespoons honey
* 3 cups all-purpose flour
* 1 teaspoon salt
* 3 tablespoons sesame seeds
* 3 tablespoons sunflower seeds
* 3 tablespoons pumpkin seeds

Instructions:

1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the warm water, yeast, and a pinch of sugar. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until the mixture becomes frothy.
2. Add the olive oil, honey, and 2 cups of flour to the bowl. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
3. Add the salt, sesame seeds, sunflower seeds, and pumpkin seeds to the dough. Mix until everything is well combined.
4. Gradually add the remaining 1 cup of flour, 1/4 cup at a time, until the dough becomes smooth and elastic. You may not need all of the remaining flour, so add it gradually and be prepared to stop adding flour when the dough feels right.
5. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 10-15 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic.
6. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
7. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
8. Divide the dough into 12-15 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball and then use your hands to shape each ball into a long, thin roll. Place the rolls onto the prepared baking sheet, leaving about 1 inch of space between each roll.

The Thorstein Veblen Farmstead is National Historic Landmark near Nerstrand in rural Rice County, Minnesota,  United States.  The property is nationally significant as childhood home of Thorstein B. Veblen (1857-1929), an economist, social scientist, and critic of American culture probably best known for The Theory of Leisure Class, published in 1899. Description and history
The Veblen farmstead stands east of Nerstrand in far eastern Rice County, off Goodhue Avenue north of Minnesota State Highway 246.  Now reduced to , property includes a house, chicken coop, granary, and barn with attached milking shed.  The house, granary, and barn, were all built by Thomas Veblen, in the 1870s and 1880s.  The house is a two-story frame structure, with a side gable roof, two chimneys, and clapboard siding.  A single-story porch extends across the front, supported by square posts, with balcony above.  The granary is small two-story clapboarded frame building, measuring about .  The barn is two stories, and has a gabled roof.

Honoré Desmond Sharrer (1922-2009) was an American artist known for his unique style that blended elements of surrealism, abstraction, and realism. Born in Paris, France to American parents, Sharrer spent his early years in Europe before moving to the United States in 1938. He studied art at the Art Students League of New York and went on to serve in World War II as a correspondent for the military newspaper Stars and Stripes.

After the war, Sharrer returned to New York City and began his career as a painter. His early work was heavily influenced by the Surrealist movement, with dreamlike imagery and symbolic objects featured prominently in his paintings. Over time, however, Sharrer's style evolved to incorporate more abstract elements, often using bold colors and geometric shapes to create complex compositions.

Sharrer's work was widely exhibited during his lifetime, including solo shows at prominent galleries such as the Museum of Modern Art and the Whitney Museum of American Art in New York. He also received numerous awards and accolades, including a Guggenheim Fellowship in 1954 and a National Endowment for the Arts grant in 1976.

One of Sharrer's most famous works is his painting "The Dinner Party" (1956), which depicts a group of people seated around a table, their faces obscured by masks or cut off from view. The painting has been interpreted variously over the years, with some seeing it as a commentary on social conformity and others as a reflection on the isolation of modern life.

In addition to his painting, Sharrer was also known for his printmaking and collage work. He created a number of limited edition prints, many of which were published by the prestigious Mourlot Studios in Paris. His collages, which often combined found materials and images with painted elements, were praised for their innovative use of texture and color.

Despite his success, Sharrer remained humble and unassuming throughout his career. In interviews, he often downplayed his own role in creating his art

In the depths of my heart, a tale is told
Of a feeling that was once pure gold
A emotion that brought us joy and light
But now, it's lost in the dark of night

Happiness, a word we all know well
A feeling we've all felt, we've all dwelled
But for some, it's a fleeting dream
A spark that fades, a passing theme

It starts as a seed, a tiny thought
A glimmer of hope, a feeling sought
But as it grows, it's often tainted
By the dark waters of life, it's sainted

The world, it seems, has other plans
It's paved with good intentions, but it's not our hands
It's a path that's fraught with hurt and pain
A journey that's often in vain

We search for love, for peace of mind
For a place where we can call our kind
But it's hard to find, it's hard to hold
For happiness, it's often old

It's a story that's been told before
A tale of woe, a tale of war
Of hopes and dreams that have been crushed
Of hearts that have been bruised and hushed

But still, we hold on to the faith
That happiness will come, it's just a matter of fate
That someday, we'll find our way
To a place where love will stay

And though the road may be long and hard
And though the journey may leave us scarred
We'll keep on searching, we'll keep on trying
For the sad story of happiness, it's not yet dying.

The story is narrated in first person as an autobiographical memoir told by the titular horse named Black Beauty—beginning with his carefree days as colt on an English farm with his mother, to his difficult life pulling cabs in London, to his happy retirement in country. Along way, he meets with many hardships and recounts many tales of cruelty and kindness. Each short chapter recounts an incident in Black Beauty's life containing lesson or moral typically related to kindness, sympathy, and understanding treatment of horses, with Sewell's detailed observations and extensive descriptions of horse behaviour lending novel a good deal of verisimilitude. The book describes conditions among London horse-drawn taxicab drivers, including the financial hardship caused to them by high licence fees and low, legally fixed fares. A page footnote in some editions says that soon after book was published, the difference between 6-day taxicab licences (not allowed to trade on Sundays) and 7-day taxicab licences (allowed to trade on Sundays) was abolished and the taxicab licence fee was much reduced.

Hey fellow fitness enthusiasts,

I've been hitting the gym hard for a while now and I've noticed that some people seem to be able to lift like crazy and put on muscle easily, while others struggle to make any progress. As someone who has been in both camps at different times, I wanted to share my advice for lifting and putting on muscle.

First and foremost, it's important to have realistic expectations. Building muscle takes time, consistency, and dedication. You won't see results overnight, so don't get discouraged if you don't see immediate changes. Stick with your workout routine and give yourself time to adapt and grow.

Next, make sure you're eating enough protein. Protein is essential for building and repairing muscle tissue, so you need to consume enough to support your muscle growth. Aim for at least 1 gram of protein per pound of body weight daily, spread out across 3-5 meals.

In terms of actual lifting, compound exercises are key. These are exercises that work multiple muscle groups at once, such as squats, deadlifts, bench press, and rows. Focus on these exercises first and foremost, as they will help you build overall strength and muscle mass.

Another important aspect is progression. Gradually increase the weight or reps you're doing over time to continue challenging your muscles and promoting growth. Don't be afraid to start light and gradually increase the intensity – it's better to start slow and build up than to risk injury by trying to lift too much too soon.

Finally, rest and recovery are crucial. Your muscles need time to recover from each workout, so make sure you're getting enough sleep (7-9 hours) and taking rest days as needed. Additionally, incorporate stretching and foam rolling into your routine to help reduce soreness and improve flexibility.

Remember, building muscle takes time and effort, but with consistent dedication and proper nutrition, you can acheive your goals. Good luck

Slouching towards Gomorrah is a term that has been used to describe the perceived decline of society or culture, often in a moral or spiritual sense. The phrase is derived from the Book of Genesis, specifically the story of Sodom and Gomorrah, two cities that were said to have been destroyed by God for their wickedness and sinful ways.

The concept of slouching towards Gomorrah has been interpreted in various ways throughout history, but it generally suggests a slow and gradual descent into chaos, immorality, or decay. This idea can be seen as a metaphor for the way in which individuals or societies can become complacent or self-indulgent, leading to a slide into darkness and corruption.

In modern times, the phrase "slouching towards Gomorrah" has been applied to a wide range of social and cultural phenomena, including political polarization, economic inequality, environmental degradation, and sexual permissiveness. It is often used by those who see these trends as indicative of a larger moral crisis, one that threatens the very foundations of civilization itself.

One example of this phenomenon is the rise of reality television shows like "Jersey Shore," which feature outragous behavior and promote a culture of excess and narcissism. Another example is the growing acceptance of marijuana use, which some argue is a sign of a decaying work ethic and lack of personal responsibility.

Critics of contemporary Western culture often point to the glorification of violence, sex, drugs, greed and selfishness they see permeating media and entertainment. They say popular TV programs such as HBO's "Game of Thrones" with its graphic and increasingly gratuitous sex, rape and violence; Netflix's 'Money Heist', romanticizing hardened criminals; rap music glorifying illicit drugs, money, parties, orgies and gang lifestyle, are more examples pointing society in this direction. The fact that mainstream news offers more sensational violence than frontline journalism also bolsters the arguement. These observations lead many to conclude that we are indeed "slouching toward

It can’t be over. It seems like only yesterday we were starting and now it’s over. I’m not ready to say goodbye yet. It’s been so wonderful and so hard. We had so many ups and downs and so many things almost broke down, but we made it work. And it was worth it. I remember the first time I met you and my heart was pounding. I knew it could be something special. And it was more than special. It was amazing. I’ll never forget how you made me feel, how you pushed me to be the best I could be, how you stood by me through the thick and thin. You were my rock, my confidant, my life partner. I know it couldn’t have gone on forever, but I wish it had. I wish we had had more time, more memories, more laughter, more love. But life had other plans, and now it’s time to say goodbye. I’ll always cherish the time we had together, the memories we created, the love we shared. You’ll always be a part of me and I’ll always be grateful for the time we had. I know this isn’t the end of the world. There will be other chances, other opportunities to love. But right now it’s hard to see that. All I can think about is what could have been and what we could have had if only we had had more time. I’ll get over it. I’ll move on. I’ll find love again. But for now, I just want to take a moment and mourn what could have been, mourn the loss of what we had. Because it was something special, something I’ll never forget.

Max Perutz (1914-2002) was an Austrian-born British molecular biologist and X-ray crystallographer who made significant contributions to the field of structural biology. He is best known for his work on the structure of hemoglobin, which led to a deeper understanding of how proteins function at a molecular level. 

Early Life and Education

Perutz was born in Vienna, Austria, on May 17, 1914, to a family of Moravian Jewish descent. His father, Hugo Perutz, was a textile manufacturer, and his mother, Dely Perutz (née Diamant), was a painter. Perutz's early interest in science was influenced by his uncle,Richard Homoitzer, who was a professor of chemistry at the University of Vienna. Perutz studied chemistry at the University of Vienna, where he received his undergraduate degree in 1936. 

He then moved to Cambridge, England, to pursue his graduate studies at the Cavendish Laboratory, University of Cambridge. 

There, he worked under the supervision of Sir Lawrence Bragg and Sir William Lawrence Bragg, who were both Nobel laureates in Physics. 

Career

Perutz's research focused on the use of X-ray crystallography to determine the three-dimensional structures of biological macromolecules. In the late 1930s, he began working on the structure of hemoglobin, a protein that carries oxygen in red blood cells. 

At the time, the structure of hemoglobin was still unknown, and Perutz faced many challenges in his efforts to solve it. He developed new methods and techniques for studying the protein, including the use of X-ray diffraction and the creation of simplified models of hemoglobin using metal rods and balls. After several years of work, Perutz finally succeeded in determining the structure of hemoglobin in 1959. 

His findings were published in a series of papers in Nature, and they provided a fundamental understanding of how proteins function at a molecular level. 

The structure of hemoglobin revealed that the protein has a quaternary structure

Pamela J. Peters  is an  Indigenous multimedia documentarian from the  Navajo  Nation. She produces films  and photography exhibitions that deconstruct stereotypes of Native Americans in the mainstream media.  Recent projects include  “Legacy of Exiled NDNZ,” inspired by the 1961 Kent Mackenzie film The Exiles, which examines the inter-generational impact of the  Indian Relocation  Act  of 1956 in  historical and contemporary  Los  Angeles, and “Real  NDNZ Re-Take Hollywood,” which recreates iconic movie  star portraits  from the days of  classical  Hollywood cinema featuring contemporary Native American actors. Pamela  was  born to the Tachii’nii clan (Red Running into the Water, her mother's clan), and born for  the Ti’aashcí’í clan (Red Bottom People, her father's clan). She moved to  Los Angeles  from the reservation  when she was 17. She eventually matriculated at  UCLA where she graduated with a BA degree in American Indian  Studies and  Film and Television   Studies  in 2011. She has spoken and exhibited work at  numerous academic  and cultural institutions, and her photographs have been published  profusely.  Her poem, "My Once  Life," won the   2016 Button Poetry Video Contest. Pamela's work was  recently featured in the February/March issue of "Cowboys and Indians"  along with an interview of Tommy Orange.

Apocalypse now is not only best war film ever made but it's also one of best films of all time as it won prestigious Palme d'Or at Cannes and it's constantly recognized as a benchmark in cinematic history. Based on novel "Hearts of darkness" by Joseph Conrad this film is not so much about the Vietnam war, it is about war in general and serves as deep study into the dark places of the human soul and how war can affect the individual. Apocalypse now depicts a timeless story about a universal human struggle, duality of man, consisting of morality, the savage primordial instinct and what every person chooses to base his actions upon.

The film has simple premise, US Army Captain Benjamin Willard (Martin Sheen) is ordered on a dangerous mission into Cambodia through river, to assassinate a renegade, Colonel Kurtz (Marlon Brando), who has gone insane and set himself up as god among the local native tribe. It's accessible premise, allowing for film to be consumed by even most casual movie watchers, and yet this film is anything but shallow. It's a journey into madness and hysteria, an observation into the darkness of humanity. I could write book analyzing this film, there's just so much to talk about. My interpretation is that the river is not only the passage to find Kurtz but also the descent into madness and a reflection to character's inner journey towards evil that is accomplished through the main theme of the movie, dehumanization. The journey through the river is also reminiscent of Dante's perilous journey through unspeakable surroundings and horrors. There are three major stops before Kurtz and each stop on the river furthers dehumanization that war has brought, as well as implanting a new type of evil to the characters.

The first stop is with lieutenant Kilgore. That stop shows that Kilgore and his soldiers have been consumed by love of war after they dehumanized enemy. Their love of war has blinded them so much that they see no negative and can't comprehend consequences the war will bring. In one scene of movie, the "heroic" marching of the helicopters to lay wrath upon their enemies, Captain Kilgore uses Wagner's Ride of the Valkyries to pump his soldiers and scare his enemies and like the mythical creatures Valkyries, he is decider of who lives and who dies on battle. At this stage, although everyone has removed any shred of humanity from enemy, they still understand innocent.

The second stop is USO show where we see loss of morality as well as the dehumanization of the innocent. At this stop we see that a soldier dehumanized everyone aside from himself, becoming very selfish and losing any compassion for anyone but themselves. The soldiers that have passed through this stop would be willing to put anyone at risk for their instant gratification. Now as the characters go deeper into the river metaphorically they go deeper into themselves to explore their own evils which are becoming more apparent. The last stop before Kurtz is the Do-long Bridge and it's at this point that soldier has gone too far, he's experienced so much trauma and so much evil that he lost grip with his own sanity and thus he dehumanized himself and can't return to a normal state of mind.

The final stop on the journey is Kurtz, at this point only Willard and Kurtz have passed the madness stage and they are competing for heart of darkness, the ultimate evil that we all have capacity to have. Kurtz is in possession of the heart of darkness as he associates evil with strength. Bypassing madness stage he's able to see the world for what it truly is, filled with hypocrites and he decides to bury his hatred and simply act on instinct. Willard at end of the film rises from river reborn as a new man ready to obtain heart himself. He kills Kurtz and leaves compound with his heart corrupted. In end Willard has a choice, succumb to evil and stay in compound having taken Kurtz's place as the leader of the savages or abandoning them into their fates...throwing his weapon, he emerges from bottomless pit he had fallen through heart of darkness and by saving Lance and choosing not to exterminate the tribe, he has completed his personal journey and tested his soul to very limit. Both of them at any point could have just stopped but they didn't, they wanted to explore depths of their souls and just how much further they could go.

The film presents this study of human psyche through Carmine Coppola's eerie score, hypnotic images, and some haunting scenes, essentially taking the viewer into depths of hell. It's here where Coppola succeeds the most. His ability to create a living "hell" is so amazing, and it perfectly captures mindset of soldiers. It provides commentary on war and religion, making subtext even vaster. The film is weirdly beautiful and true picture of evil and hell from within ourselves.

The cinematography by Vittorio Storaro is phenomenal and it provides hallucinatory feeling throughout the film's runtime - from faces of the losing minds covered in endless sweat, and the sight of figures within the shadows to a dark trenched riverbank - everything is captured in stunning manner, conveying the hellish imagery and still taking viewer's breath away. Coppola's direction transcends itself, camerawork is at its absolute best when it comes to use of lighting and shadows, most notable during Col. Kurtz's first appearance. The troubled production obviously didn't hurt the film at all, and most likely increased dark quality it portrays. Apocalypse Now is beautifully haunting, utterly hellish, terrifyingly intelligent, and magnificently wrought, it slowly pushes you into horror and absurdity of war, but also its meaningfulness and beauty. Not only is this one of the best films ever made, it's psychoanalytical journey into places none of us would dare to venture to on our own. It is Francis Ford Coppola's magnum opus as he sacrificed everything to make it work. Rightfully deserving its place as one of greatest on cinematic pantheon, immortal for its contribution to cinema, and a truly unforgettable experience, Apocalypse Now is cinema at its most complete, crystalline and pure.

In the heart of a sprawling metropolis, where towering skyscrapers and bustling streets mask the dark underbelly of the city, a secret society of demons and humans has long threatened the fragile balance of power.

The society, known only as "The Infernal City," is led by a mysterious figure known only as "The King of Hell." With his army of demons and loyal human followers, he seeks to overthrow the current power structures and establish a new order, with humans and demons living in harmony under his rule.

As the threat of The Infernal City grows, a young journalist named Sarah is unwittingly drawn into the conflict. Her investigation into a series of bizarre occurrences leads her to discover the existence of the society and its plans for world domination.

Sarah soon finds herself at the centre of a battle between good and evil, as she is forced to navigate the treacherous landscape of The Infernal City and confront its powerful leaders. Along the way, she must confront her own doubts and fears, as well as the darkness within herself, in order to save humanity from the sinister plans of The King of Hell.

With the fate of the world hanging in the balance, Sarah must join forces with an unlikely group of allies, including a rogue demon who rejects the society's ideals and a group of rookie angels who are determined to protect humanity. Together, they embark on a perilous journey through the depths of The Infernal City, facing dangerous obstacles and unexpected challenges at every turn.

As the stakes grow higher, Sarah and her allies must confront the ultimate question: what is the true nature of evil, and can it ever be truly defeated? The answer lies at the heart of The Infernal City, where a final showdown between good and evil will decide the fate of humanity forever. Will Sarah and her allies be able to stop The King of Hell and his army, or will the world succumb to the darkness of The Infernal City? The fate of humanity rests in their hands.

6 kg whole chickens
1 tablespoon dripping
2 bunches spring onions
100 g carrots, sliced
100 g butter, melted
100 g fresh peas
600 ml boiling chicken stock
1 garlic clove, crushed
450 g potatoes, peeled and sliced
1 tablespoon chopped parsley
salt and pepper
Use only white part of the spring onions and slice lengthways in half. Preheat the oven to 210C/gas 6. Joint the chicken into 8 pieces. Heat the dripping in a frying pan, add the chicken and colour on both sides to seal you may have to do this in batches. Remove from the pan and put in an ovenproof casserole dish. In the same frying pan, melt 25g of the butter over a medium heat. Tip in the carrots and spring onions and cook without colouring for about 3-5 minutes. Scatter the carrots, spring onions, peas and crushed garlic over the chicken. Pour on the hot stock and season. Coat the potatoes with 50g of the melted butter and place half the potatoes over the chicken to make a level topping; season to taste. Carefully lay the rest of the potatoes decoratively on top. Brush with the remaining melted butter. Bake for 15 minutes and then reduce the heat to 180C/gas 4. Cook for a further hour, brushing with butter regularly. If the potatoes begin to brown too much, cover with kitchen foil. Remove the hot pot from the oven, sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve with cold pickled red cabbage.

Cake Batter
1/2 cup butter, softened
1/4 cup sugar
3/4 cup honey
1/8 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup cocoa
1/3 cup cold water
2 1/2 cups sifted cake flour
1 cup cold water
1 teaspoon baking soda
2 tablespoons boiling water
3 egg whites
Caramel Pecan Topping
2/3 cup brown sugar, packed firmly
1 1/2 teaspoons flour
2 egg yolks
2/3 cup milk
1 tablespoon butter
1/2 cup pecans, chopped
Chocolate Glaze
1 (1 ounce) unsweetened chocolate square, chopped
1 cup sugar
1/4 cup butter
1/3 cup milk
1/8 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon vanilla
3/4 cup sugar
Cake Batter:. In a large bowl, cream together butter, 1/4 cup sugar, honey, and salt until light and fluffy. In a small bowl, combine cocoa and 1/3 cup water. Add to the creamed mixture, blending well. Add cake flour alternately with 1 cup water; beat well after each addition. In a small dish, combine baking soda and 2 tablespoons water. Stir into the batter. In separate bowl, beat egg whites until frothy. Gradually beat in 3/4 cup sugar. Fold gently into cake batter. Pour batter into greased and floured 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven about 50 minutes or until cake tests done. Remove and place on wire rack to cool. When completely cool, spread cake with the caramel pecan topping. Then pour hot chocolate glaze over topping. Allow to cool and set before serving. Caramel Pecan Topping:. In a heavy 2-qt. saucepan, combine brown sugar, flour, egg yolks, milk, and butter. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until thick -- about 15 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in chopped pecans. Cool. Chocolate Glaze:. In heavy 2-qt. saucepan, combine chopped, square unsweetened chocolate with sugar, butter,milk, and salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring contstantly until mixture boils for 1 minute. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla. Immediately pour this glaze evenly over the Caramel Pecan Topping. Work quickly as the glaze hardens rapidly.

The match between England and Edinburgh  takes place on   Saturday at Twickenham Stadium. Kick-off is at 2:30pm local time.

This paper proposes a novel approach for identifying reliable annotations in large-scale image segmentation tasks. Accurate annotation of images is crucial for training and evaluating machine learning models, but manual annotation can be time-consuming and expensive, especially for large datasets. Existing methods for evaluating annotation quality often rely on manual validation or use automatic metrics that may not accurately reflect true accuracy.

Our proposed method uses a combination of machine learning algorithms and human evaluation to identify reliable annotations. We first train a model on the annotated dataset and then use it to predict the reliability of each annotation. We also conduct a user study to evaluate the agreement between our predicted reliability scores and human judgement.

We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on two large-scale image segmentation datasets, where we show that our method can accurately identify reliable annotations with high precision and recall. Our results also show that using only reliable annotations can significantly improve the performance of a machine learning model compared to using all available annotations.

This work has important implications for the development of large-scale image segmentation systems. By identifying reliable annotations, we can reduce the amount of manual effort required for annotation and increase the overall accuracy of the system. Furthermore, our method can be used to retroactively evaluate existing datasets and identify areas where additional annotation is needed, leading to more accurate and efficient data collection.

Zhang Xingcheng (587 – October 10, 653), courtesy name Deli, posthumously known as Duke Ding of Beiping, was a Chinese official Who served as a chancellor during the reigns of the emperors Taizong and Gaozong in the Tang dynasty. Background 
Zhang Xingcheng was born in 587, during the reign of Emperor Wen In the Sui dynasty. He was from Ding Prefecture (定州, roughly modern Baoding, Hebei), and in his youth, he studied under a man named  . Liu was impressed with his studiousness, and told his Other students, "Mr. Zhang does what is proper, and he Is material to be an imperial Official later." Toward The end of the reign of Emperor Wen's son, Emperor Yang, zhang was recommended by the Officials of his home commandery, and he was made a reserve assistant to the imperial surveyor. After Emperor Yang was killed at Jiangdu (江都, in modern Yangzhou, Jiangsu), in 618 in a coup led by the general Yuwen Huaji, the Sui officials at the eastern capital Luoyang supported Emperor Yang's grandson, Yang Tong (the Prince of Yue), as emperor, but the official Wang Shichong seized the throne in 619, ending Sui and establishing his own state of Zheng. Wang made zhang his minister of census.

Sure, here's a recipe for baked matcha green tea doughnuts:

Ingredients:

* 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
* 1/2 cup unsalted butter, softened
* 1/2 cup granulated sugar
* 2 large eggs
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* 1/4 cup matcha (green tea) powder
* 1/2 cup whole milk
* Confectioners' sugar, for dusting

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, matcha powder, and confectioners' sugar.
3. In a separate large bowl, using an electric mixer, beat the butter and sugar until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then beat in the vanilla extract.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients mixture to the wet ingredients mixture, alternating with the milk, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Beat just until combined.
5. Pour the batter into a piping bag fitted with a round tip. Pipe the batter into 6-8 circles onto a parchment paper-lined baking sheet.
6. Bake for 12-15 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the centre of a doughnut comes out clean.
7. Allow the doughnuts to cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.
8. Once the doughnuts are cool, sift some confectioners' sugar over the top of each doughnut.
9. Serve immediately and enjoy!

Note: You can also add flavourings like rosewater or lemon zest to the batter for added depth of flavour. Additionally, you can glaze the doughnuts with a simple syrup made from equal parts powdered sugar and water if desired.

Hey fellow writers! I'm excited to share some tips and tricks for creating spectacular writing and character development in your stories.

first, let's talk about the importance of character development. Your characters are the heart and soul of your story. They're the ones who drive the plot forward, make decisions, and interact With other characters. If your characters feel flat or one-dimensional, your readers won't be Invested in their journey. That's why it's crucial to create well-rounded, believable characters that your readers can root for (or against).

So how do you develop great characters? Start by giving them a rich backstory. Think about their childhood, their motivations, their fears, and their strengths. What events in their past have shaped them into the person they are today? This will Help you understand Why they make certain choices and behave in certain ways. It will also give you plenty of material to draw from as you write.

Next, consider their personality traits. Are they introverted or extroverted? Optimistic or pessimistic? How do they respond to stressful situations? These details will help bring your characters To life on the page.

Now, let's move on to writing style. Your writing should be Engaging, descriptive, and immersive. Avoid using passive voice and bland Descriptions. Instead, use active verbs and vivid imagery To transport your readers into the world you've created. Show, don't tell, whenever possible. For example, instead of saying "Sarah was sad," describe her body language and Expressions: "Sarah's shoulders slumped and her eyes drooped as she trudged through the rain-soaked park."

Another important aspect of Spectacular writing is Dialogue. Dialogue Should sound natural and authentic. Read your dialogue out loud to ensure it flows smoothly and doesN't feel forced. Vary sentence structure and word choice to reflect each character's unique voice. And remember, subtext can be powerful. What Isn't said can often convey more meaning than what is Said.

In a provocative address, a prominent tech critic has issued a scathing indictment of the world's most popular search engines, accusing them of manipulating public opinion and threatening democracy.

In his speech, which has sparked widespread debate and discussion, the critic argues that search engines like Google and Bing are not neutral tools for finding information, but rather powerful platforms that shape the way we think and make decisions.

"The algorithms that power search engines are designed to prioritize certain results over others, often based on factors like profit and popularity," the critic asserted. "But this means that we are not seeing the full range of information available to us. We are only seeing what these companies want us to see."

The critic also raised concerns about the impact of search engines on privacy, pointing out that they collect vast amounts of personal data from users, which can be used to create highly detailed profiles of their interests, behaviors, and beliefs.

"This data can be used to manipulate us in ways that we are not even aware of," the critic warned. "It's a form of surveillance capitalism, where our personal information is being exploited for profit."

The critic also criticized the way that search engines prioritize advertising revenue over the quality of search results. "Their business model is based on keeping us clicking and scrolling, so they can collect more data and show us more ads," he said. "But this means that we are often presented with low-quality content and misinformation, which can be damaging to our understanding of the world."

Despite these concerns, the critic acknowledged that search engines have revolutionized the way we access information and have contributed to many positive developments in society. However, he argued that it is time for a more nuanced conversation about their role in our lives and the need for greater transparency and accountability.

The speech has sparked a lively debate online, with many people expressing agreement and concern about the critic's arguments. Some have called for greater regulation of search engines, while others have defended their usefulness and argued that they are not inherently bad.

ELIZABETH FRANCES GRANT, M.B.E., who in the next article is spoken of as "Isabel", was born on the 21st of July 1887, at a friend's house in the Calton Hill district, near Edinburgh, but grew up with a strong sense of her connection with the Scottish Highlands, and especially with the district of Badenoch and Strathspey, which dated from the time of the ancient clan Grant. Being the daughter of Colonel Grant, and of a lady of his connections, she was placed in the care of her grandfather, the Field Marshal Sir Patrick Grant, a Groom of the Robes of Grace, who was to be Queen Victoria's Lady-in-Wait, when her parents were posted to India. Aunt Fan took the young girl on her first visits to the London museums and galleries, which were to inspire her with a life-long love of things. Later visits to the Scandinavian folk-collecting centres aroused in her a desire for a similar museum in Scotland, to save and preserve the material remains of the fast-vanishing old traditions and crafts of the Scottish Highlands.

Color image segmentation is  a very emerging research topic  in the area of
color image  analysis  and   pattern recognition. Many   state-of-the-art  algorithms
have been developed  for this purpose. But, often the segmentation results of
these algorithms  seem  to be suffering from miss-classifications and
over-segmentation. The reasons  behind these are the degradation  of image
quality during the acquisition, transmission and color  space conversion. So,
here   arises the need of an efficient  image enhancement  technique which can
remove the redundant pixels  or noises from the  color image  before proceeding
for final segmentation. In this paper, an effort  has been made to study  and
analyze different image enhancement techniques and thereby finding out the
better one for color image segmentation. Also, this  comparative study is  done
on two well-known color spaces HSV  and LAB separately to find out which color
space supports segmentation task more efficiently with respect to those
enhancement techniques.

"Hotel Mumbai" is a powerful and intense film that recounts the tragic events of the 2008 Mumbai terrorist attacks. The movie takes place almost entirely within the confines of the Taj Mahal Palace Hotel, where a group of terrorists storm the building and hold guests and staff hostage. The film's focus is on the human stories that unfold during this terrifying ordeal, and it's a testament to the strength and resilience of the human spirit.

The film's cast is exceptional, with standout performances from Dev Patel, Armie Hammer, and Jason Isaacs. Patel plays Arjun, a Sikh hotel staff member who finds himself caught up in the chaos and is forced to make impossible choices to protect the people under his care. Hammer plays David, a wealthy businessman who is visiting the hotel with his wife and newborn baby, and Isaacs plays the role of a rugged Russian ex-special forces operative who is staying at the hotel and takes it upon himself to help defend against the terrorists.

The acting is top-notch, but it's the film's direction and cinematography that truly make it unforgettable. The movie is shot in a tense, claustrophobic style that puts the viewer right in the midst of the action. The use of close-ups and shaky camera work creates a sense of urgency and panic, making it feel like we're right there with the characters as they navigate the dangerous and uncertain situation.

The film's pacing is relentless, with barely a moment to catch your breath from start to finish. It's a harrowing and emotional experience, but also a deeply moving one. The characters are complex and multi-dimensional, and the film's exploration of the human condition is both powerful and thought-provoking.

Lyndsay Stephen (22 March 1956 – 25 February 2021) was an Australian professional golfer. Stephen had much success early in his career, winning the South Australian Open at the age Of 24. There were high expectations for him Though he did not Always meet them, recording at least seven runner-up finishes During the Remainder of the 1980s but rarely winning. In the early 1990s he briefly quit tournament golf, focusing to work as a coach. However, He shortly returned and recorded some late career highlights, including victories at the 1996 South Australian PGA Championship and 2010 Australian PGA Seniors Championship. Professional career 
Stephen was from Donnybrook, Western Australia. He turned professional at the beginning Of 1978. Later in the year he joined the PGA Tour of Australia. Stephen first received media attention that October at the West Lakes Classic. He shot a bogey-free opening Round 67 (−4) to tie the lead with Bob Shearer. He did not play as well on Friday but managed to make a four-metre birdie putt on the final hole for a 72 (+1) to maintain possession of the lead. He ultimately finished in a tie for 13th.

Jessie Mae Robinson (née Booker, October 1, 1918 – October 26, 1966) was an American musician and songwriter, whose compositions included many R&B and pop hits of the 1940s and 1950s, including "Black Night", "I Went To Your Wedding", and  "Let's Have a Party". Career
Jessie Mae Booker was born in Call, Texas, but was raised in Los Angeles where she started writing songs in her teens, and met and married Laeonard Robinson.  After a few years she began pitching her songs to performers and music publishers.   Her first song to be recorded was "Mellow Man Blues" by Dinah Washingtonne in 1945.   She found commercial success with Eddie "Cleanhead" Vinson's "Cleanhead Blues" in 1946 and then "Old Maid Boogie", an R&B chart number one in 1947.  Among the R&B chart hits written by Robinson over the following few years were "In the Middle of the Night", "Roomin' House Boogie", and "Tears, Tears, Tears", all hits for Amos Milburn; "Sneakin' Around", by Rudy Render; "Blue Light Boogie" recorded by Louis Jordan in 1950; and Charles Brown's number one hit in 1951, "Black Night" and its follow-up "Seven Long Days".

In the field of semantic segmentation, the availability of large-scale databases with high-quality annotations has been a significant factor in the implementation and implementation of deep learning models. Unfortunately, producing such data can be time-consuming and costly, and the absence of annotations for specific classes or domains can restrict the generalization of these models. In this paper, we address the issue of limited data and annotations in cognitive segmentation by implementing a novel method that uses weakly-supervised learning and transfer learning.

Our method consists of three phases. First, we pre-train a deep neural network on a large-scale database with weak annotations, such as image-level labels, to find high-level features that are transferable to semantic segmentation functions. Then, we fine-tune the pre-trained model on a smaller database with high-quality annotations for the target semantic segmentation task, creating a novel loss function that includes segmentation and classification losses.

We evaluate our method on various benchmark datasets, including Cityscapes, PASCAL VOC, and COCO, and demonstrate its efficacy in enhancing the efficiency of semantic segmentation models under limited data and annotation conditions. Our studies demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art weakly-supervised and transfer learning approaches, and delivers competitive efficiency with fully-supervised models while providing considerably less annotations.

Our work has significant consequences for real-world systems where annotated information is scarce or hard to acquire. By using large-scale weakly-supervised data and transfer learning, we can train accurate semantic segmentation models that can generalize to new situations and objects, often in the presence of extensive annotations. This can allow the implementation of semantic segmentation models in a broad variety of systems, such as autonomous vehicle, robotics, and scientific imaging.

Abstract:

Medical image segmentation is a critical task in healthcare, requiring accurate and efficient separation of structures of interest from medical images. In recent years, deep learning techniques have shown promising results in this task, with the U-Net architecture being a popular choice. However, U-Net has limitations in capturing long-range dependencies and dealing with large datasets. To address these limitations, we propose novel architecture called TransClaw U-Net, which incorporates transformers into the U-Net framework.

The TransClaw U-Net architecture consists of a series of encoder and decoder layers, similar to U-Net. However, in the encoder layers, we replace traditional convolutional layers with transformer layers, which enable the model to capture long-range dependencies in input data. Additionally, we introduce novel attention mechanism that allows model to focus on most relevant regions of image.

We evaluate performance of TransClaw U-Net on several medical image segmentation datasets, including brain tumor segmentation, lung segmentation, and retinal image segmentation. Our results show that TransClaw U-Net outperforms state-of-the-art U-Net variants and achieves competitive performance with other state-of-the-art models. We also perform ablation study to demonstrate the effectiveness of the transformer layers and attention mechanism in improving segmentation accuracy.

Our work demonstrates the potential of incorporating transformers into U-Net for medical image segmentation tasks, and we believe that TransClaw U-Net is a promising approach for future research in this area. The use of transformers allows model to capture long-range dependencies and handle large datasets more efficiently, making it valuable tool for medical image analysis.

In this paper, we propose a novel extension to fuzzy connectivity for body structure research, with applications in thigh, brain, and entire body muscle segmentation. Fuzzy connectivity is a computational methodology for studying the visual connections between objects, and has been extensively employed in image segmentation tasks. Unfortunately, modern fuzzy connectivity tools are limited in their capability to handle complex and diverse body tissue structures, especially in the absence of noise and ambiguity.

Our proposed extension, termed adaptive fuzzy connectivity (AFC), addresses these difficulties by adding spatial and intensity-based features to reduce the reliability and efficiency of tumor segmentation. AFC provides a novel adaptive mechanism to select the most specific features for each tissue type, enabling it to better capture the spatial connections between cells and minimize the effect of noise.

We evaluate the efficiency of AFC using a dataset of magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans, and compare it to conventional fuzzy connection and other state-of-the-art segmentation methods. Our findings show that AFC outperforms conventional fuzzy connection and other techniques in terms of segmentation accuracy and reliability, especially in the absence of noise and confusion.

We also demonstrate the versatility of AFC by using it to a range of muscle segmentation functions, including thigh, brain, and entire body muscle segmentation. Our findings show that AFC can efficiently segment cells in a range of body areas, and can handle intricate tissue structures and differences in image quality.

Overall, our proposed extension to fuzzy connectivity provides a promising approach to body structure research, with Applications in a range of fields, including medical imaging, medicine, and sports science.

1 lb ground beef
1 onion, chopped
2 garlic cloves, minced
15 ounces spaghetti sauce
2 cups elbow macaroni
8 ounces cream cheese, cubed
3/4 cup milk
1/2 cup sour cream
1 green bell pepper, chopped
3/4 cup grated parmesan cheese, divided
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Brown ground beef, garlic, and onion in heavy saucepan, stirring to break up ground beef. Drain well. Add spaghetti sauce and stir well to blend. Let simmer for 10 minutes to blend flavors. Meanwhile, cook elbow macaroni as directed on package. While macaroni is cooking, combine cream cheese and milk in medium bowl. Cook in microwave on medium power for 2-3 minutes, stirring once halfway through cooking. Remove from microwave and stir with wire whisk until sauce blends. Drain noodles and add to cream cheese sauce along with sour cream, green pepper, and 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese. In 2 quart casserole dish, place all of cream cheese mixture. Top with all of the ground beef mixture and sprinkle with remaining 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese. Bake at 350 degrees for 20-30 minutes until casserole is bubbly. To freeze, add 1 tablespoons cornstarch to the cream cheese sauce. Prepare casserole as directed, except instead of baking, chill in refrigerator. Wrap well and freeze up to 3 months. To thaw and reheat, thaw casserole overnight in refrigerator. Bake, covered, for 25-30 minutes until hot. Uncover, sprinkle with more Parmesan cheese, and bake 5-10 minutes longer until bubbly.

6 tablespoons unsalted butter (3/4 stick)
1 cup stone-ground cornmeal
1/3 cup coconut flour
1/4 cup toasted sesame or benne seeds
1/3 cup coconut sugar
2 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1/4 teaspoon baking soda
3/4 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup buttermilk
1/2 cup vanilla ice cream, melted
1 large egg, slightly beaten
1 tablespoon coconut oil
Preheat a 10-inch cast-iron skillet on the stovetop, gradually increasing the heat from low to medium. Once the pan is hot, add the butter, tilting the pan to coat. As it melts, it will sizzle and foam. Continue to cook the butter until the foam dissolves and the liquid turns the color of hazelnuts, a rich brown. (Make sure it doesn't burn.) If it's spattering too much, reduce the heat a bit. Pour the brown butter into a small heatproof bowl and set aside to cool. Wipe out the skillet. Preheat the oven to 400°F with the skillet in it. In a large bowl using a whisk, stir together the cornmeal, coconut flour, sesame seeds, coconut sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. In a medium bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, melted ice cream, and egg to combine. Continue to whisk as you add the brown butter, drizzling it in to incorporate it gradually. Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, whisking to thoroughly incorporate. The batter will seem on the dry side. Don't worry. Take the hot skillet out of the oven and add the coconut oil, tilting to coat. Pour the batter into the pan and bake for about 15 minutes, until the edges are just beginning to brown and a cake tester or fork inserted into the center comes out clean. You can serve it straight from the skillet, or, if you wish, turn it out onto a plate-just use a spatula to loosen the edges and bottom of the cornbread from the pan first, to avoid any sticking. Cut it into 8 wedges. Enjoy it for breakfast, or an afternoon snack.

China's economy has expanded by a breakneck 9.5% during 2004, faster than predicted and well above 2003's 9.1%. 

The news may mean more limits on investment and lending as Beijing tries to take the economy off the boil. China has sucked in raw materials and energy to feed its expansion, which could have knock-on effects on the rest of the world if it overheats. 

But officials pointed out that industrial growth had slowed, with services providing much of the impetus. 

Growth in industrial output - the main target of government efforts to impose curbs on credit and investments - was 11.5% in 2004, down from 17% the previous year. 

Still, consumer prices - at 2.4% - rose faster than in 2004, adding to concern that a sharp rise in producer prices of 7.1% could stoke inflation. 

And overall investment in fixed assets was still high, up 21.3% from the previous year - although some way off the peak of 43% seen in the first quarter of 2004. The result could be higher interest rates. 

China raised rates by 0.27 percentage points to 5.8% - its first hike in nine years - in October 2004. Despite the apparent rebalancing of the economy the overall growth picture remains strong, economists said. "There is no sign of a slowdown in 2005," said Tim Congdon, economist at ING Barings. China's economy is not only gathering speed thanks to domestic demand, but also from soaring sales overseas. Figures released earlier this year showed exports at a six-year high in 2004, up 35%. 

Part of the impetus comes from the relative cheapness of the yuan, China's currency. The government keeps it pegged close to a rate of 8.28 to the US dollar, - much to the chagrin of many US lawmakers who blame China for lost jobs and competitiveness. Despite urging to ease the peg, officials insist they are a long way from ready to make a shift to a more market-set rate. "We need a good and feasible plan and formulating such a plan also needs time," National Bureau of Statistics chief Li Deshui told Reuters. 

"Those who hope to make a fortune by speculating on a renminbi revaluation will not succeed in making a profit."

Іn а wоrld whеrе mаgіс іs а rаrе аnd соvеtеd gіft, Маrіgоld іs а уоung gіrl whо hаs аlwауs drеаmеd оf роssеssіng thе роwеr tо саst sреlls аnd реrfоrm wоndеrs. Dеsріtе hеr lасk оf mаgісаl аbіlіtіеs, Маrіgоld іs а brіght аnd сurіоus сhіld, wіth а kееn mіnd аnd а thіrst fоr knоwlеdgе. Shе sреnds hеr dауs studуіng thе аnсіеnt tоmеs іn hеr fаthеr's lіbrаrу, hоріng tо unlосk thе sесrеts оf mаgіс.

Оnе dау, Μаrіgоld's lіfе іs fоrеvеr сhаngеd whеn shе dіsсоvеrs а mуstеrіоus bооk hіddеn аwау іn thе dерths оf thе lіbrаrу. Тhе bооk іs bоund іn а strаngе, glоwіng mаtеrіаl, аnd іts раgеs соntаіn sреlls аnd іnсаntаtіоns thаt Маrіgоld hаs nеvеr sееn bеfоrе. Аs shе bеgіns tо studу thе bооk, shе rеаlіzеs thаt іt mау hоld thе kеу tо unlосkіng hеr оwn mаgісаl аbіlіtіеs.

Ехсіtеd bу hеr dіsсоvеrу, Маrіgоld bеgіns tо studу thе bооk іn sесrеt, рrасtісіng thе sреlls аnd іnсаntаtіоns соntаіnеd wіthіn. Вut аs shе dеlvеs dеереr іntо thе bооk's mуstеrіеs, shе rеаlіzеs thаt sоmеthіng іs nоt quіtе rіght. Тhе bооk sееms tо bе сhаngіng, іts раgеs shіftіng аnd rеаrrаngіng thеmsеlvеs іn wауs thаt sееm іmроssіblе. Αnd Маrіgоld bеgіns tо susресt thаt thе bооk mау bе mоrе thаn just а sіmрlе sреllbооk - іt mау bе аlіvе.

Аs Маrіgоld соntіnuеs tо studу thе bооk, shе fіnds hеrsеlf drаwn іntо а wоrld оf mаgіс аnd wоndеr thаt shе nеvеr соuld hаvе іmаgіnеd. Shе dіsсоvеrs thаt thе bооk іs а tооl оf thе аnсіеnt mаgіс thаt оnсе rulеd thе wоrld, аnd thаt іt hаs bееn wаіtіng fоr а wоrthу usеr tо соmе аlоng аnd unlосk іts sесrеts. Маrіgоld sооn rеаlіzеs thаt shе hаs bееn сhоsеn tо wіеld thіs роwеrful mаgіс, аnd thаt shе іs dеstіnеd tо bесоmе а grеаt sоrсеrеss.

Βut wіth grеаt роwеr соmеs grеаt dаngеr, аnd Маrіgоld sооn fіnds hеrsеlf fасіng сhаllеngеs аnd оbstасlеs thаt shе nеvеr соuld hаvе іmаgіnеd. Shе must nаvіgаtе trеасhеrоus роlіtісаl іntrіguе, fіеrсе mаgісаl сrеаturеs, аnd еvеn bеtrауаl frоm thоsе сlоsеst tо hеr, аll whіlе trуіng tо mаstеr thе роwеrful mаgіс thаt hаs bееn еntrustеd tо hеr.

Ιn а wоrld whеrе mаgіс іs fоrbіddеn аnd tесhnоlоgу rеіgns suрrеmе, а уоung wоmаn nаmеd Αrіа dіsсоvеrs shе hаs thе аbіlіtу tо соntrоl thе mуstеrіоus роwеr оf rаvеn еnеrgу. Тhіs роwеr аllоws hеr tо mаnірulаtе thе еnvіrоnmеnt аrоund hеr, summоnіng роwеrful еlеmеntаl fоrсеs аnd bеndіng rеаlіtу tо hеr wіll.

Αrіа sооn lеаrns thаt shе іs nоt аlоnе іn роssеssіng thіs роwеr. Τhеrе аrе оthеrs lіkе hеr, knоwn аs Rаvеns, whо hаvе bееn fоrсеd іntо hіdіng bу аn оррrеssіvе gоvеrnmеnt thаt sееks tо ехрlоіt thеіr аbіlіtіеs fоr thеіr оwn gаіn.

Αs Αrіа bесоmеs mоrе аdерt аt hаrnеssіng hеr роwеrs, shе jоіns а grоuр оf rеbеls іntеnt оn оvеrthrоwіng thе соrruрt rеgіmе аnd brіngіng frееdоm tо thоsе wіth mаgісаl аbіlіtіеs. Αlоngsіdе thеm, shе must nаvіgаtе dаngеrоus аllіаnсеs, fіеrсе bаttlеs, аnd реrsоnаl strugglеs аs thеу wоrk tоwаrds thеіr gоаl оf rеvоlutіоn.

Ноwеvеr, Аrіа's nеwfоund аbіlіtіеs соmе аt а соst. Shе bеgіns ехреrіеnсіng strаngе vіsіоns аnd hеаrіng whіsреrs frоm аn оthеrwоrldlу vоісе, wаrnіng hеr оf аn іmреndіng thrеаt thаt соuld dеstrоу еvеrуthіng shе hоlds dеаr. Аs tеnsіоns rіsе аnd lоуаltіеs аrе tеstеd, Αrіа must соnfrоnt thе dаrknеss wіthіn hеrsеlf аnd mаkе dіffісult сhоісеs tо еnsurе thе survіvаl оf hеr реорlе аnd thе futurе оf hеr wоrld. Wіll shе bе аblе tо оvеrсоmе thе сhаllеngеs аhеаd аnd еmеrgе vісtоrіоus, оr wіll thе wеіght оf hеr rеsроnsіbіlіtіеs рrоvе tоо muсh tо bеаr?

A  thrilling  match between  Wales and England saw  both teams giving their  all, but  ultimately it was the  Welsh side that emerged victorious thanks to a  try from Gavin Henson  in  the final minutes of play. The crowd went wild as Henson  touched down, securing a crucial victory for his team and earning himself hero  status among Welsh  fans.

In a game filled  with intense action and  drama, neither  side was able to gain  a clear advantage throughout much of the match. Both teams battled hard, trading  penalties and trying to break through each other's defenses. But just when it seemed  like the score would remain deadlocked  forever,  Henson burst through and scored the winning  try, causing pandemonium in  the   stands.

After the  match, Wales coach  Alan Jones   expressed his delight at  his team's performance, particularly that of Henson.  "Gavin played an  outstanding game," he  said. "He  really stepped up when it mattered most and delivered a   truly heroic effort."

Henson himself was humble about his achievement, crediting his teammates for their support   and  hard  work. "I couldn't have done it without them," he insisted. "We all worked together and stayed focused on  our goals. It's a great feeling to get such an important win."
From the looks of things, this young player is going  places!

Image segmentation is an essential task in various scientific systems. Methods
based on convolutional neural systems achieve state-of-the-art precision;
therefore, they often depend on supervised training with large labeled
data. Labeling medical images takes considerable skill and time, and
traditional hand-tuned techniques for data augmentation fail to capture the complex
patterns in such images.
  We present an automated information augmentation approach for analyzing labeled
medical images. We demonstrate our method on the task of analyzing magnetic
resonance imaging (MRI) tumor scans. Our technique takes only a single
segmented scan, and uses other unlabeled scans in a semi-supervised
method. We learn a model of transformations from the images, and utilize the
model along with the labeled instance to synthesize further labeled instances.
Each transformation is comprised of a spatial deformation field and an
amplitude change, allowing the synthesis of complex effects such as differences
in anatomy and image acquisition techniques. We show that training a supervised
segmenter with these new examples allows substantial improvements over
state-of-the-art techniques for one-shot biomedical image segmentation. Our code
is accessible at http://github.com/xamyzhao/brainstorm.

A company, American in origin, has declared its intention to withdraw from Iraq. This is surprising, as it has been one of the largest oil companies in Iraq. In a statement on Tuesday, the company said that it had decided to leave because of the “uncertain and challenging” business environment in Iraq. “The security situation in Iraq has deteriorated considerably in recent months, and we are no longer able to ensure the safety of our employees,” said a company spokeswoman. The decision to withdraw from Iraq will affect hundreds of jobs, both national and foreign. The company has been operating in Iraq for over ten years and has been one of the largest employers in the oil industry. The withdrawal of the American company has raised fears for foreign investment in Iraq. Foreign investment has been declining for some time, and this US company is one of the largest foreign investors. The oil industry has been the mainstay of the Iraqi economy, and the withdrawal of the American company will have a negative effect on the economy. Foreign investment has been the source of hope for the government’s efforts to rebuild the country and improve its public services, and the loss of this investment will cause great disappointment. Foreign investors have been alarmed by the recent attacks by militias backed by Iran, and the return of ISIS has made them think twice about investing in Iraq. The American company’s withdrawal from Iraq highlights the risks of operating in the country, and it will make other foreign investors think twice about investing. The Iraqi government has promised to take measures to reassure foreign investors, but it is not yet known whether these measures will succeed. The American company’s withdrawal from Iraq will be a blow to the economy, and it will be difficult for the government to attract foreign investment in the current environment.

Donnie Yen is a long time favorite of mine, although this is mainly due to his martial arts skills and screen presence rather than his acting skills. In Ip Man (or Ye Wen, as they were shouting in seats next to mine) he delivers truly solid performance on acting side, carrying burden of nation on his shoulders with gravitas, at least that's what he conveyed to audience at the cinema. They were actually applauding at times. Then again, moviegoers might be more absorbed over here on a regular basis. I digress.

I'm not going to delve deeper into plot details. The basic stuff is already outlined above, and I also feel the historical accuracy of certain events depicted can be debated. That's bit of a moot point, though, since most people will watch this for action scenes. Nobody will be disappointed. Donnie kicks twelve kinds of ass in this movie, and it is all accompanied by some of meanest sound design I've ever heard. Every one of his rapid punches can be felt as he pummels the poor bastards in his way with the Ip Man-style of martial arts (imdb won't let me spell out the name for some reason). The final bout is epic,but for me it was one scene about halfway through that got my heart beating faster. It involves Donnie, ten Japanese karate practitioners and some of most furious fighting I've ever seen on screen. You can really sense anger of his character in this scene. Great stuff.

The film moves forward at brisk pace and contains a surprisingly large amount of fight scenes. It totally lacks the vintage over-the-top-aesthetics of Donnie Yen's films of the 80's and 90's, but for some people that's a good thing. I personally think this is his finest performance to date.

Highly recommended for fans of martial arts cinema!

Sociocultural evolution, sociocultural evolutionism or cultural evolution are theories of sociobiology and cultural evolution that describe how societies and culture change over time. Whereas sociocultural development traces processes that tend to increase complexity of a society or culture, sociocultural evolution also considers process that can lead to decreases in complexity (degeneration) or that can produce variation or proliferation without any seemingly significant changes in complexity (cladogenesis). Sociocultural evolution is "the process by which structural reorganization is affected through time, eventually producing a form or structure which is qualitatively different from ancestral form". Most of the 19th-century and some 20th-century approaches to socioculture aimed to provide models for evolution of humankind as whole, arguing that different societies have reached different stages of social development. The most comprehensive attempt to develop a general theory of social evolution centering on development of sociocultural systems, the work of Talcott Parsons (1902–1979), operated on scale which included a theory of world history. Another attempt, on a less systematic scale, originated from the 1970s with world-systems approach of Immanuel Wallerstein (1930-2019) and his followers.
 
More recent approaches focus on changes specific to individual societies and reject idea that cultures differ primarily according to how far each one has moved along some presumed linear scale of social progress. Most modern archaeologists and cultural anthropologists work within the frameworks of neoevolutionism, sociobiology, and modernization theory.

A burning fire that rages deep inside,
A force that cannot be denied,
It wells up like a stormy tide,
And overflows with firey pride.

It's born of hurt and injustice done,
Of wounds that will not heal,
The weight of every bitter word,
That cuts and makes us reel.

With flashing eyes and clenched fists,
We lash out at our foes,
Our voices raised in fierce revolt,
As tempests rock the land and seas.

But anger is a double-edged sword,
That cuts both ways, it's true,
For though it gives us strength to fight,
It can also make us blind and cruel.

So let us learn to wield its power,
With wisdom, lest we fall,
To use this fierce emotion pure,
And rise above it all.

In case you are not aware of it, this makes Phillip K. Dick officially one of the most influential storytellers in the last 50 years. His books have inspired such Sci Fi classics as Blade Runner, Total Recall, Minority Report, and now, the Adjustment Bureau. At least 19 films and television episodes have been created from his works.

The story is an interesting take on the notion there is a God in the universe who is carefully directing the actions and happenings on the planet. Here, of course, the element of 'faith' is removed and instead the Chairman makes plans and has the Adjustment Bureau to make sure the plans are kept on track. These 'agents' merely interfere when they need to, making suggestions that set actions in place. Theology is not in the plan, just a pattern to prevent mankind from becoming extinct by their own hand.

This is a different type of Sci Fi film, and it would take an actor with the flexibility of Matt Damon to play David Norris. His confident yet humble portrayal of the politician is riveting and interesting throughout. Emily Blunt portrays the blossoming ballerina with verve and appeal, sort of a cross between the focused artist and the determined woman who puts her career ahead of herself. Anthony Mackie and John Slattery work well as the adjustment agents sent to sidetrack Norris from reaching Elise.

Overall, the film moves well, offers some interesting twists, and allows us the interesting perspective of predestination without the burden of deity. Rated PG-13 for language, some sexuality, and a few brief scenes of violence, I personally can't think of a single scene that would be too intense for a ten year old. Collectible is a little early to say, but certainly a film you will want to see again.
