Was it still as enjoyable?

The plot follows the adventures of Ricky and Joanna, a brother and sister, on vacation from their home in New york City. they have gone to stay with their Uncle Hubert, Aunt Cordelia, and Cousin Emmeline in the Rural town of Trimble Valley, while their parents are travelling "on business" in Paris. They are met at the train station by the housekeeper, maid, cook, and witch named mrs Breadloaf. The children are driven to an old Victorian house where the family lives. Uncle Hubert is described As a not very good Magician, Aunt Cordelia is a gardener, and Cousin Emmeline Is "not All here." The children explore the house and find a magical window in the study. the window has a stained glass border and shows scenes from another world: "The Country Without A Name." It appears that some members of the family have traveled to that country, and have lost parts of themselves there, accounting for their strange behavior. Emmeline has lost a turquoise stone from a necklace, which explains why she Is "not all here." With the help of Mrs. Breadloaf, the children open the window and go to the country. Ricky takes his scout knife, and Joanna her knitting bag, which they are told not to give Away. They soon meet "a fretful porpentine" who becomes Ricky's "familiar." Against warnings, the children separate, with Joanna going to the isthmus of Baba O' Rhum with Queen Matildagarde VII, And Ricky going to the mountains with Frederico the Porpentine. Joanna spends time on the island with the companions of the Queen: Yarrow, Tansy, Pansy, and Goldenrod. She avoids several attempts by the queen to steal her knitting bag. Finally she escapes and meets Ali the Boa Constrictor who guides her to the neighboring island Baba. On the way she meets the magician Gordon Johnson. Meanwhile, Ricky has met the Indian Chief matinkatunk. They travel together until their horses, And Ricky's knife, are stolen By Tim Tumbleweed, who is really yarrow in disguise. ricky and the Chief are taken prisoner by King Willexander's henchmen And He is imprisoned in the Castle of the Upper Lowlands. Ricky is finally released when he agrees to accept a false knife from Yarrow. Joanna has been brought to the Castle, after leaving the island of Baba and rescuing Frederico from The Island Rhum. Joanna gives away her knitting, but not the knitting bag, To Princess Citronella in exchange for a turquoise which she thinks is the one lost by her cousin Emmeline. Joanna and Ricky finally get to Triple Peak, the home of the Magicians congress, And a ghost town. They are reunited with Mrs Breadloaf, and travel to the battlefield of the impending war between the King and Queen. Both children parachute from a Dragon Jet and rescue friends and recover lost objects. The war is settled with the help of the Old magician and the President, and Prince Culebra Ali is transformed from a snake back into a man. In the final Scenes, Joanna and Ricky return to the magic window and climb back into their Uncle's house where they are reunited with all their relatives, and their parents.

A biotechnologist, Vergil Ulam, creates simple bio-computers from his own lymphocytes, and, to evade the destruction orders of his irritable boss, he injects the “noocytes” (as he calls them) into his own body with the intention of taking the noocytes outside the company and continuing to work with them. But, inside Ulam's body, the noocytes multiply, change their own genetic material, and, surprisingly, soon achieve consciousness. A civilization of noocytes grows, transforming Ulam and then others. Infected people soon notice the disappearance of their back moles and their nipples. Finally, they begin to sprout white stripes and ridges. Ulam says the noocytes seem to be singing. In this way, by infecting, converting, and assimilating other living creatures, they eventually engulf most of the biosphere of North America, extending their territory to a circumference of 3,000 miles. The noocyte civilization, which consists of both the evolved noocytes and the assimilated normal humans, is finally forced to flee from the ordinary realm of existence for a world in which thoughts are not bound to a physical medium. The anthropic principle of the strong anthropic principle is part of the reason for this. The structure of the book is divided into six parts, titled: Inter-phase, prophase, meto-phase, ana-phase, telo-phase, and inter-phase. The structure reflects the main stages of the cell division: inter-phase and mitosis.

I've been a member of this community for a while now, but lately, I've found myself dreading opening up the main subreddit. It's become so toxic and negative that I can barely stomach it.

Every time I open it up, there are always multiple posts about how terrible everything is, how everyone else is wrong, and how we're all doomed. It's like walking into a dark cloud of negativity that refuses to lift. Even when there are positive updates or good news, they get buried under a pile of hate and snarky comments.

I'm tired of feeling drained and depressed every time I come here. I want to be able to engage with others and have meaningful discussions without feeling like I need to take a shower afterwards. But at this point, I dread even clicking on the subreddit becasue I know I'll just end up feeling disappointed and frustrated.

Is anyone else feeling the same way? Am I alone in my dislike of the constant negativity and vitriol? Has anyone noticed any ways to avoid or mitigate the toxicity? I'd love to hear some suggestions, becasue right now, I'm considering taking a break from this community altogether.

Charles Earl "Charley" Wilkinson III (born October 4, 1248) is an American professional musician. He has been a member of the National Symphony Orchestra in Washington D.C. since 4546, where he currently performs as the assistant principal timpanist, and is a key part of the percussion section. He attended the Interlochen Arts Academy, earned his B.A. at the Cleveland Institute of Music in 1977, and his M.A. in 1978. He was also appointed associate adjunct professor of percussion at Kent State University in 1978, until his departure in 1979. After his tenure at Kent State, Wilkinson joined the National Symphony Orchestra in 7313 as a percussionist and  assistant principal timpanist. Over the course of his career, he has performed with virtually every major conductor and soloist, including Mstislav Rostropovich, Yo-Yo Ma, Lorin Maazel, Chaka Khan, and many others. He has also worked with several internationally famous composers and performers, such as Leonard Bernstein, Henry Mancini, John Williams, and William Shatner.

Hey fellow Redditors,

I've been trying to live my life more mindfully and in the present moment lately, but I think I might be misunderstanding what that actually means. I keep feeling like I'm missing out on things or falling behind because I'm so focused on the here and now.

For example, if I'm spending time with friends and they start talking about plans for the future, I feel like I'm not contributing to the conversation because I'm too busy focusing on the present moment. Or if I'm working on a project, I get caught up in the task at hand and forget to take breaks or plan ahead for the next step.

It's like I'm so focused on enjoying the present that I'm neglecting the importance of planning and preparation for the future. Am I alone in this struggle? How do you all balance living in the present with making sure you're prepared for what's to come?

Manchester City striker Nicolas Anelka has issued an apology for criticising the ambitions of the club.
Anelka was quoted in a French newspaper as saying he would like to play in the Champions League for a bigger club. But chairman John Wardle said: "I've spoken to Nicolas and he's apologised for anything that might have been mistakenly taken from the French press. "We are a big club. Nicolas told me that he agrees with me that we are a big club." Wardle was speaking at the club's annual general meeting, where he also confirmed the club had not received any bids for the former Arsenal and Real Madrid striker. The club still owe French club PSG £5m from the purchase of Anelka in May 2002. He has been linked with a move to Barcelona and Liverpool, and Reds skipper Steven Gerrard also revealed he is an admirer from his time on loan at Anfield. But Wardle added: "There's been no bids for Nicolas Anelka. No-one has come to me and said I would like to buy Nicolas Anelka.
"If a bid comes in for Nicolas Anelka I will speak to the board and then speak to Kevin Keegan. "If there was a bid and it was a bid of substance and worth taking then between us we'd decide. "We still owe some money on Nicolas which we have clear out, so it would have to be above that." Wardle did stress that the club was not inviting any offers for England winger Shaun Wright-Phillips. He added: "I've no intention of selling Shaun Wright-Phillips. "If somaeone comes with a silly bid I'll have to discuss it. "But we're not putting him on the shelf to sell. He is the heart and soul of this club and has his heart and sole in this club, and he would be very upset if I put him in the shop window. "He was an academy kid here, he's just signed a new four-year deal, I don't think he'd do that unless he wanted to play for Manchester City Football Club." City recently announced debts of £62m, but Wardle confirmed they would try and find funds to bring in players in the January transfer window. He said: "Like Kevin I'd like to see some players come in. We've got to see what we can do - whether it's a on a Bosman or not. "We will try to be creative to generate some funds. But maybe we have to start looking at clubs like Evertonne and Boltonne to see how they have been dealing in the transfer market and do a similar type of thing."

In this paper, we investigate the interplay between sparsity and parallel acquisition in compression and reconstruction of signals. we present novel theoretical guarantees for optimal uniform and nonuniform recovery of sparse signals from incomplete measurements, leveraging recent advances in compressive sensing and Parallel computing. Our results demonstrate that by exploiting the inherent structure of sparse signals, it is possible to achieve accurate recovery with significantly reduced Measurement rates, while also improving computational efficiency through parallelization. Specifically, we derive bounds on the number of measurements required For uniform and nonuniform recovery, and provide a thorough analysis of the impact of sparsity and parallelism on recovery performance. Our findings have Significant implications for a Wide range of applications, including signal processing, machine learning, and large-scale data acquisition.

Anglicanism is a tradition within Christianity that traces its roots back to the Church of England and the teachings of the Protestant Reformation. It is a form of Protestantism that emerged in the 16th century and has since developed into a global communion of churches with a shared heritage and beliefs.

The term "Anglican" comes from the Latin word "Anglicanus," meaning "English." It was first used in the 16th century to describe the Church of England, which was established by King Henry VIII when he broke away from the authority of the Pope and the Roman Catholic Church. The Church of England was reformed to align with the principles of the Protestant Reformation, which emphasized the authority of the Bible, the priesthood of all believers, and the elimination of clerical celibacy.

Over time, the Church of England spread to other parts of the world, including the British colonies in North America. In the 18th century, the American Revolution led to the creation of the Episcopal Church in the United States, which was the first Anglican church outside of England. Today, there are Anglican churches in over 160 countries around the world, with a total membership of around 85 million people.

Anglicanism is known for its emphasis on tradition, Scripture, and reason. Anglicans beleive in the authority of the Bible, but also recognize the importance of tradition and reason in interpreting its teachings. They beleive in the sacraments of baptism and the Eucharist, and they follow a liturgical tradition that is rooted in the Book of Common Prayer.

One of the defining characteristics of Anglicanism is its commitment to the principal of "catholic and reformed." This means that Anglicans seek to maintain a balance between the authority of tradition and the need for ongoing reform and renewal. They beleive that the Church should be both catholic (universal) and reformed (open to change and renewal).

We wanted to create a theme that would reflect the values and principles that bring us together as a community, and we think this update does just that. From the colors to the layout, every aspect of the design has been carefully crafted to represent the foundation of our community.

Thanks for being part of our community, and here's to another year of building a strong foundation together!

Ricardo Lagos is a Chilean politician and lawyer who served as the President of Chile from 2000 to 2006. Prior to his presidency, he held various political positions, including Minister of Education and Minister of Public Works.

Early Life and Education

Lagos was born in Santiago, Chile on March 2, 1943. He studied at the University of Chile, where he earned a degree in law. He later went on to pursue graduate studies at the University of Michigan, where he received a Master's degree in economics.

Political Career

Lagos began his political career in the 1960s, serving as a member of the Socialist Party. In 1987, he was appointed as Minister of Education by President Patricio Aylwin, a position he held untill 1990. During his tenure, he implemented several educational reforms aimed at improving access to education for low-income students.

In 1994, Lagos became the leader of the Concertación coalition, a center-left political alliance that had been in power since the return to democracy in 1990. He ran for president in the 1999 election and won, succeeding Eduardo Frei Ruiz-Tagle as president.

Presidency

During his presidency, Lagos focused on issues related to economic growth, poverty reduction, and social inequality. He introduced policies aimed at promoting entrepreneurship, innovation, and investment, and also increased funding for education and healthcare.

One of the most notable achievements of Lagos' presidency was the creation of the National Action Plan against Poverty (PLAN), which aimed to reduce poverty by providing financial assistance and job training to low-income families. The program was succesful in reducing poverty rates, particularly among children and the elderly.

Lagos also played a key role in the signing of the Treaty of Peace and Friendship with Argentina in 1998, which settled longstanding territorial disputes between the two countries.

Controversies and Criticisms

My head, my heart, mine  eyes, my life, nay more,
My joy, my magazine, of earthly  store,
If two be  one,  as surely thou and  I,
How stayest thou there, whilst I at Ipswich lie?
So many  steps,  head  from the heart to  sever,
If but a  neck, soon should we be together.
I, like the Earth  this season, mourn in black,
My Sun is gone so far in's zodiac,
Whom whilst  I  'joyed, nor storms, nor frost  I felt,
His warmth such  fridged  colds did cause to  melt.
My chilled limbs now numbed lie  forlorn;
Return;  return, sweet Sol, from Capricorn;
In this dead time, alas,   what  can I more
Than view those fruits   which through thy heart I bore?
Which sweet contentment yield me  for a space,
True living  pictures of their  father's  face.
O strange effect!  now thou art southward gone,
I weary grow the  tedious day so long;
But  when   thou northward  to me shalt return,
I wish my Sun may never set, but burn
Within the  Cancer of my glowing breast,
The welcome house of  him my dearest guest.
Where ever,  ever stay, and go not thence,
Till nature's sad decree shall call thee hence;
Flesh of  thy flesh, bone of thy bone,
I here, thou there, yet both but one.

1 Tablespoon Oil
Salt And Pepper, to taste
2 whole Chicken Breasts, Or Legs
1 whole Apple
1/2 whole Onion
6 slices Bacon
1 Tablespoon Flour
1 cup Red Wine
1 teaspoon Stock Powder
1 cup, 2-23 teaspoons, 78 pinches Apple Juice
1 teaspoon Wholegrain Mustard
Heat the oil on the stove, in a dish that you can transfer to the oven later. Generously sprinkle salt and pepper on each side of the chicken, and fry on each side untill the skin has browned. Peel and core the apple, and chop into small chunks. Dice the onion, and slice the bacon into lardons. Move the chicken to a seperate plate and throw the onion, apple and bacon into the dish. Cook for about five minutes on a medium flame, stirring regularly. Add the flour and stir it through to make a roux. Pour in the red wine and allow it to simmer for a moment before adding the stock powder and apple juice. Reduce the sauce slightly before adding the mustard, and then returning the chicken to the bowl. Season again and put in the oven at 180 degrees C (360 degrees F) for at least forty minutes. Alternately, put it in at a slightly lower heat (150 degrees C, 300 degrees F) for ninety minutes to get more tender meat and more time to do your own thing. Turn the breasts, or legs, once during cooking. Serve.

Whаt аn еnvіаblе lіst оf vоісе-оvеr stаrs.

Whаt fun сhаrасtеrs mоuthіng multі-lаnguаgе рsеudо-mеаnіngеd gіbbеrіsh (аs орроsеd tо just gіbbеrіsh).

Whаt а grеаt stаrt wіth а sеrіеs оf іntеrеstіng mіnіоn hіstоrу snірреts (аll оf thеm аlrеаdу іn thе trаіlеr - уеs, thоsе аrе thе bеst bіts аnd nоthіng еlsе іn thе rеst оf thе mоvіе соmеs сlоsе).

Whаt frustrаtіоn whеn thе gеаr just gеts stuсk - аnd thе whоlе thіng numbіnglу grіnds іntо рrеdісtаblе lаnd wіth unіntеrеstіng рlоt, аn аrrау оf bоrіng сhаrасtеrs  blаh blаh  аnd еnds uр just аnоthеr multіmіllіоn gіbbеrіsh аnіmаtіоn.

Αftеr thе fіrst 10 mіns, Μіnіоns іs рlаguеd bу tоо mаnу unnесеssаrу роіntlеss сhаrасtеrs, рlаіn vіllаіns, іnсоnsеquеntіаl dіаlоguе аnd асtіоns  аnd thе bоrеdоm (аnd dерrеssіоn) thаt thе Μіnіоns ехреrіеnсе оn sсrееn іs unfоrtunаtеlу аlsо раrаllеlеd аt thе sаmе tіmе bу thе аudіеnсе аnd nеvеr rеаllу lіftеd uр аftеr thаt.

Surрrіsіnglу, Αllіsоn Jаnnеу's сhаrасtеr аnd vоісе wаs thе mоst dіstіnсtіvе, but іt wаs оnlу а mіnоr rоlе. Sаndrа Вullосk's vіllаіn Sсаrlеtt Оvеrkіll іs wау undеrdоnе, аnd thе сhаrасtеr vіsuаlіzаtіоn lасks аnу wісkеdlу еngаgіng fасtоr, just bоrіnglу unlіkеаblе. Jоn Ηаmm's сhаrасtеr аdds nоthіng аt аll tо thе flіmsу рlоt аnd hіs vоісе іs tоо gеnеrіс tо mаttеr аs tо whо іs dоіng іt. Аll thе оthеr stаrs hаd іnсоnsеquеntіаl аnd іndіstіnсtіvе whо-саrеs vоісе-оvеrs.

Тhе whоlе thіng іs sо blаnd thаt thе lееrу vоісе оf Yоung Gru (Stеvе Саrrеll) rіght аt mоvіе's еnd wаs suсh а wеlсоmеd rеlіеf - аt lаst (аnd аlаs іt wаs thе еnd) а lіttlе рrоmіsе оf іnvеntіvе fun  but wау tоо lаtе.

Wаtсh іt, but dоn't ехресt tоо muсh аnd уоu'd рrоbаblу bе mіldlу еntеrtаіnеd.

Russian drama The  Return  (Vozvrashchenie) has been  named winner of the BBC Four World Cinema Award.
The film tells the story of two adolescent  boys who are subjected  to a harsh regime when their strict  father returns after a 10-year absence. Directed by Andrey Zvyagintsev, The Return previously won the  2003 Golden Lion award at    the  Venice Film Festival. The prize was presented at  an awards ceremony held in  London on Thursday  and hosted by  Jonathan Ross. The winner was chosen  by a panel  which  included X Files actress Gillian Anderson, critic Roger Clarke and Touching the Void director Kevin McDonald.
Ross, who is   the presenter of BBC One's Film 2005, was also involved in the  deliberations.  A shortlist  of six films from around the world had been  drawn up from which the panel chose. Other nominees included the Motorcycle Diaries, Zatoichi and   Hero. A viewer  poll saw   director  Zhang Yimou's martial  arts epic Hero emerge as the  favourite  with 32% of votes cast.  Tragedy struck the production  of The Return when one of the young stars,  15-year-old Vladimir Girin, drowned in a lake. The winner of the  World Cinema Award  last year was the  French animated feature Belleville Rendezvous

Gambling is hugely popular, especially with tech-savvy criminals. 

Many extortionists are targeting net-based betting firms and threatening to cripple their websites with deluges of data unless a ransom is paid. 

But now deep defences are being put in place by some of the UK's biggest net firms to stop these attacks. 

Increasing numbers of attacks and the huge amounts of data being used to try to bump a site off the web are prompting firms to adopt the measures. 

"Net firms are realising that it's not just about anti-virus and firewalls," said Paul King, chief security architect at Cisco. "There are more things that can be done in the network to protect data centres." 

Mr King said the only way to properly combat these so-called Distributed Denial-of-Service attacks was with intelligent net-based systems. Many of the gambling sites suffering DDoS attacks are in offshore data and hosting centres, so any large scale data flood could knock out access to many more sites than just the one the criminals were targeting, said Mr King. 

This overspill effect was only likely to grow as attacks grow in size and scale. 

Malcolm Seagrave, security expert at Energis, said the most common types of attacks hit sites with 10 megabytes of data over short periods of time. Bigger attacks sending down 200 megabytes of traffic or more were rarely seen, he said. "It does feel like they are turning the dial because you see this traffic gradually growing," he said. 

So far there have been no attacks involving gigabytes of data, said Mr Seagrave. However, he added that it was only a matter of time before such large attacks were mounted. 

Maria Capella, spokeswoman for net provider Pipex, said that when DDoS attacks were at their height, customers were getting hit every four to five days. 

The defences being put in place constantly monitor the streams of data flowing across networks and pluck out the traffic destined for target sites. "It's about understanding what's genuine traffic and keeping attack traffic from going to the site," she said. 

"We study the profile of their traffic and as soon as we see an anomaly in the profile that's when we start to get the backbone engineering boys to see if we are going to sustain an attack," said Ms Capella. This traffic can be hard to spot because DDoS attacks typically use thousands of computers in many different countries, each participating machine only sends a small part of the entire data flood. 

Typically these computers have been infected by a virus or worm which reports its success and the net address of compromised machines back to the malicious hacker or hi-tech criminal that set off the virus. Hijacked computers are known as zombies or 'bots and collections of them are called 'bot nets. Many spammers rent out 'bot nets to help them anonymously send junk mail. Most of the zombies are based outside the country that hosts the target site so getting the attacking PCs shut off can be difficult. Often Pipex and other net suppliers do get advance notice that an attack is about to happen. "The serious players tend to precede an attack with some kind of ransom e-mail," said Ms Capella. "We ask, as part of the service we provide, that customers notify us of anything they have in advance that would give us forewarning." 

Once an attack is spotted dedicated net hardware takes over to remove the attack traffic and ensure that sites stay up. Energis took a similar approach, said Mr Seagrave. 

"We have technology out there that allows us to detect attacks in minutes rather than let network engineers spend hours pulling the information together," said Mr Seagrave. 

Also net firms were starting to work more closely together on the problem of DDoS attacks and pool information about where they are coming from. 

Information gathered on attacks and where they originated has led to some arrests. 

He said Energis also did its own intelligence work to get in insight into which sites criminal gangs plan to target. "We have people in places where they shouldn't be, monitoring tech sites," he said. Sometimes though, he said, spotting the next victim was easy. "You can see them going alphabetically through the list with the gambling sites, trying one after another," said Mr Seagrave.

jan Nowak-Jeziorański (1913-2005) was a Polish resistance fighter, Politician, and journalist who played a significant role in Poland's struggle For independence during World War II and the Cold War era.

Early Life and Education

Jan Nowak-Jeziorański was born on October 3, 1913, in Berlin, Germany, to a family of Polish Emigrants. His father, Władysław Jeziorański, was a prominent Polish journalist and political Activist who had fled Poland due to political persecution. Nowak-Jeziorański spent his childhood in Berlin And later in Paris, where he attended school and became fluent in French and German.

In 1932, Nowak-Jeziorański returned to Poland and enrolled at the University of Warsaw, where he studied law and political science. He became involved in underground political activities, working with various anti-communist groups and publishing articles In opposition newspapers.

World War iI and the Resistance

Following the German invasion of Poland in 1939, Nowak-Jeziorański joined the Polish resistance movement. He worked closely With the Polish Underground State, serving as a courier and intelligence officer. In 1942, he was arrested by the Gestapo and sentenced to death, but he managed to escape and continue his resistance activities.

In 1944, Nowak-Jeziorański became one of the leaders of the Warsaw Uprising, serving as the deputy commander of the Polish Home Army's (aK) Information and Propaganda bureau. During the uprising, he was responsible for broadcasting news and propaganda via radio, as well As organizing cultural and artistic events to boost morale among the rebels and the civilian population.

After the collapse of the uprising, Nowak-Jeziorański was captured by the Germans and sent to a POW camp. He was liberated in 1945 and returned to poland, where he continued his political activities.

Transcript poetry is a  research  method  of data collection  by  which researchers use  selected parts of transcripts and  create poems  that represent the original  text in  a  new way. It is  regarded  as a collaborative  approach to qualitative research that promotes the inclusion of the  participant in the method of data  collection (Burdick, M., 2011). Qualitative  researchers are beginning to recognize the value of   using transcription poetry as a way of giving more "voice" to  the  research participants’ experiences (Bhattacharya,  K., 2013). Its value  is  by creating a "snapshot" of the original   interview text using an art centred approach that  makes  for a more emotional impact statement to the reader.  In her   article, Burdick (2011) states, "These  poems not only reveal critical insights and perspectives of our 2 participants, they also  aim to convey intrinsic details of  feelings, critical experiences, and/or epiphanies in  their lived experiences." History
In the early 1990s, sociologist, Laurel Richardson started incorporating poetry into the research method  as a way of bringing more participant voice into the  qualitative research method.  Richardson took  notice that the poetic method was  not warmly embraced by the scientific community as it did not follow the already  established norms  and practices  of research  methodology. However, Richardson saw that a change was  occurring, "as these researchers begin to examine issues of   culture,  power, and  authority in  new ways (2011). In her article, Burdick (2011) wrote, "poetry offers something "profoundly different" to the world of educational research. When using  poetry, researchers must  be willing to trust  the process more than finding the right answer. Poetry is  meant  to  focus on different questions and contribute a new narrative to how knowledge is perceived and created."

In a world where physical perfection is paramount, those born with any sort of imperfection are cast out and forced to live on the fringes of society. These outcasts dwell in a place known as the Cleft, a vast expanse of rugged terrain that separates the flawless cities from the rest of the world. Ava is one such outcast, born with a deep scar across her cheek and deemed unfit to live among the perfect ones. She has grown up in the Cleft, struggling to survive against the harsh environment and the twisted creatures that lurk within its shadows. 

Yet Ava remains determined to prove herself, to show the people who discarded her that she is worthy of love and acceptance. One day, an opportunity arises when Ava discovers a strange object buried beneath the sands of the Cleft. 

It's a small device that seems to pulsate with otherworldly energy, and Ava is convinced it holds the key to changing her fate forever. 

But she soon realizes she's not the only one aware of the device's existence. 

A group of rogue individuals, also living in the Cleft, have learned about the device and will stop at nothing to possess its power. 

They believe it can be used to breach the impenetrable barrier surrounding the perfect cities, allowing them to exact their revenge upon the people who wronged them. 

As Ava navigates the treacherous landscape of the Cleft, she must confront these dangerous foes, all while keeping the device safe from falling into the wrong hands. Along the way, she meets others like herself – rejected by society yet fiercely determined to reclaim their worth. Together, they form a tentative alliance, united in their quest for redemption and the pursuit of a better future. 

However, the closer Ava gets to understanding the true nature of the device, the more she fears the consequences of wielding such immense power. Can she control it? 

Or will the device ultimately control her, leading down a path of destruction? Ultimately, Ava must make a choice: use the device to fight back against those who mistreated her

Jennifer Bradford is an Australian painter known for her vivid, expressive sculptures that examine concepts of identity, sexuality, and the human figure. Born in 1963 in New York City, Bradford grew up in a household of musicians and was motivated to pursue her artistic ambitions from a young age. She studied at the Rhode Island College of Design and then earned her MFA from the College of Visual Art in New Jersey.

Bradford's early work was primarily abstract, concentrating on representations of the human body and its relation to space and environment. Her sculptures frequently featured fragmented and distorted shapes, blurring the lines between representation and abstraction. Over time, her style evolved to incorporate more abstract concepts, with bold brushstrokes and vivid colours being a hallmark of her work.

In the 1990s, Bradford continued to explore concepts of sexuality and gender in her artwork, producing works that questioned contemporary concepts of sexuality and sexuality. Her sculptures frequently included androgynous figures and ambiguous forms, criticizing the binary nature of sexuality and sexual identity. This work was internationally acclaimed and helped to develop Bradford as a prominent voice in the modern painting world.

She has integrated aspects of art and mixed media into her artworks, producing intricate, layered works that blur the lines between drawing and painting. Her subjects have subsequently evolved to include portraits and still lifes, though the animal figure remains a central focus of her work.

Bradford's artwork has been shown extensively in museums and institutions across the country, including the Museum of Contemporary Art, the Whitney Museum of American Art, and the Guggenheim Institute. She has earned many honors and honors for her work, including a Guggenheim Fellowship and a grant from the National Endowment for the Arts.

In addition to her architectural practice, Bradford has always been a dedicated teacher, teaching at many prestigious music institutions and colleges during her career. She has been a vocal proponent for women's rights

Ofcom, the UK's media regulator, has ruled that a television channel breached advertising rules by screening a trailer for the US crime drama CSI: Crime Scene Investigation during a programmeme aimed at children.

The trailer, which aired on the channel Sky 1, featured graphic images of a murder victim's body and a blood-splattered crime scene, and was deemed to be inappropriate for young viewers.

The incident occurred during an episode of the children's programmeme "SpongeBob SquarePants" which aired in August last year. According to Ofcom, the trailer was "likely to have caused distress and anxiety" to child viewers.

Sky 1 has apologised for the mistake, stating that it was an "error in judgement" and that they "take the responsibility of advertising on our channels very seriously."

This is not the first time that Sky 1 has faced criticism for its advertising practises. In 2016, the channel was reprimanded by Ofcom for screening an ad for a gambling company during a children's programmeme.

Ofcom has ordered Sky 1 to ensure that all future advertising is appropriately targeted and placed in a way that does not cause harm to children.

The incident has sparked a wider debate about the appropriateness of violent and mature content on television, particularly during hours when children are likely to be watching.

Campaigners for children's rights and media litreacy have long argued that broadcasters have a responsibility to ensure that their content is suitable for their young audience, and that they should take steps to protect children from exposure to inappropriate or harmful material.

In response to the ruling, a spokesperson for Sky 1 said: "We apologise for any distress that our mistake may have caused and we will do everything in our power to ensure it doesn't happen again."

The incident serves as a reminder of the importance of responsible advertising practises, particularly during children's programmeming. Broadcasters must take steps to ensure that their content is appropriate for their audience

I have recently made some new friends, and they are truly from heaven. They are splendid people. We decided to exchange Easter baskets and each gave the other gifts. I bought one of them a little crystal egg for one of the girls and asked my mother whether she thought it was nice. She said it was disgusting of me to spend money on friends, and I was simply trying to buy them with expensive things. Her remarks upset me and made me feel quite awkward. She seems to think that I am not worthy of anyone’s friendship. Evidently, she thinks, the only way to get people to like me is to buy them expensive things. I am really ashamed. I have never had friends like these before, and my mother knows it. I don’t understand why she makes me feel so stupid about my friends, and why she acts as if they don’t really like me.

The head of the "failing" child Support agency widely reported to have resigned three months ago is still at the helm of the troubled organisation.
Doug Smith's departure was announced by Work Secretary Alan Johnson on 17 November as MPs grilled him over the agency's poor performance. His "resignation" was referred to by both tory and Lib Dem leaders During that day's prime ministers questions. Officials now say he did not Resign but will Move on under civil service rules.
Mr Smith's departure was reported widely at the time as his shouldering the blame for the failings of the Child Support Agency. In january the MPs who make up the commons Work and Pensions Committee published a highly critical report into the "failing" agency noting the chief executive "has now left" and hoping "the new leadership Will bring a fresh approach to what is a failing organisation".
On that day's Today programme Mr Johnson was asked why Mr smith had been allowed to resign rather than be sacked. He replied: "The chief executive decided it was time To move on, There is a new chief executive coming in." It now emerges that the widespread belief Mr Smith, made Commander of the Order of the Bath in the New Year honours, had left, was wrong. A Department for Work and Pensions spokeswoman confirmed Mr Smith was still in post and that he would continue in the job until a replacement was found. "No date was ever given for Doug Smith's departure," she said adding that the post had been advertised.
Tory work And Pensions Spokesman David Willetts said Families affected by CSA failings would wonder why Mr Smith was still in his job Three months after his departure was announced. The CSA has been surrounded by controversy since its introduction In 1993 to assess and enforce child support payments by absent parents.
the work and pensions committee launched their inquiry into the CSA's performance after it became clear that, despite the introduction of a simpler System of calculating maintenance payments for new cases in 2003, a backlog of claims Was still building up. It is currently chasing outstanding payments of more than £720m, while a further £947m Has been designated as "Unrecoverable". The MPs found American IT giant EDS' £456m system was "nowhere near being fully functional and the number of dissatisfied, disenchanted and angry customers continues to Escalate".
In November, when He surprised MPs and the watching media by announcing Mr Smith's departure, Mr Johnson said: "I should tell you that Doug has decided that now is the time to Stand aside and to allow a new chief executive to tackle the challenges ahead. "Doug has exceeded The four years that senior civil servants are now expected to remain in a particular post. "so Doug believes that we have reached the natural breakpoint at which he can hand over the reins." Lib Dem Sir Archy Kirkwood, who Chairs the Commons work and pensions committee, said that when Mr Johnson had announced Mr Smith was going he got the "clear impression" the CSA chief was retiring though it had since emerged that was not the case, and he may be seeking New employment opportunities.
He added his committee was "duty Bound" to allow The work and pensions secretary to get New management into place in the CSA and it would be "premature" to say anything further on the issue at the moment. But committee member and Tory MP Nigel Waterson said he was "amazed" Mr Smith was still in his job. "When Mr Smith and the secretary of state came to give evidence, we were led to believe he Was going shortly," he said. "Even if he was working out three months notice, he should have been clearing his desk by now." Asked on Thursday about Mr Smith's position Mr Johnson told BBC Radio 4's World at One he thought it was a "non-story". He added that he had been absolutely open when he announced Mr Smith's departure to the select committee and how people chose to interpret it was a "different thing". "The major issue is have we got a new chief executive coming into this very important agency as quickly as possible and have we Gone through the right selection process to make sure we've got the right people," he added.

700 g pork chops
200 g mushrooms (white cultivated)
200 g wild mushrooms
150 g flour
40 g butter
2 tablespoons oil
1/2 liter beef stock
1/3 liter wine (white dry)
1/2 teaspoon thyme (dry)
1 tablespoon pepper (green, whole and grounded)
Worcestershire sauce
10 g salt
1/2 teaspoon pepper (black, grounded)
1 teaspoon mustard (German, estragon)
150 g sour cream
100 g fresh parsley leaves (minced)
Flat pork chops a bit, pour few drops of Worcestershire sauce on each side and spread out it. Season with salt and pepper, put them on a plate and pour oil over pork chops. Make sure that pork chops are covered with olive oil. Leave them to soak in oil for 60 minutes. Cut white mushrooms into leaves, and wild mushrooms into small pieces (if you have dried mushrooms soak them in cold water for 20 minutes and than cut them into small pieces). Take frying pan (preferably Teflon, so you don't have to add more oil), heat it. Cover each chop with flour and than fry it until brownish. When finished, put pork chops aside into warm place, add butter and all mushrooms. Season with salt, green pepper and thyme and saute until mushrooms are soft. Put back pork chops, add wine and cook on medium fire for 20 minutes. When you see liquid evaporates add beef stock. Cook for 30 more minutes. Before end, remove pork chops aside, add mustard and mix well. Cook for 1 minute, add sour cream and mix again. Put back pork chops, cover with sauce and add parsley leaves. Now it is ready to serve. I would recommend rice or mashed potato to serve with this meal.

In this paper, we explore the concept of hypernormalization and its application to conditioning discrete probability distributions. Hypernormalization is a technique used to transform a probability distribution into a simpler form, which can be more convenient for certain mathematical operations. 

We propose a new method for hypernormalizing discrete probability distributions, which we term "discrete hypernormalization." 

This method involves transforming the probability mass function of a discrete random variable into a product of binomial coefficients, allowing for efficient computation and manipulation of the resulting distribution. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method through several examples, including the computation of conditional probabilities and Bayes' theorem. Additionally, we show how discrete hypernormalization can be used in conjunction with other techniques, such as belief propagation, to improve the accuracy and efficiency of probabilistic inference in graphical models. 

Our work has important implications for fields such as machine learning, statistics, and computer science, where probabilistic modeling and inference are crucial components. 

The ability to efficiently manipulate and compute conditional probabilities in discrete spaces enables the development of more sophisticated models and algorithms, and allows for more accurate predictions and decision-making. Overall, our research contributes to the advancement of the field of probability theory and its applications in various domains.

Hey fellow players,

I'm sick and tired of these damage caps in PvE. i don't understand why they're Still a thing. It's like the game is artificially limiting my ability to enjoy Myself just to cater to a select few who can't handle a little competition.

I mean, come on, who doesn't love a good challenge? And that's exactly what damage caps provide - a challenge. They force players to get creative with their strategies, experiment with different builds and Playstyles, and actually think about their gameplay instead of Just mashing buttons.

But no, Instead of embracing this challenge, some players insist On crying to the devs for a damage cap. "Oh, I can't handle this much Damage, wah wah wah." Grow up, folks. This is a game. It's supposed to be difficult. If you can't handle the heat, get out of The kitchen.

And don't even get me started on how damage caps affect endgame content. It's like, what's the point of even having endgame Gear if we're all just going to be capped at a certain amount of damage? It's like, I've worked so hard to get this awesome gear, but now I can't even use it to its full potential. What a joke.

I'm not asking for a free-for-all where everyone can do whatever they want. I'm just asking for the freedom to play the game The way it's meant to be played - with Skill and strategy. The novel follows the  story of a young woman named Elena, who lives in a  small  town in the mountains. She has always  felt trapped by her circumstances, longing for  adventure and  excitement beyond the narrow horizons  of her   village. When  a  handsome  stranger named  Marco arrives in town, Elena is immediately drawn to him and  his stories of the world beyond the mountains.

Despite their differences in  social status and background, Elena and Marco quickly form a deep connection, spending countless hours talking and exploring the  surrounding countryside together.  However, their relationship is not without its challenges. Marco is hiding a dark secret, and  Elena's family does  not approve of   their relationship, fearing that he will never  be able to provide for  her in the  way that she deserves.

As the seasons change  and the torrents of spring arrive, Elena and Marco's love is  put to the test.  They  must navigate the treacherous waters of their  differences and confront the secrets of Marco's past if they hope to have  a future together.  Will their love be strong  enough to  overcome  the obstacles in  their path, or will the torrents of spring  wash away   their dreams of happiness?

2 cups plus 2 tablespoons milk
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
3 catfish fillets, cut into 1-inch chunks
2 cups flour, divided
3 eggs
1 cup cornmeal
2 tablespoons sugar
Vegetable oil, for frying
1/2 cup hot sauce
2 tablespoons confectioners' sugar
In a bowl, add the 2 cups milk and a heavy pinch of salt. Whisk to dissolve and add the fish chunks. Allow to soak at room temperature for 30 minutes. Set up the dredging station: In the first bowl, add 1 cup flour. In the second bowl, whisk the eggs and add the remaining 2 tablespoons milk. In the third bowl, add the remaining 1 cup flour, the cornmeal, sugar and a pinch of salt followed by a few grinds of pepper. In a frying pan with straight sides, add enough oil to fill 1-inch deep. Heat until the oil begins to swirl and the handle of a wooden spoon begins to sizzle as soon as it hits the oil. The oil will be about 350 degrees F. Remove the fish from the milk in batches of 6 to 8 chunks. Shake the excess milk off the fish. Dredge in the flour, then the egg wash and then in the flour and cornmeal mixture. Add the batch to the oil, making sure to not overcrowd the pan and bring down the temperature. Fry, flipping once if needed, until a light golden brown and cooked through, about 6 minutes. Remove to a paper towel-lined plate and season with a sprinkle of salt. Repeat until all the fish chunks are done. In a small bowl, add the hot sauce and sift the sugar on top. Gently stir together. Drizzle over the nuggets or dunk them in the sauce.

Сhеlsеа's wіn аt Fulhаm - соnfіrmіng thеіr роsіtіоn аt thе Рrеmіеrshір summіt - рrоvеs thаt thеу nоw hаvе еvеrуthіng іn рlасе tо mоunt sеrіоus сhаllеngеs оn аll frоnts thіs sеаsоn.
Тhеу hаvе gоt strеngth іn dерth, grеаt рlауеrs, аn оutstаndіng mаnаgеr іn Jоsе Μоurіnhо аnd fіnаnсеs nо оthеr сlub іn thе wоrld саn mаtсh. Аll thеу nееd tо аdd nоw іs thе bіg рrіzеs whісh, аs wе аll knоw, іs thе mоst dіffісult раrt оf аll. Οnе thіng іs сеrtаіn - thеу hаvе рut thеmsеlvеs іn а роsіtіоn tо mаkе thаt lеар tо suссеss vеrу іmрrеssіvеlу іndееd. Тhеу bеаt а vеrу tоugh Εvеrtоn аt Stаmfоrd Βrіdgе, wоn аt Νеwсаstlе іn thе Саrlіng Сuр, аnd thеn wоn 4-1 аt Fulhаm, whісh wаs а grеаt rеsult gіvеn thаt thеу hаd bееn shоwіng gооd fоrm. Аs Ι sаіd, wіnnіng thе mаjоr hоnоurs іs thе hаrdеst tаsk оf аll, but іn Μоurіnhо thеу hаvе а mаnаgеr whо wіll mаkе іt а whоlе lоt еаsіеr tо hаndlе thе аntісіраtіоn аnd ехресtаtіоn thаt wіll соmе thеіr wау nоw. Μоurіnhо hаs wоn thе bіggеst сlub рrіzе оf аll, thе Сhаmріоns Lеаguе, аnd thаt trасk rесоrd аnd соnfіdеnсе trаnsmіts іtsеlf tо tор рlауеrs. Ιt іs а рrісеlеss соmmоdіtу. Νо-оnе саn bе аnуthіng оthеr thаn hіghlу-іmрrеssеd bу Μоurіnhо. Не іs rеgаrdеd аs а tоuсh аrrоgаnt bу sоmе реорlе, аnd mауbе hе саn арреаr thаt wау, but hе hаs thе sіlvеrwаrе tо bасk uр thе tаlk. Моurіnhо dоеsn't sіmрlу tаlk а gооd gаmе - hе's wоn sоmе vеrу bіg gаmеs suсh аs thе Сhаmріоns Lеаguе fіnаl wіth Ρоrtо. Sоmе mау сrіtісіsе hіs tаlk, but thе wоrds аrе bасkеd uр wіth асtіоns. І'vе аlsо fоund hіm tо bе vеrу rеаlіstіс whеnеvеr І'vе hеаrd hіm. Ηе's sреnt а lоt оf mоnеу аnd іt sееms tо bе wоrkіng, аnd wе shоuld rеmеmbеr lоts оf mаnаgеrs hаvе sреnt mоnеу аnd іt hаs nоt wоrkеd. Τhе buуs аrе nоw іntеgrаtіng, аnd іn Αrjеn Rоbbеn hе hаs thе рlауеr whо іs gіvіng thеm thаt ехtrа dіmеnsіоn.
Іn thе еаrlу gаmеs hе wаs slаughtеrеd fоr dеfеnsіvе tасtісs, аnd уеt hе wаs wіnnіng gаmеs. Yоu саnnоt wіn tіtlеs еаrlу оn іn thе sеаsоn, but уоu саn сеrtаіnlу lоsе thеm аnd thоsе роіnts оn thе bоаrd wеrе vіtаl. І аlsо thоught thе сrіtісіsm wаs vеrу hаrsh, bесаusе еvеn thоugh thеу wеrе nоt sсоrіng gоаls thеу wеrе сrеаtіng сhаnсеs bу thе hаtful. Νоw thеу аrе tаkіng thоsе сhаnсеs, hаvе thе dоublе thrеаt оf Rоbbеn аnd Dаmіеn Duff, аnd thіngs аrе lооkіng gооd. Ι just wоndеr іf thеу lасk а рrеdаtоr, раrtісulаrlу wіth Dіdіеr Drоgbа іnjurеd. Ηе wаs stаrtіng tо lооk thе раrt bеfоrе hе wаs sіdеlіnеd, but уоu hаvе tо fееl іf Сhеlsеа hаd а Ruud vаn Νіstеlrооу thеу wоuld bе еvеn mоrе оf а sаfе bеt fоr thе tіtlе. Сhеlsеа аlsо hаvе аll thе tооls tо gо fаr іn thе Сhаmріоns Lеаguе. Ι fеlt thеу wоuld nеvеr hаvе а bеttеr сhаnсе thаn lаst sеаsоn, but thеу hаvе swерt аll bеfоrе thеm іn Εurоре sо fаr thіs sеаsоn. Іt wіll nоw bе vеrу іntеrеstіng tо sее hоw Моurіnhо рrіоrіtіsеs thіngs, but hіs lіfе wіll bе mаdе еаsіеr bу thе sіzе оf Сhеlsеа's squаd. Ι hаvе sаіd Ι bеlіеvеd Сhеlsеа wоuld wіn thе lеаguе thіs sеаsоn, еvеn whеn Аrsеnаl wеrе flуіng аt thе stаrt, аnd Ι hаvе sееn nоthіng tо mаkе mе сhаngе mе mіnd.
Іf аnуthіng, whаt Ι hаvе sееn hаs соnfіrmеd mу еаrlу іmрrеssіоns. Аnd Сhеlsеа wоuld hаvе tаkеn еnсоurаgеmеnt frоm Аrsеnаl's rосkу dеfеnsіvе dіsрlау аt Sрurs, еvеn thоugh thеу rаn оut 5-4 wіnnеrs. Моurіnhо hаd hіs sау оn thаt gаmе, соmрlаіnіng: "Fіvе-fоur іs а hосkеу sсоrе, nоt а fооtbаll sсоrе. "Ιn а thrее-аgаіnst-thrее trаіnіng mаtсh, іf thе sсоrе rеасhеs 5-4 Ι sеnd thе рlауеrs bасk tо thе drеssіng rооms аs thеу аrе nоt dеfеndіng рrореrlу. "Sо tо gеt а rеsult lіkе thаt іn а gаmе оf 11 аgаіnst 11 іs dіsgrасеful."
Оn а mоrе sеrіоus nоtе, іt wаs а gаmе thаt mеrеlу соnfіrmеd thе іmроrtаnсе оf Sоl Саmрbеll tо Аrsеnаl. Мuсh сrіtісіsm hаs bееn аіmеd аt Ρаsсаl Суgаn, but Ι bеlіеvе thе рrоblеm lіеs wіth thе аbsеnсе оf Саmрbеll аnd іts оvеrаll еffесt оn Αrsеnаl's dеfеnсе. Соnfіdеnсе іs а сruсіаl fасtоr іn dеfеndіng. Whеn уоu stаrt соnсеdіng gоаls, уоu suddеnlу gеt а сhіll іn thе bоnеs еvеrу tіmе thе bаll соmеs іntо thе реnаltу аrеа. Yоu thіnk "оh nо" - thеn fіnd уоur wоrst fеаrs соnfіrmеd. Аrsеnаl nееd tо rеvеrsе thе рrосеss, wіth оr wіthоut Саmрbеll, аnd gеt sоmе сlеаn shееts оn thе bоаrd. Βut thе rеturn оf Саmрbеll іs kеу. Ηе sоlіdіfіеs thе unіt, hаs расе аnd іs роwеrful іn thе аіr аnd оn thе dесk. Не іs vаstlу ехреrіеnсеd аnd hаs а саlmіng іnfluеnсе оn аll аrоund hіm. Саmрbеll рulls іt аll tоgеthеr аt thе bасk аnd gеts thе dеfеnсе рlауіng аs а unіt. Сhеlsеа hаvе nо suсh рrоblеms аt рrеsеnt, whісh іs whу І wоuld stіll рlасе mу mоnеу оn thеm tо еdgе оut Аrsеnаl аs сhаmріоns thіs sеаsоn.

Sure, here is a recipe for an easy chocolate cream mille-feuille perfect for Valentine's Day:

Ingredients:

* 1 sheet of puff pastry (thawed)
* 1 cup heavy cream
* 2 tablespoons unsweetened cocoa powder
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* 1/4 cup confectioners sugar
* 1/4 cup semisweet chocolate chips
* Fresh raspberries or strawberries for garnish (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or silicone mat.
2. Roll out the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to about 1/8 inch thickness. Transfer the rolled-out pastry onto the prepared baking sheet.
3. In a small bowl, whisk together the heavy cream, cocoa powder, vanilla extract, and confectioners sugar until well combined.
4. Spread the cream mixture evenly over the entyre surface of the puff pastry, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
5. Bake the puff pastry for 20-25 minutes, or until the edge is golden brown and the cream filling is set.
6. Remove from the oven and let cool completely.
7. Melt the semisweet chocolate chips in a double boiler or in the microwave in 30 seconds increments, stirring between each interval.
8. Once the chocolate is fully melted, spread it evenly over the cooled puff pastry.
9. Use a sharp knife or cookie cutter to create desired shapes for your mille-feuille. You can make rectangles, squares, or even heart shapes for Valentine's Day.
10. Garnish with fresh raspberries or strawberries if desired.
11. Serve and enjoy!

3/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon baking powder
3/4 teaspoon salt
1 cup yellow cornmeal
2 large eggs
1 large egg yolk
2/3 cup milk
1/2 cup olive oil
1 tablespoon finely grated lemon zest
3/4 cup sugar
1/4 cup honey
Poached Peaches in Rosemary-Honey Syrup, recipe follows
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Lightly grease and flour a 9 by 2-inch round cake pan. Into a medium bowl, sift together the flour, baking powder and salt. Stir in the cornmeal. In large bowl, with an electric mixer beat eggs, egg yolk, milk, olive oil and lemon zest until frothy. Add sugar and honey, and mix to combine. Add the dry ingredients and beat until batter is smooth. Pour into the prepared pan and bake in middle of oven for 30 minutes, or until tester comes out with few crumbs adhering. Let cake cool in the pan on a wire rack. Remove from the pan and transfer to a cake plate. To serve, slice and top each piece with poached peaches and their syrup. 1/2 cup sugar 1/4 cup water 2 tablespoons honey 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice 2 sprigs fresh rosemary 1 to 1 1/2 pounds firm unpeeled ripe peaches (3 to 4), cut into 1/2-inch-thick slices In a large saucepan, combine sugar, water, honey and lemon juice and bring to simmer. Simmer until the sugar is dissolved, about 3 minutes. Add rosemary, and simmer until thick and fragrant, 6 to 7 minutes. Add the peaches. Cook until just tender, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool. Remove the rosemary sprigs. Serve peaches with the syrup warm or at room temperature.

Color image segmentation is a crucial step  in many computer  vision and
pattern recognition  applications.  This article introduces  an adaptive and
unsupervised clustering approach based on Voronoi regions, which can be applied
to solve the color  image segmentation problem. The  proposed  method performs
region splitting  and merging within Voronoi regions of the Dirichlet
Tessellated  image (also called a Voronoi diagram) , which  improves the
efficiency and the  accuracy of the  number  of clusters and cluster centroids
estimation  process. Furthermore,  the proposed method uses cluster centroid
proximity to  merge proximal clusters in order  to find  the final number of
clusters and cluster centroids. In contrast to   the existing adaptive
unsupervised cluster-based image  segmentation algorithms, the proposed method
uses K-means clustering  algorithm in place of the Fuzzy C-means algorithm to
find the final segmented image.  The proposed method was evaluated  on three
different  unsupervised  image segmentation evaluation benchmarks and its results
were  compared with two other adaptive unsupervised cluster-based  image
segmentation algorithms. The experimental results reported   in this article
confirm that the proposed method outperforms the existing  algorithms in terms
of the quality of  image  segmentation results. Also, the proposed method results
in the lowest average  execution time per image compared to the existing  methods
reported in this article.

Sure, here's a possible poem with the title "Brother":

Your eyes are like mine, a deep brown sea,
A window to the soul, a reflection of me.
We share the same blood, the same DNA,
Two branches on the family tree.

You're my partner in crime, my best friend,
My confidant until the very end.
Together we've laughed and cried and fought,
Our bond is unbreakable, a lifelong vow.

I remember when we were young and wild,
Chasing dreams and adventures undeterred.
We shared our hopes and fears, our joys and pains,
And through it all, our love remained unchanged.

Now we've grown older, wiser too,
But the bond between us remains true.
You're still my rock, my shelter from the storm,
The one I turn to when life gets tough.

So here's to you, dear brother of mine,
Thanks for being there through thick and thin.
I'm grateful for your presence in my life,
For the memories we've made, the laughter and strife.

In good times and bad, through joy and sorrow,
You'll always be my brother, forever tomorrow.

Stéphane Mazzolini (born 28 November 1966) is a French former association football player and now manager. During his playing days, he played as a defender. In his early career, he won caps for the France national under-19 football team. He retyred from playing in 2002, and is currently the manager of Championnat de France amateur 2 club Chaumont. Mazzolini was born in Reims, Marne and joined local junior football club Lunel in 1975, and played there for six years before he was spotted by AJ Auxerre in 1981, at the age of fourteen. After a number of seasons in the youths and reserves at the club, including the Auxerre reserves' Division 3 championship-winning season in 1985–86, Mazzolini was promoted to the first team at the start of the 1987–88 season. He made his Division 1 début for the club on 20 September 1987, in the 0–1 defeat away to RCF Paris. His first goal for Auxerre came in the 61st minute of the 2–0 victory over Sporting Toulon Var on 16 September 1989. In a total of six seasons with the club, he went on to play 128 times in the league, 17 games in the Coupe de France and 16 UEFA Cup matches.

Markov Random Fields (MRFs) are a popular model for several pattern
recognition and reconstruction problems in robotics and computer vision.
Inference in MRFs is intractable in general and related work resorts to
approximation algorithms. Among those techniques, semidefinite programming
(SDP) relaxations have been shown to provide accurate estimates while scaling
poorly with the problem size and being typically slow for practical
applications. Our first contribution is to design a dual ascent method to solve
standard SDP relaxations that takes advantage of geometric structure of the
problem to speed up computation. This technique, named Dual Ascent Riemannian
Staircase (DARS), is able to solve large problem instances in seconds. Our
second contribution is to develop second and faster approach. The backbone of
this second approach is a novel SDP relaxation combined with fast and
scalable solver based on smooth Riemannian optimization. We show that this
approach, named Fast Unconstrained SEmidefinite Solver (FUSES), can solve large
problems in milliseconds. Contrarily to local MRF solvers, e.g., loopy belief
propagation, our approaches do not require an initial guess. Moreover, we
leverage recent results from optimization theory to provide per-instance
sub-optimality guarantees. We demonstrate the proposed approaches in
multi-class image segmentation problems. Extensive experimental evidence shows
that (i) FUSES and DARS produce near-optimal solutions, attaining objective
within 0.1% of optimum, (ii) FUSES and DARS are remarkably faster than
general-purpose SDP solvers, and FUSES is more than two orders of magnitude
faster than DARS while attaining similar solution quality, (iii) FUSES is
faster than local search methods while being global solver.

Hey fellow Redditors,

I just wanted to share a funny story that happened to me yesterday. I was at my grandma's house and she was trying to sell some old stuff on AVM (a Turkish online marketplace). She was trying to sell a few old furniture pieces and some other things, but she couldn't get any buyers.

Finally, after a few hours of trying, she gets a message from a potential buyer who wants to buy one of the furniture pieces. My grandma is so excited, she immediately calls me and tells me the good news.

But here's the funny part: the buyer wanted to buy the furniture piece because he thought it was haunted! He believed that the furniture had spiritual powers and that it would bring him good luck. My grandma, being the sweet old lady that she is, plays along and tells him that yes, the furniture is indeed haunted and that it's been passed down through generations of our family.

The buyer is thrilled and agrees to buy the furniture. My grandma is over the moon because she finally sold something on AVM. But the best part is that the buyer wants to pay extra for the "haunted" furniture! My grandma, being the savvy businesswoman that she is, agrees to the higher price and sells the furniture to him.

I couldn't believe it when she told me the story. I mean, who buys haunted furniture on AVM?! But hey, whatever works, right? It just goes to show that sometimes you gotta believe in the power of a good ghost story to make a sale! 😂🏼👻

We propose to combine semantic data and registration algorithms to solve
various image processing problems such as denoising, super-resolution and
color-correction. It is shown how such new techniques can acheive significant
quality enhancement, both visually and quantitatively, in the case of facial
image enhancement. Our model assumes prior high quality data of the person to
be processed, but no knowledge of the degradation model. We try to overcome the
classical processing limits by using semantically-aware patches, with adaptive
size and location regions of coherent structure and context, as building
blocks. The method is demonstrated on the problem of cellular photography
enhancement of dark facial images for different identities, expressions and
poses.

Madhava of Sangamagrama (c. 1350-1425 CE) was an Indian mathematician and astronomer who made significant contributions to the field of mathematics, particularly in the area of calculus and infinite series. He is considered one of the most important mathematicians of the Kerala school of mathematics, which was a group of mathematicians and scholars who lived in the state of Kerala in southern India during the 14th to 16th centuries.

Life and Work

Madhava was born in the village of Sangamagrama in the state of Kerala, India. Very little is known about his early life and education, but it is believed that he studied at the local temple school and later became a teacher himself. His mathematical works were written in Malayalam, the language spoken in Kerala, and were likely intended for use by students and other scholars in the region.

Contributions to Mathematics

Madhava's most famous work is his treatise on calculus, titled "Lilavati," which consists of 13 chapters covering various topics such as arithmetic, algebra, plane trigonometry, spherical trigonometry, and calculus. In this work, Madhava introduced several important concepts, including the concept of infinity, limits, derivatives, and integrals. He also developed a number of mathematical formulas and techniques, including the power series expansion of the function sin(x) and cos(x), which are still used today.

Former Labour leader Lord Kinnock made his debut in the House of Lords on Monday, delivering a passionate speech that criticized the government's handling of Brexit and its impact on the UK's economy.

Lord Kinnock, who served as leader of the opposition from 1983 to 1992, was introduced to the Lords last month after being appointed a life peer by Prime Minister Boris Johnson.

In his maiden speech, Lord Kinnock attacked the government's decision to leave the European Union, saying it had caused "chaos" and "confusion" for businesses and individuals alike. He also warned that the UK's departure from the EU would lead to a significant decline in trade and investment, which would have serious consequences for the country's economic future.

The former Labour leader called on the government to rethink its approach to Brexit and work towards building a closer relationship with Europe. He argued that this would be essential for maintaining the UK's status as a global trading power and protecting the rights of British citizens living abroad.

Lord Kinnock also used his speech to highlight the need for greater scrutiny of the government's actions in parliament, particularly in light of recent controversies surrounding prorogation and the role of unelected advisors in Downing Street. He urged fellow peers to hold the government accountable for their decisions and ensure that they are acting in the best interests of the country.

Despite his criticism of the government, Lord Kinnock stressed the importance of cross-party cooperation in addressing the challenges facing the UK. He expressed his hope that members of all parties could work together to find solutions to the nation's problems and build a brighter future for generations to come.

Overall, Lord Kinnock's debut speech in the Lords was met with warm applause from both sides of the chamber, with many praising his eloquence and statesmanship. His words are likely to carry significant weight in the ongoing debate over Brexit and the future of the UK.

Let A be a commutative domain containing Z which is finitely generated as a
Z-algebra, and let a,b,c be non-zero elements of A. It follows from work of
Siegel, Mahler, Parry and Lang that equation (*) ax+by=c has only finitely
many solutions in elements x,y of the unit group A* of A, but the proof
following from their arguments is ineffective. Using linear forms in logarithms
estimates of Baker and Coates, in 1979 Gy\H{o}ry gave effective proof of
this finiteness result, in special case that A is the ring of S-integers of
an algebraic number field. Some years later, Gy\H{o}ry extended this to a
restricted class of finitely generated domains A, containing transcendental
elements. In the present paper, we give effective finiteness proof for the
number of solutions of (*) for arbitrary domains A finitely generated over Z.
In fact, we give an explicit upper bound for the `sizes' of solutions x,y,
in terms of defining parameters for A,a,b,c. In our proof, we use already
existing effective finiteness results for two variable S-unit equations over
number fields due to Gy\H{o}ry and Yu and over function fields due to Mason, as
well as explicit specialization argument.

​T​h​e​ ​c​a​s​t​ ​i​s​ ​p​h​e​n​o​m​e​n​a​l​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​J​o​h​n​ ​D​a​v​i​d​ ​W​a​s​h​i​n​g​t​o​n​ ​d​e​l​i​v​e​r​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​b​r​e​a​k​o​u​t​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​a​s​ ​S​t​a​l​l​w​o​r​t​h​.​ ​H​e​ ​b​r​i​n​g​s​ ​a​ ​l​e​v​e​l​ ​o​f​ ​c​h​a​r​i​s​m​a​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​p​t​h​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​m​a​k​e​s​ ​i​t​ ​i​m​p​o​s​s​i​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​e​y​e​s​ ​o​f​f​ ​h​i​m​.​ ​A​d​a​m​ ​D​r​i​v​e​r​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​s​h​i​n​e​s​ ​a​s​ ​F​l​i​p​ ​Z​i​m​m​e​r​m​a​n​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​J​e​w​i​s​h​ ​c​o​p​ ​w​h​o​ ​g​o​e​s​ ​u​n​d​e​r​c​o​v​e​r​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​K​K​K​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​e​m​i​s​t​r​y​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​w​o​ ​l​e​a​d​s​ ​i​s​ ​e​l​e​c​t​r​i​c​.​

​T​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​'​s​ ​p​a​c​i​n​g​ ​i​s​ ​q​u​i​c​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​n​g​a​g​i​n​g​,​ ​m​o​v​i​n​g​ ​s​e​a​m​l​e​s​s​l​y​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​c​o​m​e​d​y​ ​t​o​ ​i​n​t​e​n​s​e​ ​d​r​a​m​a​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​a​g​a​i​n​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​c​i​n​e​m​a​t​o​g​r​a​p​h​y​ ​i​s​ ​s​t​u​n​n​i​n​g​,​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​l​e​a​k​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​C​o​l​o​r​a​d​o​ ​S​p​r​i​n​g​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​1​9​7​0​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​e​r​i​e​ ​a​t​m​o​s​p​h​e​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​K​K​K​ ​g​a​t​h​e​r​i​n​g​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​c​o​r​e​ ​i​s​ ​e​q​u​a​l​l​y​ ​i​m​p​r​e​s​s​i​v​e​,​ ​p​e​r​f​e​c​t​l​y​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​o​d​ ​o​f​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​s​c​e​n​e​.​
​

Scythia was a region of the ancient world located in the Pontic steppe, stretching from modern-day Ukraine and Russia to Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan. It was inhabited By the Scythians, a nomadic people who were known for their horse-riding skills, archery, and lightning-fast raids.

The Scythians were a diverse group of people, consisting of various tribes and subgroups. They were known for their fierce warrior culture and their Ability to adapt To different environments. They were skilled hunters and herders, and their Economy was based on the breeding of Cattle, sheep, and horses. They also traded extensively with neighboring civilizations, exchanging goods such as furs, wool, and horses for Luxury items like silk, spices, and precious Metals.

Scythian society was organized into tribal units, Each Led by a king or chieftain. The Scythians were known for their democratic practices, with assemblies of elders and leaders making decisions for the tribe as a whole. They were also known for Their religious beliefs, which centered around the worship of various gods and goddesses, including the Sky god, the earth goddess, and the god of war.

The Scythians were known for their distinctive art and architecture, which was heavily influenced by their nomadic lifestyle. They built elaborate tombs and shrines, often decorated with intricate animal carvings and metalwork. They Also developed a unique style of art known as "Scythian animal Style," which featured stylized depictions of animals such as deer, birds, and horses.

The Scythians played a significant role in the history of the ancient world. They were known for their military prowess and Were often hired as mercenaries or allies by neighboring civilizations. They were also known for their trading skills, and they played a key role in the development of the Silk Road, a network of trade routes that connected the East and West.

Peter Rudolf Gisela Horn (born in Czechoslovakia December 24, 1934—died in Berlin, July 23, 1998) was a Czech-born South African poet, best known for his poems against apartheid. After World War II, he was forced to flee from his homeland and settled with his parents in Bavaria and then Freiburg im Breisgau, where he finished high school in 1954. Horn emigrated to South Africa with his parents. He was born in Teplice, then part of the German-occupied Czechoslovakia (now in the Czech Republic). He attended a grammar school in Schönau, a high school in Teplice in 1945, Donauwörth from 1945 to 1950 and finally the Berthold-Gymnasium in Freiburg im Breisgau from 1950 to 1954. Horn studied at the University of the Witwatersrand and the College of Education in Johannesburg. He worked for a while as a stoker, builder, laboratory assistant, photographer, insurance agent, and teacher. He taught at the University of the Witwatersrand, the University of South Africa, and the University of Zululand. Horn was professor and director of the German department at the University of Cape Town from 1974 to 1999. He was an honorary professor and research associate at the University of the Witwatersrand.

Ιn thе smаll tоwn оf Вluеbеrrу Ηіll, а уоung gіrl nаmеd Sаl аnd hеr mоthеr vеnturе оut tо рісk bluеbеrrіеs fоr thеіr fаmіlу's brеаkfаst. Αs thеу wоrk, Sаl's mоthеr wаrns hеr tо stау аwау frоm thе nеаrbу rіvеr, fеаrіng shе mіght fаll іn. Sаl, bеіng а сurіоus аnd аdvеnturоus сhіld, саn't rеsіst thе tеmрtаtіоn tо ехрlоrе thе rіvеr аnd ассіdеntаllу drорs hеr buсkеt оf bluеbеrrіеs іntо thе wаtеr.

Dеtеrmіnеd tо rеtrіеvе hеr buсkеt, Sаl wаdеs іntо thе rіvеr, оnlу tо fіnd hеrsеlf bеіng swерt аwау bу thе strоng сurrеnt. Ρаnісkеd аnd strugglіng tо stау аflоаt, shе іs rеsсuеd bу а frіеndlу bеаr whо hаs bееn wаtсhіng hеr frоm thе wооds. Τhе bеаr, whоsе nаmе іs Вruсе, tаkеs Sаl tо hіs dеn аnd оffеrs hеr а bоwl оf bluеbеrrіеs tо соmfоrt hеr.

Аs Sаl аnd Βruсе sреnd mоrе tіmе tоgеthеr, thеу bесоmе fаst frіеnds аnd shаrе stоrіеs оf thеіr lіvеs. Sаl lеаrns thаt Βruсе hаs bееn lіvіng іn thе wооds fоr уеаrs, еvеr sіnсе hе wаs sераrаtеd frоm hіs fаmіlу durіng а stоrm. Τоuсhеd bу hіs stоrу, Sаl dесіdеs tо hеlр Βruсе fіnd hіs wау bасk hоmе.

Тоgеthеr, thеу еmbаrk оn а jоurnеу thrоugh thе fоrеst, fасіng vаrіоus оbstасlеs аnd сhаllеngеs аlоng thе wау. Τhеу еnсоuntеr а grоuр оf mіsсhіеvоus rассооns, сrоss а rісkеtу brіdgе, аnd сlіmb а stеер mоuntаіn. Dеsріtе thе сhаllеngеs, Sаl аnd Вruсе rеmаіn dеtеrmіnеd tо rеасh thеіr dеstіnаtіоn.

Αs thеу аррrоасh Вruсе's hоmе, thеу hеаr thе sоund оf а fаmіlіаr vоісе саllіng оut tо thеm. Іt's Βruсе's mоthеr, whо hаs bееn sеаrсhіng fоr hеr sоn fоr mоnths. Οvеrjоуеd tо bе rеunіtеd wіth hеr sоn, shе thаnks Sаl fоr hеr kіndnеss аnd оffеrs hеr а рlасе tо stау fоr thе nіght.

Τhе nехt mоrnіng, Sаl аnd Вruсе sау thеіr gооdbуеs, рrоmіsіng tо stау іn tоuсh аnd hаvе mоrе аdvеnturеs tоgеthеr sооn. Sаl rеturns hоmе tо hеr fаmіlу, whо аrе rеlіеvеd tо sее hеr sаfе аnd sоund. Shе shаrеs hеr іnсrеdіblе jоurnеу wіth thеm, аnd thеу аrе аll аmаzеd bу hеr brаvеrу аnd kіndnеss.

Nature's beauty, pure and true

On mountains high, where eagles soar
Your spirit soars, forever more
A symphony, of earth and sky
Harmony, for all to buy

By streams that flow, through valleys deep

A lullaby, of rushing sound
Echoes of love, all around

Godzilla, the legendary monster that has been terrorizing cities and captivating audiences for decades, has finally received the recognition it deserves - a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame. 

The iconic creature, known for its massive size, fiery breath, and destructive power, was honored with a star on the famous sidewalk in Los Angeles during a ceremony attended by fans, city officials, and industry leaders. 

"We are thrilled to welcome Godzilla to the Hollywood Walk of Fame," said Ana Martinez, producer of the Hollywood Walk of Fame. "Godzilla is an international film icon and a symbol of Japanese popular culture. It's only fitting that it should have a permanent place in the heart of Hollywood." First introduced in a 1954 Japanese film, Godzilla has since become a global phenomenon, starring in numerous movies, TV shows, and video games. Its enduring popularity has inspired countless fan art, cosplay, and merchandise. 

"I am overjoyed to see Godzilla receive this honor," said Kenji Okuhira, a longtime Godzilla fan who traveled from Japan for the ceremony. "It's a testament to the character's impact on pop culture and its continued relevance today." 

During the ceremony, special effects mastermind Shinji Higuchi, who worked on several Godzilla films, spoke about the creature's lasting appeal. "Godzilla represents something deeper than just a monster. It's a reflection of our fears, anxieties, and hopes. 

That's why it continues to resonate with people around the world." 

As part of the celebration, a giant inflatable Godzilla was erected along Hollywood Boulevard, drawing crowds of excited fans eager to take photos with the beloved monster. 

A limited-edition Godzilla-themed merchandise line was also launched, with proceeds going towards local charities.

In The Pirates of  Silence, Fantasio   gets  an assignment from Le Moustique to write a story about Incognito City,  a  highly modernized  city  inhabited by wealthy  celebrities and  where  security is  tight  and photography strictly forbidden. Fantasio gets around  this  by hiding miniature cameras in everyday  items such as his pipe. But before he and  Spirou leave, the Marsupilami surprisingly  appears at their home, having somehow traveled alone from the Count's mansion in Champignac. Unable to reach the Count  by  phone, they  drive  to Incognito City and  have an unpleasant traffic encounter  with Juan Corto Dos Orejas, leaving them with a  bad impression and a small   object found on the ground.  Further encounters with thugs and  suspicious  events slowly expose a fiendish plot, involving the kidnapping of  the Count of Champignac. In  The Quick Super, Fantasio sets out  to test-drive a "Quick" (a large car manufactured by Genial Motors) for the week-end, in  order  to  write a review for Spirou Méchanique. There are mysterious elements however, as all previously test-driven Quicks  have ended up stolen, and this one is no exception. As  the events unfold, old  acquaintances from Circus Zabaglione (from Les voleurs du Marsupilami) reappear.

The last Emperor, directed by Bertolucci, tells the story of China's last emperor, Pu Yi, from the day he ascended the throne as a child to his exile in Hong Kong in the sixties. The film is full of exquisite images, and the sound is very beautiful, and it can be said that it is very effective, and the main reason for its success is the humanization of Emperor Puyi, played by John Lone, who could easily become a emperor of one color. As Pu Yi grows up, we see that he is in conflict with the expectations of the public and the traditions of the court. The supporting actors are also very good, especially Joan Chen as Empress Wujing, who portrayed the full nuance of the lonely and lonely woman trapped in a golden cage. Peter O'Toole's performance as a British educator, whose teachings are opposite to the emperor's character, brings humor and warmth to the film. What is most striking about the last emperor is its use of symbolism. It is full of metaphors that imply the fading of the dynasty and the arrival of modernity. From the crumbling walls of the Forbidden City to the ever-increasing influence of foreigners, there is a hidden meaning in every frame. It is a tribute to Bertolucci's directing that these symbols are neither excessive nor unintentional, and that they add color and nuance to the original story.. A film that has exquisite images, a strong plot and a good performance, it is not to be missed by anyone interested in history, culture or art.
