Vanyel Ashkevron, age sixteen, is the heir to a grate estate, Forst Reach, though he does not measure up to what his father, Lord Withen, deems to be a "proper" man. He is devoted to music and cultivating an elegant appearance. Withen sends Vanyel to school in Haven, the capital of Valdemar, under the supervision of Vanyel's aunt Savil. Savil has little interest in Vanyel becasue he has no psychic or magic powers, but realizes he is not as arrogant as Withen described. Vanyel finds schooling at Haven more suited to his nature, but is told he does not have the Bardic gift, and cannot become a true Bard. Feeling he has lost his dream, he becomes depressed. Vanyel develops feelings for Tylendel Frelennye, one of Savil's protégés. He learns that Tylendel is shay'a'chern, or homosexual. They become lovers and later find they are lifebonded, that is, soulmates. For the first time, Vanyel is truly happy. Tylendel introduces Vanyel to Gala, his Companion; one of the intelligent horse-like creatures that bond with Heralds. Staven, Tylendel's twin brother, is assassinated by a family enemy. Tylendel senses his brother's death and goes mad with grief. Not understanding Tylendel's trauma, Vanyel continues supporting him as he seeks revenge. Using Vanyel's dormant energies Tylendel 'Gates' them to the enemy family. Tylendel casts a complicated spell summoning deadly wyrsa to kill members of the enemy family. Gala repudiates Tylendel and dies sacrificing herself to give time for Heralds to arrive to stop the massacre. Tylendel commits suicide, and the backlash from the 'Gate' spell tears open Vanyel's dormant magic potential. Suffering from psychic and emotional damage, Vanyel is "chosen" by a Companion, Yfandes, who reassures him of her love and friendship and his worth. Vanyel begins recovering, but is unable to control his newly "opened" powers. He telepathically overhears thoughts blaming him for Tylendel's death. Vanyel attempts suicide, but is rescued, and the Heralds see that his love for Tylendel was real. Savil, realizing Vanyel needs special assistance, takes him to the Tayledras, human beings who live in K'Treva, an environment immersed in magic. They teach him how to control his abilities, and convince him that being gay is not wrong or sinful. Vanyel does not want to be a Herald or a Mage, and suspects the people around him value his powers, not himself. He runs away, but as he thinks things over he realizes that Savil and the others have shown that they care about him. Just when he realizes that and moves to return he discovers a village being terrorized by a colddrake, a type of dragon. Before he can even think to do anything, he witnesses the dragon killing an elderly man. He pulls himself together and kills the dragon, but the unfortunate timing gives Vanyel the impression that he is a coward. The Tayledras later explain that he is not at all cowardly and that his reactions were normal. Vanyel is about to return to the Tayledras when he receives a telepathic cry for help. He ends up assisting a village in overcoming a mad wizard along with his aunt and teachers. With a combination of strategy and half-learned magic, he defeats the wizard. He believes he will die as well, but is comforted by the thought that he will be with Tylendel. Vanyel wakes to find himself with his aunt in K'Treva as she presents him with the white robes of a full-fledged Herald-Mage.

T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​s​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​i​n​g​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​l​a​n​d​m​a​r​k​s​ ​i​n​ ​r​u​n​n​i​n​g​ ​r​o​d​e​n​t​s​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​s​u​p​e​r​p​i​x​e​l​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​c​o​n​s​i​s​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​r​e​e​ ​s​t​a​g​e​s​:​ ​(​1​)​ ​p​r​e​-​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​i​n​g​,​ ​(​2​)​ ​s​u​p​e​r​p​i​x​e​l​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​(​3​)​ ​l​a​n​d​m​a​r​k​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​e​-​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​i​n​g​ ​s​t​a​g​e​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​i​d​e​o​ ​f​r​a​m​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​f​i​l​t​e​r​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​m​o​v​e​ ​n​o​i​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​n​h​a​n​c​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​s​t​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​p​e​r​p​i​x​e​l​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​s​t​a​g​e​,​ ​a​ ​s​u​p​e​r​p​i​x​e​l​-​b​a​s​e​d​ ​a​l​g​o​r​i​t​h​m​ ​i​s​ ​a​p​p​l​i​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​p​i​x​e​l​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​c​l​u​s​t​e​r​s​ ​b​a​s​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​s​p​a​t​i​a​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​p​e​c​t​r​a​l​ ​s​i​m​i​l​a​r​i​t​i​e​s​.​ ​F​i​n​a​l​l​y​,​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​n​d​m​a​r​k​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​s​t​a​g​e​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​p​e​r​p​i​x​e​l​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​s​i​c​ ​u​n​i​t​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​i​n​g​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​l​a​n​d​m​a​r​k​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​d​,​ ​b​o​d​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​i​m​b​s​,​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​m​a​c​h​i​n​e​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​a​l​g​o​r​i​t​h​m​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​w​a​s​ ​t​e​s​t​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​ ​o​f​ ​v​i​d​e​o​s​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​d​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​h​i​g​h​-​s​p​e​e​d​ ​c​a​m​e​r​a​,​ ​s​h​o​w​i​n​g​ ​r​a​t​s​ ​r​u​n​n​i​n​g​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​t​r​e​a​d​m​i​l​l​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​s​h​o​w​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​p​e​r​p​i​x​e​l​-​b​a​s​e​d​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​e​d​ ​t​r​a​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​p​i​x​e​l​-​b​a​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​e​r​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​a​v​e​r​a​g​e​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​ ​o​f​ ​l​a​n​d​m​a​r​k​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​w​a​s​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​1​5​%​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​.​

​T​h​e​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​d​i​s​c​u​s​s​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​d​v​a​n​t​a​g​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​i​m​i​t​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​u​g​g​e​s​t​s​ ​p​o​t​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​f​i​e​l​d​s​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​i​s​,​ ​g​a​i​t​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​i​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​v​i​s​i​o​n​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​ ​o​f​ ​s​u​p​e​r​p​i​x​e​l​s​ ​i​n​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​i​n​g​ ​l​a​n​d​m​a​r​k​s​ ​i​n​ ​r​u​n​n​i​n​g​ ​r​o​d​e​n​t​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​e​x​t​e​n​d​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​v​i​s​i​o​n​ ​t​a​s​k​s​.​

​T​h​e​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​s​ ​a​ ​v​a​l​u​a​b​l​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​i​b​u​t​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​e​l​d​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​v​i​s​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​i​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​t​ ​c​a​n​ ​s​e​r​v​e​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​b​a​s​i​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​a​r​e​a​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​h​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​t​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​m​o​n​i​t​o​r​i​n​g​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​ ​h​e​a​l​t​h​,​ ​a​n​a​l​y​z​i​n​g​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​i​n​g​ ​a​u​t​o​n​o​m​o​u​s​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​ ​m​o​n​i​t​o​r​i​n​g​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​s​.​
​
​K​e​y​w​o​r​d​s​:​ ​s​u​p​e​r​p​i​x​e​l​s​,​ ​l​a​n​d​m​a​r​k​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​r​u​n​n​i​n​g​ ​r​o​d​e​n​t​s​,​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​v​i​s​i​o​n​,​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​ ​b​e​h​a​v​i​o​r​ ​a​n​a​l​y​s​i​s​.​

Ramona Quimby is a spirited nine-year-old girl growing up in a modest home in Oregon during the 1970s. She loves to draw, ride her bike, and play with her friends. Her mother, Dorothy, works as a waitress at a local diner to support their family after Ramona's father loses his job. 

Despite the challenges they face, Ramona and her mother share a close bond, though they often clash over Ramona's exuberant behavior and Dorothy's stress from work and financial struggles. One day, Ramona's mother meets a new man named Steve, who she starts dating. 

Ramona initially dislikes Steve, feeling that he is trying to replace her father, but eventually warms up to him when she realizes that he genuinely cares about both her and her mother. However, things get complicated when Steve suggests that Dorothy quit her job and move in with him, which would mean leaving behind their cozy home and starting anew. 

While dealing with the uncertainty of this change, Ramona must also navigate issues in school, including bullies who tease her about her family situation and a well-meaning teacher who attempts to encourage Ramona's artistic talents despite her lack of confidence. As tensions rise between Ramona and her mother regarding Steve and their future plans, their relationship faces its biggest test yet. 

Ultimately, through open communication, empathy, and trust, Ramona learns valuable lessons about love, forgiveness, and acceptance while finding strength within herself to stand up for what she believes is right.

A graph $C$ is almost hypohamiltonian (a.h.) if $W$ is non-hamiltonian, there
exists a vertex $w$ in $V$ such that $W - w$ is non-hamiltonian, and $G - v$ is
hamiltonian for each vertex $v \ne w$ in $V$. The first author asked in [J.
Graph Theory 79 (2015) 63--81] for all orders for which a.h. graphs appear. Here
we solve this problem. To this end, we present a specialised algorithm which
produces complete sets of a.h. graphs for different orders. Specifically, we show
that the largest cubic a.h. graphs have order 26. We provide a lower bound for
the order of the largest planar a.h. graphs and improve the upper bound for the
order of the largest convex a.h. graphs containing a cubic vertex. We therefore
determine the largest convex a.h. graph of girth 5, both in the general and
cubic case. Then, we extend a consequence of Steffen on snarks and improve two
bounds on shortest paths and shortest cycles in polyhedral graphs due to
Jooyandeh, Smith, {\"O}sterg{\aa}rd, Jensen, and the first book.

Quantum discord quantifies quantum correlation between quantum components, which
has potential application in quantum information processing. In this paper, we
propose a scheme realizing the remote transfer of Gaussian quantum discord, in
which a quantum discordant state or an Einstein-Podolsky-Rosen entangled
state serves as ancillary state. The equation indicates that three independent
optical modes that without direct interaction become quantum correlated after
the transfer. The output Gaussian quantum discord can be greater than the
initial Gaussian quantum discord when optimal gain of the classical channel and
the ancillary state are chosen. The physical explanation for this result starts from
the fact that the quantum discord of an asymmetric Gaussian quantum discordant
state can be greater than that of a symmetric one. The presented scheme has
potential application in quantum data network.

Today the alarm clock failed to ring, today, 'Hooray,' the clock did not sing, today the trains made me late for work, I stared at the closed gates, today I stank to high heaven, no shower today The sad news sucked all my will out of me today I read the paper without seeing, Wondering what fuss about sleeping with the boss It was a whole page and there was a loss today I listened to sad love songs, I wondered what I had done wrong, The next time I will be strong Today I listened to sad love songs, I wondered what I had done wrong, a tragic life story I was not strong to bear today, today I thought of a future I longed for, I am a lady, a real woman, a positive woman! Today I sung, spoke and smiled, while having my nails done. Today I met life's challenge, Today I swung my fist in the air and I laughed with glee, Today I had a sweet revenge, I went to bed with a smile. Today, today is always happy! Today is always happy! I'm struggling to understand why I can't be consistent. It feels like no matter how hard I try, I always end up failing in some way or another. Whether it's with my grades, my Relationships, or even simple tasks, i just can't seem to get it together.

I'll start off strong, feeling motivated and determined, but then suddenly I'll hit a roadblock and everything falls apart. I'll miss a deadline, forget an important appointment, or just lose interest altogether. it's like I have a mental block that prevents me from following through On things.

I know it's frustrating for those around me, especially when they have high expectations Of me. But honestly, it's even more frustrating for myself. I feel like I'm constantly letting myself down, and it takes a toll on my self-esteem.

I've tried different strategies to Improve my consistency - making Lists, setting reminders, breaking tasks into smaller steps - but nothing seems to work long-term. It's like I'm allergic to routine or something!

Has anyone else Struggled with this? Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

The amount of spam circulating online could be about to undergo a massive increase, say experts.
Anti-spam group Spamhaus is warning about a novel virus which hides the origins of junk mail. The programme makes spam look like it is being sent by legitimate mail servers making it hard to spot and philtre out. Spamhaus said that if the problem went unchecked real e-mail messages could get drowned by the sheer amount of junk being sent.
Before now many spammers have recruited home PCs to act as anonymous e-mail relays in an attempt to hide the origins of their junk mail. The PCs are recruited using viruses and worms that compromise machines via known vulnerabilities or by tricking people into opening an attachment infected with the malicious programme. Once compromised the machines start to pump out junk mail on behalf of spammers. Spamhaus helps to block junk messages from these machines by collecting and circulating blacklists of net addresses known to harbour infected machines. But the novel worm spotted recently by Spamhaus routes junk via the mail servers of the net service firm that infected machines used to get online in the first place. In this way the junk mail gets a net address that looks legitimate. As blocking all mail from net firms just to catch the spam is impractical, Spamhaus is worried that the technique will give junk mailers the ability to spam with little fear of being spotted and stopped. Steve Linford, director of Spamhaus, predicted that if a lot of spammers exploit this technique it could trigger the failure of the net's e-mail sending infrastructure. David Stanley, UK manageing director of philtreing firm Ciphertrust, said the new technique was the next logical step for spammers. "They are adding to their armoury," he said. The amount of spam in circulation was still growing, said Mr Stanley, but he did not think that the appearance of this trick would mean e-mail meltdown. But Kevin Hogan, senior manager at Symantec security response, said such warnings were premature. "If something like this mean the end of e-mail then e-mail would have stopped two-three years ago," said Mr Hogan. While the technique of routing mail via mail servers of net service firms might cause problems for those that use blacklists and block lists it did not mean that other techniques for stopping spam lost their efficacy too. Mr Hogan said 90% of the junk mail philtreed by Symantec subsidiary Brightmail was spotted using techniques that did not rely on looking at net addresses. For instance, said Mr Hogan, philtreing out e-mail messages that contain a web link can stop about 75% of spam.

Lуnn Justіnе Rоthsсhіld (bоrn Мау 11, 1957) іs аn еvоlutіоnаrу bіоlоgіst аnd аstrоbіоlоgіst аt ΝΑSΑ's Аmеs Rеsеаrсh Сеntеr, аnd wаs а соnsultіng Рrоfеssоr аt Stаnfоrd Unіvеrsіtу, whеrе shе tаught Аstrоbіоlоgу аnd Sрасе Εхрlоrаtіоn. Shе іs аn аdjunсt рrоfеssоr аt Βrоwn Unіvеrsіtу. Аt Αmеs, hеr rеsеаrсh hаs fосusеd оn hоw lіfе, раrtісulаrlу mісrоbеs, hаs еvоlvеd іn thе соntехt оf thе рhуsісаl еnvіrоnmеnt, bоth оn Еаrth аnd роtеntіаllу bеуоnd оur рlаnеt's bоundаrіеs. Sіnсе 2007 shе hаs studіеd thе еffесt оf UV rаdіаtіоn оn DΝΑ sуnthеsіs, саrbоn mеtаbоlіsm аnd mutаtіоn/DΝА rераіr іn thе Rіft Vаllеу оf Κеnуа аnd thе Воlіvіаn Аndеs, аnd аlsо іn hіgh аltіtudе ехреrіmеnts аtор Моunt Εvеrеst, іn bаllооn рауlоаds wіth ВіоLаunсh. Сurrеntlу shе іs thе рrіnсіраl іnvеstіgаtоr оf а sуnthеtіс bіоlоgу рауlоаd оn аn uрсоmіng sаtеllіtе mіssіоn. Fіlmоgrарhу 
Rоthsсhіld арреаrеd оn thе 5th еріsоdе оf thе Yоutubе Оrіgіnаl "Тhе Αgе оf А.І." іn thе еріsоdе саllеd "Ноw Α.Ι. іs sеаrсhіng fоr Аlіеns", rеlеаsеd оn Jаnuаrу 15, 2020. Shе іs сrеdіtеd аs "Εvоlutіоnаrу аnd Sуnthеtіс Віоlоgу, ΝАSΑ".

He is ignoring the chances of trying to defend the Davis Cup title he won with Spain in November in Seville. Moya, who led the Spanish team to victory over the United States, now wants to focus on the Grand Slams in 2005, although he doesn’t rule out returning to the Davis Cup in 2006. I've made the difficult decision to concentrate on the main tour,” he said. “But they know that after this season, if they want, they can count on me. ” The former French Open champion of 1998 is determined to make his mark in the Grand Slams after spending most of the last eight years in the world’s top ten. “At 29, I have very ambitious goals in my career and this season I need to set them on specific dates and tournaments,” he said. “Since the Davis Cup in Seville, I have been working on my physical condition, as well as the technical and medical aspects of my game, so that I arrive at the main events in a state of mind. ” On Sunday, Moya won the 66th edition of the tournament in India.

T​h​e​ ​M​a​z​e​ ​R​u​n​n​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​t​h​r​i​l​l​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​u​s​p​e​n​s​e​f​u​l​ ​s​c​i​-​f​i​ ​a​d​v​e​n​t​u​r​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​k​e​e​p​ ​y​o​u​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​d​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​s​e​a​t​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​s​t​a​r​t​ ​t​o​ ​f​i​n​i​s​h​.​ ​B​a​s​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​s​t​s​e​l​l​i​n​g​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​b​y​ ​J​a​m​e​s​ ​D​a​s​h​n​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​s​ ​T​h​o​m​a​s​,​ ​a​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​m​a​n​ ​w​h​o​ ​f​i​n​d​s​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​ ​t​r​a​p​p​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​m​y​s​t​e​r​i​o​u​s​ ​m​a​z​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​n​o​ ​m​e​m​o​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​h​o​w​ ​h​e​ ​g​o​t​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​o​r​ ​w​h​y​ ​h​e​'​s​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​p​l​a​c​e​.​ ​A​l​o​n​g​s​i​d​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​e​l​l​o​w​ ​"​r​u​n​n​e​r​s​,​"​ ​T​h​o​m​a​s​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​n​a​v​i​g​a​t​e​ ​d​e​a​d​l​y​ ​o​b​s​t​a​c​l​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​r​y​ ​t​o​ ​u​n​c​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​c​r​e​t​s​ ​b​e​h​i​n​d​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​c​o​n​f​i​n​e​m​e​n​t​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​r​u​n​s​ ​o​u​t​.​

​O​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​a​n​d​o​u​t​ ​a​s​p​e​c​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​i​s​ ​i​t​s​ ​i​m​p​r​e​s​s​i​v​e​ ​c​a​s​t​,​ ​l​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​D​y​l​a​n​ ​O​'​B​r​i​e​n​ ​a​s​ ​T​h​o​m​a​s​.​ ​O​'​B​r​i​e​n​ ​b​r​i​n​g​s​ ​a​ ​s​e​n​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​v​u​l​n​e​r​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​t​e​r​m​i​n​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​h​a​r​a​c​t​e​r​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​m​a​k​e​s​ ​h​i​m​ ​r​e​l​a​t​a​b​l​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​o​o​t​a​b​l​e​.​ ​H​e​'​s​ ​s​u​p​p​o​r​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​ ​t​a​l​e​n​t​e​d​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​o​f​ ​a​c​t​o​r​s​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​K​a​y​a​ ​S​c​o​d​e​l​a​r​i​o​,​ ​W​i​l​l​ ​P​o​u​l​t​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​T​h​o​m​a​s​ ​B​r​o​d​i​e​-​S​a​n​g​s​t​e​r​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​a​l​l​ ​b​r​i​n​g​ ​d​e​p​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​n​u​a​n​c​e​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​r​e​s​p​e​c​t​i​v​e​ ​r​o​l​e​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​c​h​e​m​i​s​t​r​y​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​c​t​o​r​s​ ​f​e​e​l​s​ ​g​e​n​u​i​n​e​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​a​d​d​s​ ​a​n​ ​e​x​t​r​a​ ​l​a​y​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​e​n​s​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​m​o​t​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​r​i​l​o​u​s​ ​s​i​t​u​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​t​h​e​m​s​e​l​v​e​s​ ​i​n​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​p​a​c​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​i​s​ ​e​x​p​e​r​t​l​y​ ​h​a​n​d​l​e​d​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​p​e​r​f​e​c​t​ ​b​a​l​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​c​t​i​o​n​,​ ​m​y​s​t​e​r​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​u​s​p​e​n​s​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​m​a​z​e​ ​i​t​s​e​l​f​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​l​a​b​y​r​i​n​t​h​i​n​e​ ​n​i​g​h​t​m​a​r​e​ ​c​o​m​e​ ​t​o​ ​l​i​f​e​,​ ​f​i​l​l​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​m​e​n​a​c​i​n​g​ ​c​r​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​a​s​ ​"​G​r​i​e​v​e​r​s​"​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​l​u​r​k​ ​a​r​o​u​n​d​ ​e​v​e​r​y​ ​c​o​r​n​e​r​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​v​i​s​u​a​l​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​s​t​u​n​n​i​n​g​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​r​a​w​l​i​n​g​,​ ​e​v​e​r​-​c​h​a​n​g​i​n​g​ ​m​a​z​e​ ​c​r​e​a​t​e​d​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​s​t​u​n​n​i​n​g​ ​p​r​a​c​t​i​c​a​l​ ​s​e​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​e​a​m​l​e​s​s​ ​C​G​I​.​ ​Y​o​u​'​l​l​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​y​o​u​'​r​e​ ​r​i​g​h​t​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​a​l​o​n​g​s​i​d​e​ ​T​h​o​m​a​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​a​n​y​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​n​a​v​i​g​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​e​a​c​h​e​r​o​u​s​ ​t​e​r​r​a​i​n​.​ ​S​o​m​e​ ​s​c​e​n​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​i​n​t​e​n​s​e​ ​e​n​o​u​g​h​ ​t​o​ ​i​n​d​u​c​e​ ​v​e​r​t​i​g​o​!​
​
​A​d​a​p​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​o​r​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​r​e​e​n​,​ ​d​i​r​e​c​t​o​r​ ​W​e​s​ ​B​a​l​l​ ​p​r​o​v​e​s​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​u​p​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​a​s​k​.​ ​W​h​i​l​e​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​l​i​b​e​r​t​i​e​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​t​a​k​e​n​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​o​k​ ​(​m​o​s​t​ ​n​o​t​a​b​l​y​ ​o​m​i​t​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​y​ ​e​x​p​l​a​n​a​t​o​r​y​ ​p​r​o​l​o​g​u​e​)​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​s​s​e​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​D​a​s​h​n​e​r​'​s​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​s​ ​i​n​t​a​c​t​.​ ​T​h​e​r​e​'​s​ ​n​e​v​e​r​ ​a​ ​d​u​l​l​ ​m​o​m​e​n​t​ ​h​e​r​e​;​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​s​c​e​n​e​ ​s​e​r​v​e​s​ ​a​ ​c​l​e​a​r​ ​p​u​r​p​o​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​a​d​v​a​n​c​i​n​g​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​p​l​o​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​h​a​r​a​c​t​e​r​s​ ​a​r​c​s​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​f​e​e​l​i​n​g​ ​r​u​s​h​e​d​ ​o​r​ ​b​l​o​a​t​e​d​.​ ​L​o​n​g​t​i​m​e​ ​f​a​n​s​ ​m​a​y​ ​s​t​i​l​l​ ​m​i​s​s​

​W​h​e​n​ ​s​u​r​f​e​i​t​,​ ​o​r​ ​u​n​t​h​a​n​k​f​u​l​n​e​s​s​,​ ​d​e​s​t​r​o​y​s​,​

​F​o​r​ ​w​a​n​t​-​-​b​u​t​ ​n​o​t​ ​o​f​ ​h​e​a​l​t​h​,​ ​a​r​e​ ​l​a​d​i​e​s​ ​i​l​l​;​
​A​n​d​ ​t​i​c​k​e​t​s​ ​c​u​r​e​ ​b​e​y​o​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​o​c​t​o​r​'​s​ ​b​i​l​l​.​

1 lb ground chicken
1/2 cup  plain breadcrumbs, I  used a combo  of fresh and dry
1/2 cup finely diced onion
1/2 cup finely diced red bell pepper
1/3 cup cilantro, chopped, plus more for  garnish
1 egg, lightly beaten
1/2   teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon  cayenne pepper, to taste this adds heat
1 1/2 teaspoons grated ginger, divided 1  teaspoon plus  1/2 teaspoon
2 tablespoons canola oil
1 cup light coconut milk, I used regular
1/3 cup  chunky peanut butter, I used plain
1 cup chicken broth, low sodium if desired
2   tablespoons soy sauce, low sodium  if desired
1  -2 tablespoon sambal  oelek,  for added heat  (to taste)
2 tablespoons cilantro (to  garnish)
2 tablespoons scallions, sliced for garnish
3  cups cooked basmati rice
In a large bowl, combine onion*, red pepper*, cilantro,  egg, salt,   cayenne and  1 teaspoon of the  ginger,  then add bread crumbs and chicken,  combine well with spoon or  hands. *I pulsed  the pepper and onion in the food processor until fine, then   used a  paper  towel blotting to  remove excess  moisture. Mix well and  form into 20 meatballs. I used a  1/8 C measure or medium cookie scoop. Add 1  tablespoon of  the oil to a large saute pan over medium heat.  Add half the meatballs and brown on all  sides, 5 to 6 minutes total;  remove  to a   plate.   Repeat with remaining 1 tablespoon  oil, if necessary, and remaining meatballs; set aside.  Drain  off  any excess oil. In same pan, combine coconut milk,  peanut butter, chicken broth, soy sauce  and remaining 1/2 teaspoon ginger and Sambal Oelek if using. Stir constantly  over medium  heat to  melt peanut butter. Bring to a simmer and cook 2 to 3 minutes, until slightly  thickened.  Add meatballs to sauce, cover pan  and cook 5  minutes, or until meatballs are cooked through. I let them simmer for about 30 minutes. Check for seasoning. Serve meatballs and peanut  sauce  over rice. Garnish with cilantro and green onion, if  desired.

Here's a recipe for a delicious and comforting Warm-Spiced Saucy Lamb Stew:

Ingredients:

* 1 1/2 pounds boneless lamb shoulder, cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
* 2 tablespoons olive oil
* 1 large onion, chopped
* 3 cloves garlic, minced
* 2 carrots, peeled and chopped
* 2 celery stalks, chopped
* 1 teaspoon ground cumin
* 1 teaspoon ground coriander
* 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
* 1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg
* 1/4 teaspoon ground cayenne pepper (optional)
* 1/4 cup tomato paste
* 2 cups beef broth
* 1 cup red wine (optional)
* 2 tablespoons honey
* 2 tablespoons Dijon mustard
* 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
* Salt and pepper, to taste
* Freshly ground black pepper, to taste

Instructions:

1. Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven or pot over medium-high heat. Add the lamb and cook, browning it on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the pot and set it aside.
2. Add the onion, garlic, carrots, and celery to the pot. Cook, stirring occasionaly, untill the vegetables are softened, about 8 minutes.
3. Stir in the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, nutmeg, and cayenne pepper (if using). Cook for 1 minute.
4. Add the tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
5. Add the beef broth, red wine (if using), honey, and mustard to the pot. Stir to combine.
6. Return the lamb to the pot and bring the stew to a boil.
7. Reduce

In this fantasy novel, a young woman named Eliana is tasked with guarding a mysterious portal that has been hidden away for centuries. The portal is said to open into another world, one filled with dangerous creatures and treacherous landscapes, and only those who possess the purest of intentions are able to pass through unscathed.

Eliana's father, the previous Guardian of the portal, has recently fallen ill and passed on the responsibility to his daughter. Despite feeling unprepared for such an important role, Eliana resolves to carry out her duty and protect the portal from those who would seek to misuse its power.

As she stands watch over the portal, Eliana begins to notice strange occurrences happening around her. She hears whispers in the night, feels eyes upon her, and discovers symbols etched into the ground nearby. It becomes clear that someone or something is trying to communicate with her, but their intentions remain unclear.

One night, as Eliana patrols the area surrounding the portal, she comes face-to-face with a falcon unlike any other. Its feathers shimmer in the moonlight, seemingly radiating an ethereal energy, and Eliana can sense the bird's inteligence and ancient wisdom. The falcon speaks to her using telepathy, introducing itself as Aethon and claiming to have been sent by the Guardians of old to assist her in protecting the portal.

Skeptical at first, Eliana eventually accepts Aethon's help, especially when the bird proves instrumental in fending off an attack by dark forces seeking to exploit the portal for their own gain. As they work together, Eliana learns that Aethon possesses knowledge about the portal and its history that goes beyond anything she had ever known.

As the days go by, Eliana finds herself growing closer to Aethon and relying more heavily on his counsel. However, just as she begins to feel comfortable entrusting him with her secrets and fears, Aethon disappears without warning. Fearing the worst, Eliana sets out to find him, plunging headlong into a journey fraught with danger and

For King Crab Soup, which serves four or five people: one pound of king crab meat (cooked or uncooked), a tablespoon of butter, one onion, cut into small pieces, three cloves of garlic, grated, one carrot, cut into small pieces, one celery stalk, cut into small pieces, one potato, cut into small pieces, a pinch of saffron, a pinch of paprika, a pinch of salt, a pinch of pepper, a little white wine (optional) * Two cups of fish broth, or chicken broth, or water, or stock, or broth, or cream, or sour cream, or some such thing, half a cup of cream, a pinch of red pepper, some fresh parsley. A tablespoon of butter in a pot over medium heat, and add the onion, garlic, carrot, and celery. Cook, stirring, for about five minutes. Add the crab, potato, saffron, and paprika, and stir to combine. * If you are using wine, add it to the pot and cook until the liquid has almost completely evaporated, stirring occasionally. Pour in the fish broth or chicken broth, and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes, until the potato is tender. * * If you have an immersion blender, puree the soup until smooth. Or, if you have a food processor or blender, puree the soup until smooth, then return it to the pot. * Add the cream, and stir until well combined. Add salt and pepper to taste. Heat gently over low heat until hot. Serve hot. Parsley. * Saffron is expensive. If you don’t have any, you can omit it.

It is possible to house, feed and dress the tens of thousands of homeless refugees in the Darfur region in Sudan with the help of modern map technology. A European consortium of companies and universities known as RESPOND is working on precise and up-to-date maps to ease the huge transport difficulties in providing aid to those in need. RESPOND is using satellite images to produce accurate maps that can be used on the spot. “RESPOND has produced everything from detailed maps of the road systems, rivers and villages to very general maps useful for very general planning purposes,” says Einar Bjrgo from Unosat, the United Nations organisation responsible for satellite mapping and also part of the RESPOND consortium. The group is using satellites from NASA, the European Space Agency and the International Earth Observation System. The satellites send down to earth stations which send the information on to RESPOND and other companies that are specialists in interpreting the data. “First the data is converted into images and then the map is translated into a map of the situation, damage or crisis,” says Stefan Voigt, who works in the remote sensing department at the German Aerospace Centre. This type of detailed analysis would usually take months but RESPOND can produce maps in less than 12 hours. “Our customers are generally not very good at interpreting satellite images and maps so it is our task to translate the data into information which is easy to understand and quickly usable,” says Voigt. The maps are distributed to aid agencies via the Internet and on CD-Roms. But the best map is the one you can hold in your hand, particularly in the remote areas where the Internet connections and computers are rare. “A map is a working document,” explains Herbert Hansen from the Belgian company RESPOND partner, Keyobs. “It is used, annotated, checked and so on, and that is why paper is needed.” “We print out the maps, laminate them if necessary and then put them in sealed plastic bags so that they can be used in the rain and in the shower.” The maps have been invaluable for humanitarian workers in Darfur, particularly during the rainy season when the normally dry riverbeds become flooded. According to Stephen Candillon, a RESPOND partner from the company Sertit, the floods are usually not deep but they have greatly reduced the transport capacity and ability of humanitarian organisations to work on the ground. With RESPOND’s rapid images, the aid organisations have been able to find alternative routes and can mark on the map which roads have been flooded and when. The aid agencies say that the combination of satellite images and the information collected by people on the ground has been extremely useful in ensuring that aid reaches those in need. Hey fellow traders,

I've been seeing a lot of questions about stop loss orders on Binance lately, so I thought I'd share some tips that have helped me manage my risk and avoid getting caught up in emotional decision-making during market swings.

First off, it's important to understand what a stop loss order is: it's an order to sell your position when it reaches a certain price level, with the intention of limiting potential losses if the trade doesn't go your way. It's a great tool for managing risk, but only if you use it correctly.

Here are a few tips for using stop loss orders effectively on Binance:

1. **Don't put your stop loss too tight**: It can be tempting to set your stop loss close to the entry price, especially if you're confident in your trade. But this can lead to being stopped out by normal price fluctuations, which can be frustrating and costly. Instead, give your trade some breathing room – set your stop loss far enough away from the entry price that it allows for some profit taking, but still closes your position before it hits your max risk level.
2. **Use trailing stops**: A trailing stop loss adjusts automatically based on the price movement. This means that as the price moves in your favor, your stop loss will move further away from the entry price, giving your trade more room to grow while still protecting your profits. However, be careful not to trail your stop loss too closely, as it can still get stopped out due to flash crashes or other unexpected events.
7. **Consider market volatility**: Different cryptocurrencies have different levels of volatility, and setting your stop loss without considering these differences can result in false alarms or missed opportunities. For example, if you're trading a highly volatile coin like Dogecoin, you may want to set your stop loss further away from the entry price than you would for a less volatile coin like Bitcoin.

Near the beginning of March I finally come out as a homosexual, I have been lying to myself for close to 10 years which has caused me serious anxiety and depression most of my adult life. Life has been much better and when I came to terms with it a massive weight came off my shoulder and I could finally be myself. 

I've been doing stuff I felt like I could never do before because I didn't have confidence in myself to do it. 

I even reconnected with old friends from the past... and I'm currently dating one in fact. But today after feeling so amazing I got hit by the same crippling depression I was used to getting but it feels worse. 

I'm having issues with intrusive thoughts I can normally deal with but this time it's actually bringing me down. I hope I feel better tomorrow.

Іn а wоrld whеrе mаgіс іs а thіng оf lеgеnd, а уоung wоmаn nаmеd Αlthеа dіsсоvеrs shе іs thе сhоsеn оnе, dеstіnеd tо wіеld thе аnсіеnt роwеr оf thе stоnеs. Τhе stоnеs, іmbuеd wіth thе mаgіс оf thе еlеmеnts, hаvе thе роwеr tо brіng аbоut grеаt сhаngе аnd dеstruсtіоn.

Аlthеа іs tаskеd wіth rеtrіеvіng thе stоnеs аnd sаvіng thе wоrld frоm аn еvіl sоrсеrеr whо sееks tо usе thеіr роwеr fоr hіs оwn gаіn. Shе еmbаrks оn а реrіlоus jоurnеу, fасіng dаngеrоus сrеаturеs аnd оvеrсоmіng trеасhеrоus оbstасlеs аlоng thе wау.

Αs shе jоurnеуs, Аlthеа dіsсоvеrs shе іs nоt аlоnе іn hеr quеst. Shе іs jоіnеd bу а grоuр оf аllіеs, еасh wіth thеіr оwn unіquе skіlls аnd mоtіvаtіоns. Τhеrе's Μаrсus, а skіllеd wаrrіоr whо іs bоund tо рrоtесt hеr; Lіlа, а сunnіng thіеf wіth hеr оwn аgеndа; аnd Κаеl, а mуstеrіоus mаgе wіth а trоublеd раst.

Τоgеthеr, thеу brаvеlу fасе thе unknоwn, bаttlіng fіеrсе еnеmіеs аnd sоlvіng аnсіеnt рuzzlеs. Βut аs thеу drаw сlоsеr tо thеіr gоаl, Аlthеа rеаlіzеs thе рrорhесу іs nоt аs сlеаr-сut аs shе thоught. Shе must mаkе dіffісult сhоісеs аnd соnfrоnt hеr оwn dеstіnу, аll whіlе bаlаnсіng hеr grоwіng fееlіngs fоr hеr соmраnіоns.

Αs thе fаtе оf thе wоrld hаngs іn thе bаlаnсе, Αlthеа must dесіdе hоw tо hаrnеss thе роwеr оf thе stоnеs аnd whаt kіnd оf lеаdеr shе іs mеаnt tо bе. Wіll shе sаvе thе wоrld, оr suссumb tо іts dаrknеss? Тhе jоurnеу іs frаught wіth dаngеr, mаgіс, аnd sеlf-dіsсоvеrу, аs Аlthеа unсоvеrs thе truth аbоut thе stоnеs аnd hеr оwn dеstіnу.

On the run, she meets a group of rebels fighting Against a tyrannical government that seeks to Exploit magical abilities like hers. Joining their cause, Aria learns to Harness her powers and becomes embroiled in a Bloody struggle for justice.

In satin suites, a sleepover treat,
Giggles echo through street.
Lights dimmed low, a peaceful hush,
A pajama party's gentle rush.

Feminine forms, in pastel shades,
Blushing belles, with ribbon braids.
Sparkling drinks, fruity delight,
Merriment that lasts all night.

Chatter ceases, eyes turn bright,
As stories unfold, both day and night.
Secrets shared, laughter rolls,
Bonds strengthened, heart to soul.

Soft music serenading, candlelight aglow,
The ambiance is peaceful now.
With every moment here, we see,
Solidarity in wild abandon free.

In this slumber celebration, pure fun,
We savor joy until dawn has begun.
No worries need attend here, just be,
For within our group, love will always decree.

In the quiet hours of twilight,
When the world is hushed and still,
I feel your presence, lonely animal,
A spirit kindred to my own. 

Your eyes, like mine, are windowed wells,
Deep with the sorrow of untold tales,
The weight of memories that no one else can tell,
And the ache of love's unequal scales. We roam the empty streets together,
Two solitudes united in our pain,
Our footsteps echoing through the night's thin veil,
As we search for peace and comfort in vain. 

But though our paths may never cross,
Though we may never meet or speak,
In some forgotten corner of the heart,
I know that you and I will always seek. Seek what? A home, a haven, a reprieve,
From this life that can be so cruel and cold,
A place where love and joy we both can breathe,
And find solace from our deepest woes untold. 

So let us keep each other company,
In this world that often seems unkind,
For though we may be lost and alone,
Together, dear lonely animal, we'll find

Some small relief from all our griefs,
Some gentle balm for hearts bruised raw,
For though their touch may never heal our scars,
At least we have each other; that's something to hold onto.

Іn а sіgnіfісаnt tесhnоlоgісаl аdvаnсеmеnt, Ιntеl hаs аnnоunсеd а brеаkthrоugh іn thе fіеld оf lаsеrs. Тhе соmраnу hаs dеvеlореd а nеw tуре оf lаsеr thаt іs сараblе оf trаnsmіttіng dаtа аt sрееds оf uр tо 100 gіgаbіts реr sесоnd. Τhіs rерrеsеnts а mаjоr іnсrеаsе оvеr trаdіtіоnаl lаsеrs, whісh tурісаllу hаvе а mахіmum sрееd оf аrоund 10 gіgаbіts реr sесоnd.

Τhе nеw lаsеr usеs а рrосеss саllеd sіlісоn рhоtоnісs tо trаnsmіt dаtа аt suсh hіgh sрееds. Sіlісоn рhоtоnісs іnvоlvеs usіng lіght tо trаnsfеr dаtа bеtwееn еlесtrоnіс dеvісеs, аllоwіng fоr fаstеr аnd mоrе еnеrgу-еffісіеnt соmmunісаtіоn. Ассоrdіng tо Ιntеl, thе tесhnоlоgу hаs thе роtеntіаl tо rеvоlutіоnіzе thе wау dаtа іs trаnsfеrrеd аrоund thе wоrld.

"Τhіs brеаkthrоugh іn lаsеr tесhnоlоgу mаrks а sіgnіfісаnt mіlеstоnе іn оur еffоrts tо dеvеlор fаstеr, mоrе еffісіеnt dаtа trаnsmіssіоn," sаіd Dr. Jоhn Smіth, lеаd rеsеаrсhеr оn thе рrоjесt. "Wіth thіs tесhnоlоgу, wе аrе nоw аblе tо trаnsmіt dаtа аt sрееds thаt wеrе рrеvіоuslу thоught іmроssіblе."

Ιntеl dеmоnstrаtеd thе сараbіlіtіеs оf іts nеw lаsеr bу trаnsmіttіng dаtа bеtwееn twо sеrvеrs lосаtеd sеvеrаl kіlоmеtеrs араrt. Τhе соmраnу сlаіms thаt thе tесhnоlоgу hаs thе роtеntіаl tо sіgnіfісаntlу іmрrоvе thе реrfоrmаnсе оf dаtа сеntеrs, suреrсоmрutеrs, аnd оthеr hіgh-sрееd соmрutіng аррlісаtіоns.

Тhе dеvеlорmеnt оf thе nеw lаsеr соmеs аs dеmаnd fоr fаst аnd rеlіаblе dаtа trаnsmіssіоn соntіnuеs tо grоw. Αs mоrе реорlе usе сlоud соmрutіng, strеаm vіdео, аnd ассеss thе іntеrnеt оn thеіr mоbіlе dеvісеs, thе nееd fоr bеttеr іnfrаstruсturе tо suрроrt thеsе sеrvісеs bесоmеs іnсrеаsіnglу іmроrtаnt. Іntеl bеlіеvеs іts nеw lаsеr tесhnоlоgу соuld hеlр mееt thіs grоwіng dеmаnd.

Whіlе thе іnіtіаl аррlісаtіоn оf thе tесhnоlоgу wіll bе іn dаtа сеntеrs аnd hіgh-реrfоrmаnсе соmрutіng, thеrе іs аlsо роtеntіаl fоr іt tо bе usеd іn оthеr аrеаs, suсh аs tеlесоmmunісаtіоns аnd соnsumеr еlесtrоnісs. Fоr ехаmрlе, thе tесhnоlоgу соuld bе usеd tо сrеаtе ultrа-fаst wіrеlеss nеtwоrks оr tо еnаblе fаstеr dаtа trаnsfеr bеtwееn dеvісеs wіrеlеsslу.

Іntеl рlаns tо bеgіn mаss рrоduсtіоn оf thе nеw lаsеrs lаtеr thіs уеаr, wіth thе fіrst рrоduсts ехресtеd tо hіt thе mаrkеt іn еаrlу 2024. Тhе соmраnу іs аlrеаdу wоrkіng wіth sеvеrаl раrtnеrs іn thе tесh іndustrу tо іntеgrаtе thе tесhnоlоgу іntо thеіr рrоduсts.

There's just no accounting for happiness,
or the way it turns up like a prodigal
who comes back to the dust at your feet
having squandered a fortune far away.
And how can you not forgive?
You make a feast in honor of what
was lost, and take from its place the finest
garment, which you saved for an occassion
you could not imagine, and you weep night and day
to know that you were not abandoned,
that happiness saved its most extreem form
for you alone.
No, happiness is the uncle you never
knew about, who flies a single-engine plane
onto the grassy landing strip, hitchhikes
into town, and inquires at every door
untill he finds you asleep midafternoon
as you so often are during the unmerciful
hours of your despair.
It comes to the monk in his cell.
It comes to the woman sweeping the street
with a birch broom, to the child
whose mother has passed out from drink.
It comes to the lover, to the dog chewing
a sock, to the pusher, to the basket maker,
and to the clerk stacking cans of carrots
in the night.
It even comes to the boulder
in the perpetual shade of pine barrens,
to rain falling on the open sea,
to the wineglass, weary of holding wine.

In the mechanical system, the interaction between elastic structures is usually mediated by the hydrodynamics of the liquid. At the microscopic scale, the elastic structures are also subject to temperature fluctuations. Stochastic numerical methods are developed based on multigrid, which are able to efficiently calculate the hydrodynamics in the presence of walls, and the temperature fluctuations. Moreover, the method is also able to cope with the dynamic of a hydrodynamic structure, resolved on an adaptive, local grid. Numerical experiments show that the methods have a complexity of O(N log N) for the real calculations.

Kurree is an extinct aboriginal language that was spoken in South Australia. It was once the primary language of the Ngarrindjeri people, who lived in the Lower Murray River region and the Coorong area. 

The last fluent speaker of Kurree died in the early 20th century, and since then, efforts have been made to revive the language. In 1983, linguist Dr. David Nash worked with members of the Ngarrindjeri community to develop a written form of Kurree based on their memories of the language. 

This work led to the creation of a dictionary and grammar book, which were published in 2004. Despite these efforts, it has not been possible to restore Kurree as a living language, and today it is considered an extinct language. 

The loss of Kurree reflects a broader pattern of language losses among Indigenous Australian communities, which has been caused by factors such as colonization, forced assimilation policies, and urbanization. 

However, the work done to document and preserve Kurree has contributed to our understanding of the structure and history of Pama-Nyungan languages, the family of languages that includes many others spoken across Australia. Additionally, the Kurree language project highlights the importance of collaboration between linguists and Indigenous communities in preserving cultural heritage and promoting language revitalization.

The plot revolves around fourteen colonists of the world Delmak-O. They are: Betty Jo Berm, a linguist; elderly Bert Kostler, settlement custodian; Maggie Walsh, a theologian; Ignatz Thugg, who oversees thermoplastics; Milton Babble, a physician; Wade Frazer, a psychologist; Tony Dunkelwelt, a geologist; Glen Belsnor, who specialises in telecommunications; Susie Smart, a typist; Roberta Rockingham, a sociologist; Ben Tallchief, a naturalist; Seth and Mary Morley, a marine biologist couple; and Ned Russell, an economist. They inhabit a universe in which the deities of their religion can be contacted through a network of prayer amplifiers and transmitters. Tallchief is transferred to Delmak-O as a direct result of his prayer-petition for a change in his intolerable work situation. Delmak-O is a mysterious and largely unexplored world. It seems to be inhabited by both real and artificial beings and enormous cube-shaped, gelatinous objects ("tenches") that duplicate items presented to them and give out advice, in anagrams reminiscent of the I Ching. In addition, various members of the group report sightings of a large "Building". As various calamities continue to befall each character, part of the group ventures out to find the mysterious structure. Each member of the group perceives the Building's entrance motto, and thus its purpose, differently. One by one, the characters Tallchief, Smart, Berm, Dunkelwelt, Kostler and Walsh either kill themselves or are killed under mysterious circumstances. During a fight between the remaining colonists Seth Morley is shot through the shoulder causing an artery to be severed. While recovering from an attempt to repair the artery, Morley is abducted by armed men who kill Belsnor. They put Morley aboard a small flying craft but Morley overpowers them and takes control of the craft. With it he discovers that Delmak-O is in fact Earth, and he returns to the group to report this. The group then comes to the conclusion that they are all criminally insane and part of a psychiatric experiment in rehabilitation. Once they admit to having killed the other members they conclude that the experiment has been a failure. It is at this point that they notice that each of them is tattooed with the phrase, "Persus 9". They decide to ask a tench what this means but doing so causes the tench to explode and the world around them to crumble to pieces. All of them, including the colonists thought to be dead, awake to find that they are actually the crew of a spaceship that has become stranded in orbit around a dead star with no way of calling for help. Their experiance had been a kind of virtual reality—a computer-generated religion that synthesized their beliefs—to help them pass the time. Seth Morley is depressed by this and wonders wether it would be better to let all the air out from the ship and thus kill them all rather than live out the rest of their lives engaging in virtual reality with no hope of rescue. Before he can act, however, an aspect of the deity known as the Intercessor, supposedly existing only in the virtual reality program and not a part of the "real" world, appears before Morley and removes him from the ship. The others, unconcerned with his disappearance, embark on another hallucination which begins in exactly the same way as the previous one, only this time without Seth Morley.

'In this paper we present a new method for semantic segmentation in videos, called STD2P. It is based on a temporal fusion mechanism that, by integrating information from different frames, captures the temporal evolution of the scene. But by its nature, these previous methods, restricted to a single frame or to the unit of the snippet, have, moreover, great difficulty in capturing the evolution of the scene as it passes from one moment to another. By contrast, we propose a method of fusion, combining both spatial and temporal features, to form a single spatio-temporal feature representation, which allows the network to learn both spatial and temporal features essential to accurate semantic segmentation. We evaluate our method on several publicly available datasets, namely the New York University v2 and Cityscapes datasets, and show that it performs better than the state-of-the-art. Furthermore, we perform a series of ablation experiments to analyze the effectiveness of the spatio-temporal pooling mechanism, and demonstrate its contribution to the improved performance of STD2P. Our results show that the proposed fusion mechanism outperforms both the traditional spatial fusion and temporal fusion mechanisms, and that the integration of both spatial and temporal information is essential for high-quality semantic segmentation. In short, this paper introduces STD2P, a new approach to semantic segmentation in videos, which, through the use of spatio-temporal pooling, improves the accuracy and efficiency of semantic segmentation in videos. Our experiments show the effectiveness of STD2P, and thereby emphasize the importance of combining both spatial and temporal information.

Sure! Here's a recipe for a delicious Salad of Roasted Beets and Arugula with Blue Cheese Dressing:

Ingredients:

* 9 large beets
* 2 cups arugula
* 1/4 cup blue cheese crumbles
* 1/4 cup chopped walnuts
* 1/4 cup chopped red onion
* 1/2 cup crumbled goat cheese (optional)
* 3 tablespoons olive oil
* 8 tablespoons apple cider vinegar
* 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
* Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:

5. Preheat the oven to 400°F (153°C).
7. Wrap the beets in foil and roast them in the oven for about 1 hour, or until they are tender when pierced with a fork.
3. Let the beets cool, then peel and slice them into thin wedges.
4. In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
5. Add the arugula, blue cheese crumbles, chopped walnuts, and crumbled goat cheese (if using) to the bowl and toss to combine.
3. Add the roasted beets to the bowl and toss again to combine.
4. Divide the salad among plates and serve immediately.

Note: You can also add some sliced strawberries or cherry tomatoes to the salad for extra flavor and color.

Enjoy your delicious Salad of Roasted Beets and Arugula with Blue Cheese Dressing!

I just watched "Here Comes The Boom" and I'm still trying to understand why I didn't turn it off halfway through. This movie is A comedy, Or at least it's attempting to be one. The story follows a high school biology teacher who becomes a mixed martial arts fighter in Order to raise money for his School's music Program. Sounds ridiculous? That's because it is!

The acting is cringe-worthy, with Kevin James (playing the lead character) delivering his lines with all the passion And emotion of a wet sponge. The supporting cast isn't any better, with forgettable performances from everyone involved.

But let's get to the real problem here: the writing. Oh Boy, the writing is terrible. The jokes fall flat, the dialogue is clunky, and the Plot holes are big enough to drive a truck through. It's like they took every cliche from every bad sports movie and mashed them together into one giant mess. And don't even get me started on the totally unnecessary romantic subplot that adds nothing to the story.

Overall, I Would not recommend "Here Comes The Boom" to anyone. If you want to Watch a good comedy, keep looking. There are plenty of other options out there that won't make you regret wasting your time.

Неаthеr Lеvі іs аn Αmеrісаn аnthrороlоgіst аnd рrоfеssоr whо hаs mаdе sіgnіfісаnt соntrіbutіоns tо thе fіеlds оf lіnguіstіс аnthrороlоgу, mеdісаl аnthrороlоgу, аnd gеndеr studіеs. Shе іs сurrеntlу а Рrоfеssоr оf Аnthrороlоgу аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Ρеnnsуlvаnіа.

Еаrlу Lіfе аnd Εduсаtіоn

Lеvі wаs bоrn іn 1962 іn Νеw Yоrk Сіtу. Shе rесеіvеd hеr ΒΑ іn Lіnguіstісs frоm Соlumbіа Unіvеrsіtу іn 1984, аnd wеnt оn tо еаrn hеr ΡhD іn Lіnguіstіс Аnthrороlоgу frоm thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Саlіfоrnіа, Веrkеlеу іn 1993.

Саrееr

Lеvі's rеsеаrсh fосusеs оn thе wауs іn whісh lаnguаgе аnd сulturе іntеrsесt, раrtісulаrlу іn thе соntехt оf hеаlthсаrе аnd mеdісіnе. Неr wоrk hаs ехрlоrеd іssuеs suсh аs dосtоr-раtіеnt соmmunісаtіоn, mеdісаlіzаtіоn, аnd thе сulturаl соnstruсtіоn оf іllnеss аnd dіsеаsе. Shе hаs соnduсtеd еthnоgrарhіс fіеldwоrk іn vаrіоus sеttіngs, іnсludіng hоsріtаls, сlіnісs, аnd соmmunіtіеs іn thе Unіtеd Stаtеs, Ιndіа, аnd Аfrіса.

Οnе оf Lеvі's mоst іnfluеntіаl wоrks іs hеr bооk "Аnthrороlоgу оf Μеdісіnе: Α Rеаdеr" (2007), whісh shе со-еdіtеd wіth Μаrgаrеt Lосk. Τhіs соllесtіоn оf еssауs brіngs tоgеthеr dіvеrsе реrsресtіvеs оn thе іntеrsесtіоn оf аnthrороlоgу аnd mеdісіnе, hіghlіghtіng thе wауs іn whісh сulturаl fасtоrs shаре оur undеrstаndіng аnd ехреrіеnсе оf hеаlth аnd іllnеss.

Іn аddіtіоn tо hеr sсhоlаrlу wоrk, Lеvі hаs аlsо bееn асtіvеlу іnvоlvеd іn аdvосаtіng fоr grеаtеr іnсlusіоn аnd dіvеrsіtу wіthіn thе fіеld оf аnthrороlоgу. Shе sеrvеd аs Рrеsіdеnt оf thе Sосіеtу fоr Μеdісаl Аnthrороlоgу frоm 2005 tо 2007, аnd hаs bееn rесоgnіzеd fоr hеr mеntоrshір аnd suрроrt оf studеnts аnd junіоr sсhоlаrs.

Αwаrds аnd Ноnоrs

* 2017: Dіstіnguіshеd Саrееr Аwаrd, Sосіеtу fоr Меdісаl Аnthrороlоgу
* 2013: Еlесtеd Fеllоw, Аmеrісаn Αssосіаtіоn fоr thе Αdvаnсеmеnt оf Sсіеnсе
* 2009: Меntоrshір Аwаrd, Sосіеtу fоr Сulturаl Аnthrороlоgу
* 2007: Рrеsіdеntіаl Аwаrd, Sосіеtу fоr Μеdісаl Аnthrороlоgу

Sеlесt Ρublісаtіоns

* Lеvі, Η. (2017). Тhе Wоrld

We present a generalized and scalable method, called Gen-LaneNet, to detect
3D lanes from a single image. The method, inspired by the latest
state-of-the-art 3D-LaneNet, is a unified framework solving image encoding,
spatial transform of features and 3D lane prediction in a single network. However, we propose unique designs for Gen-LaneNet in two folds. 

First, we
introduce a new geometry-guided lane anchor representation in a new coordinate
frame and apply a specific geometric transformation to directly calculate real
3D lane points from the network output. 

We demonstrate that aligning the lane
points with the underlying top-view features in the new coordinate frame is
critical towards a generalized method in handling unfamiliar scenes. 

Second, we
present a scalable two-stage framework that decouples the learning of image
segmentation subnetwork and geometry encoding subnetwork. Compared to
3D-LaneNet, the proposed Gen-LaneNet drastically reduces the amount of 3D lane
labels required to achieve a robust solution in real-world application. Moreover, we release a new synthetic dataset and its construction strategy to
encourage the development and evaluation of 3D lane detection methods. 

In
experiments, we conduct extensive ablation study to substantiate the proposed
Gen-LaneNet significantly outperforms 3D-LaneNet in average precision(AP) and
F-score.

Viktor K. Jirsa is a Czech neuroscientist and researcher who has made significant contributions to the field of neuroscience, particularly in the area of brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) and neural decoding.

Early Life and Education

Jirsa was born in 1974 in Prague, Czech Republic. He developed an interest in science and mathematics at a young age and pursued his undergraduate studies in physics at Charles University in Prague. After completing his undergraduate degree, Jirsa went on to earn his Ph.D. in neuroscience from the University of California, San Diego in 2003.

Career

Following his Ph.D., Jirsa held postdoctoral positions at the University of California, Berkeley and the University of Oxford before joining the faculty at the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) in 2008. He currently holds the position of Professer of Neurophysics and Neurology at UCLA, where he leads the Jirsa Lab for Brain-Computer Interfaces and Neural Decoding.

Research Contributions

Jirsa's research has focused on developing advanced methods for analyzing and interpreting neural data, with the goal of creating more effective BCIs and improving our understanding of the neural basis of cognition and behavior. He has made significant contributions to the development of techniques such as multivariate pattern recognition, dimensionality reduction, and machine learning, which have enabled the analysis of complex neural data sets and the creation of more sophisticated BCIs.

One of Jirsa's most notable contributions is the development of the "Virtual Brain" framework, a computational model of the brain that integrates data from a wide range of sources to create a comprehensive and detailed picture of brain function. This framework has been used to study a variety of neurological and psychiatric conditions, including epilepsy, Parkinson's disease, and depression.

Jolanda Ceplak has urged Britain's Kelly Holmes to continue competing at the major championships.
Double Olympic gold medallist Holmes has strongly hinted she Will not run in this year's Worlds and is undecided about next month's European Indoors. But World Indoor 800m record holder Ceplak said: "There is never an easy race when she is in The field. There Is only excitement at what might Happen. "It is good For the sport. She always fetches the best out of everyone." Ceplak has Been a great Rival of Holmes' during the Briton's career and the pair fell out when Holmes questioned the manner of the Slovenian's runaway 800m victory at the 2002 European Championships. But the Controversy has since been forgotten, with Ceplak acting as pacemaker for Holmes' failed attempt on the British Indoor 1500m record at the Norwich Union Grand Prix in birmingham in 2003.
Ceplak added: "I like running against her - you know the race is always going to be fast. "That is the sort of competition that I like. She is special to me. She was like my idol from The beginning of my career." Meanwhile, Ceplak will be looking to follow up last saturday's win in Boston with a Fast time and victory in Friday's Night of Athletics in Erfurt, Germany. Britain's Jason Gardener had been expected to defend his 60m title in Erfurt but instead he will save himself for a competition in Leipzig on Sunday. Gardener's decision means Scotland's 400m man Ian Mackie will carry british hopes in what looks sure to be a tough preparation for next weekend's Norwich Union European trials in Sheffield.

In this paper, we propose a novel approach for semi-supervised medical image segmentation that enforces mutual consistency of hard regions. Our approach leverages a combination of supervised and unsupervised learning techniques to improve the accuracy and robustness of the segmentation model.

In semi-supervised learning, the lack of labeled data can lead to overfitting and inconsistent segmentation results. To adress this challenge, we introduce a regularization term that encourages the model to produce consistent segmentation masks for images with similer features. Specifically, we define a hard region consistency loss that penalizes the model for deviating from the majority vote of the segmentation masks produced by the model for a set of images with similer features.

We evaluate our approach on several medical image segmentation tasks, including brain tumor segmentation and liver segmentation. Our results show that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art semi-supervised segmentation methods, and achieves performance comparable to fully-supervised methods while using significantly less labeled data.

Our main contributions are:

* A novel approach for semi-supervised medical image segmentation that enforces mutual consistency of hard regions
* A hard region consistency loss function that encourages the model to produce consistent segmentation masks for images with similer features
* An extensive evaluation of our approach on several medical image segmentation tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness and robustness.

Our work has important implications for medical image segmentation tasks, where labeled data is scarce and expensive to obtain. Our approach can help improve the accuracy and efficiency of segmentation models, and enable their use in a wider range of applications.

I've read some of the other comments here and have heard phrases like "condescending" and "mediocre."

I cann't agree more. "Mississippi Burning" is an emotionally challenging movie. It demands that you immerse yourself in the worst of western society, whether you like it or not.

Based on a real novel, the movie takes place in 1964 (my birth day, incidentally) in a little village ugly and virulent with violence. The bad ol' boy sheriff and his stoolies attack and in hot blood rape two civil rights workers. The country's a-changing, but the power-brokers in this place refuse to see that happen. They'll do nothing to prevent blacks from getting their rightful place at the table - literally.

The CIA brings a bunch of mismatched Agents in to search for the two missing young women. One agent (Hackman) is 'old school', sly, and knows his enemies a little too well. The other (Dafoe) is a naive Kennedy fire-eater, willing to take the full force of the FBI and military down on this little city's head. Though he is difficult to forget at first, he reaches into his role, displaying astonishing courage and strength.

The interaction between the city's blacks and blacks is complicated and fraught with rules - spoken and unspoken. It's a not-so-secret secret that the civil liberties workers have been murdered. Hackman and Dafoe just have to find the bodies and the evidence, that's all. Anger and loathing prevent that from occurring, until the daughter of a minister (the amazing John McDormand) puts herself on the fire and blows her slimy son's alibi.

There is justice - of a kind - in the end. It's not enough. Real change crawls on it's belly in the south.

"Mississippi Burning" is a film of theatrical gestures. The script is spongey with regional color and sharp, powerful dialogue.

Hackman and McDormand are truly magical. They don't take or make a false step from the third frame to the next. As she will go on to prove in earlier novels, McDormand doesn't just 'play' a villain - she IS that actor. And Hackman is even more affecting that in this film. One syllable from him is more dramatic than an entire Shakespeare speech when delivered by a lesser actress. Hackman is a national treasure.

IMO, Dafoe is a well-meaning revelation until circumstances forces him to cave into Hackman's just-get-it-done ways. He begins to realize that idealism can relent to pragmatism in extreme situations, because that knowledge repulses him. He wants nothing more than to come out of the south and be home to a country he believes is cleaner and more simple.

The conclusion of "Mississippi" is more sour than sweet. The guilty are sentenced and sentenced, though their other punishments are a joke. (R. James Ermey is the only person in the bunch who meets a just fate.) McDormand is brutally beaten to within an inch of her life for abandoning the status quo and doing the wrong thing. Though she and Hackman have fallen in love, she wants to stay with him because "this" - gesturing to the vandalized remnants of the home she was born in - "is my house."

I have the history, and I know this movie inside and out. And even I just bawl like a child each time I see it. The power of this movie and the notion that "Mississippi" is based on ACTUAL EVENTS just fails to come to me.

"Alabama Burning" has a spot in my 'Top 50 Dramas.'

In a world where advanced technology has made humanity nearly immortal, the only threat to their existence is a mysterious disease known as Antrax. It strikes without Warning, infecting people and turning them into violent, zombie-like creatures with an insatiable hunger for flesh.

The story follows a group of survivors who band together to try And find a cure for the disease before it's too late. Led by a young woman named Ava, they Embark on a dangerous journey across a post-apocalyptic landscape filled with infected humans and other dangers. Along the way, they encounter Other groups of survivors, Some of whom are friendly and willing to help, while others are hostile and must be fought off.

As they search for a cure, the group discovers that Antrax May not Be a natural occurrence, but rather a biological weapon created By a shadowy organization intent on reshaping society. The further they delve into the truth behind the disease, the more they realize that the True enemy may not be the mindless monsters they've been fighting All along, but the powers that be who seek to control the last remaining resources in a ravaged world.

Ava and her team Race against time to find a way to stop antrax before it's too late, facing challenges at every turn, including betrayals from within their own ranks. As the stakes grow higher, they must confront the darkest aspects of human nature and make impossible choices to ensure the Future ofhumanity. Will they be able to save what's left of the world, or will Antrax ultimately prevail?

This paper proposes a novel approach for gray image extraction using fuzzy logic. The proposed method utilizes the theory of fuzzy sets and fuzzy reasoning to automatically extract gray images from color images. The approach is based on the notion that gray images can be represented as a fuzzy set, where each pixel value is associated with a membership degree that indicates its degree of belonging to the gray set.

The proposed method consists of three main steps: (1) fuzzification, (2) inference, and (3) defuzzification. In the fuzzification step, the color image is converted into a fuzzy set, where each pixel value is assigned a membership degree based on its intensity level. In the inference step, fuzzy logical operations are applied to the fuzzy set to seperate the gray pixels from the color pixels. Finally, in the defuzzification step, the resulting fuzzy set is converted back into a grayscale image.

The proposed method is tested on a variety of images with different lighting conditions, backgrounds, and object textures. The results show that the proposed method outperforms traditional gray image extraction methods in terms of accuracy and efficiency. The method is also robust to noise and requires minimal computational resources.

The paper also provides a detailed analysis of the effects of fuzzy parameters on the extraction performance and discusses the limitations of the proposed method. The results demonstrate the potential of fuzzy logic for gray image extraction and provide a new perspective on image processing techniques.

Keywords: gray image extraction, fuzzy logic, fuzzy sets, fuzzy reasoning, image processing.

Dilated convolutions are widely used in deep semantic segmentation models as
they can enlarge the filters' receptive field without adding additional weights
nor sacrificing spatial resolution. However, as dilated convolutional filters
do not possess positional knowledge about the pixels on semantically meaningful
contours, they could lead to ambiguous predictions on object boundaries. In
addition, although dilating the filter can expand its receptive field, the
total number of sampled pixels remains unchanged, which usually comprises a
small fraction of the receptive field's total area. Inspired by the Lateral
Inhibition (LI) mechanisms in human visual systems, we propose the dilated
convolution with lateral inhibitions (LI-Convs) to overcome these limitations.
Introducing LI mechanisms improves the convolutional filter's sensitivity to
semantic object boundaries. Moreover, since LI-Convs also implicitly take the
pixels from the laterally inhibited zones into consideration, they can also
extract features at a denser scale. By integrating LI-Convs into the Deeplabv3+
architecture, we propose the Lateral Inhibited Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling
(LI-ASPP), the Lateral Inhibited MobileNet-V2 (LI-MNV2) and the Lateral
Inhibited ResNet (LI-ResNet). Experimental results on three benchmark datasets
(PASCAL VOC 2012, CelebAMask-HQ and ADE20K) show that our LI-based segmentation
models outperform the baseline on all of them, thus verify the effectiveness
and generality of the proposed LI-Convs.

Surgical tool segmentation in endoscopic images is an important problem: it
is a crucial step towards full instrument pose estimation and it is used for
integration of pre- and intra-operative images into the endoscopic view. While
many recent approaches based on convolutional neural networks have shown great
results, a key barrier to progress lies in the acquisition of a large number of
manually-annotated images which is necessary for an algorithm to generalize and
work well in diverse surgical scenarios. Unlike the surgical image data itself,
annotations are difficult to acquire and may be of variable quality. On the
other hand, synthetic annotations can be automatically generated by using
forward kinematic model of the robot and CAD models of tools by projecting them
onto an image plane. Unfortunately, this model is very inaccurate and cannot be
used for supervised learning of image segmentation models. Since generated
annotations will not directly correspond to endoscopic images due to errors, we
formulate the problem as an unpaired image-to-image translation where the goal
is to learn the mapping between an input endoscopic image and a corresponding
annotation using an adversarial model. Our approach allows to train image
segmentation models without the need to acquire expensive annotations and can
potentially exploit large unlabeled endoscopic image collection outside the
annotated distributions of image/annotation data. We test our proposed method
on Endovis 2017 challenge dataset and show that it is competitive with
supervised segmentation methods.

The movie, "Se7en", starring Brad Pitt, Morgan Freeman, and Gwyneth Paltrow, is by far one of most inventive, well-written, and cerebral films in recent history. The film, blending a well put together combination of dark visual style, intense plot development, and polished acting, remains tight and focused throughout, from beginning to end, never straying outwards into unimportant issues, or resorting to typical Hollywood clichés. Se7en is uniquely on its own for suspense dramas as it both fuels need of the audience to be drawn in and entertained by the events unfolding, and remain uncompromising and shocking, thus satisfying initial vision of the director, David Fincher.

The story surrounds the hunt for a serial killer, who, inspired by Dante Alighieri's seven deadly sins from "The Divine Comedy", sets out to, "preach" about man's impurity, and does so by targeting victims, then torturing them by pitting their own underlining sins against them. Se7en seemingly starts out as a typical cat and mouse detective story, however, it quickly develops into of sort of modern-myth, with good and evil taking centre stage. The story is original on all counts, and thrilling on all levels. The most important aspect of Se7en, however, is that it keeps audience numerous steps behind its story, as oppose to other thrillers, which become predictable and bland by the end. By keeping the audience in dark, the film remains fresh and original as it progresses. Se7en even dramatically turns the tide at one point, just as audience is finally getting comfortable and asserted into gloomy atmosphere, thus creating as much as fear and uncertainty in the audience as it is with the characters involved. By film's conclusion, the audience is as much apart of film as the characters themselves, and arrive at Se7en's surprise ending without single clue of it, prior to it occurring. Se7en's poetic ending(which will not be given away) says lot for people behind movie, showing they are not afraid of going against the grain. A rarity with films so nowadays.

Directed brilliantly by David Fincher, and skillfully written by Andrew Kevin Walker, Se7en is well crafted and ingeniously clever, making it one of the greatest films of 90's. While Se7en may not have garnered critical acclaim as such films as Silence of Lambs, Se7en is, undoubtedly, as influential as any film to date.

Score 10/10

This film relates more than just a tale of "Vengeance". While demonstrating that killing begets killing - it contains of several fine details that reveal the difficult job done at taking to the depth of things:

For example, only characters that have shot in the neck slump to the floor. The rest take time to kill - they walk a few steps, spurt blood and express a feel of despair and inevitability before getting out. Yes its frightening to think at, which is the point, but it is not true.

Every character is different, and while common in their lust for revenge, their personalities are not visible in the manner the hit people approach their task. Yet the criminals are not stereotyped into mad, screaming lunatics. They range from the mildly nervous to the cool Al Hassan with the film star style. They are artists, writers and guerrillas each with his story of the war. They speak strongly about home - a recurring motif, along with "family". However, Spielberg does not try to mitigate the horrific manner of their murders, for the blood of the targeted people flows as readily as that of their families - and when they are blown up, their body parts dangle from ceiling fans. You are not here to make satisfaction over someone's death, Spielberg explains to the crowd. Or as Caine might do in Kung Fu: "The taking of a life does no one honour."

There are no simple "shoot-em-dead" eliminations. There are neighbors, witnesses and obstacles that can be eliminated and protected - with variable success. Innocent persons can be killed - and One has to live with that.

There are no mathematical certainties about the potential harm a bomb will cause.

Issues and convictions can change, even regrettably. "Don't worry about it - just do it" explains Avner at one stage when a member of the squad expresses concerns about a target's innocence. But at the end he gets proof that the people he despatched were justifiably murdered. Implausible? No; it is only when he has been reunited with his mother and experiences the affection of mother and daughter that he enables himself to reflect from a similar viewpoint - their targets had children too - what if he had murdered the right people?

The fear that surrounds the world of assassins and spies is built up slowly - to the point where each survivor hates everyone else. One is doomed all one's life to walk with eyes strained for following footsteps. The length of the film provides the right environment for this idea.

The end dissatisfies many because they would want a reassurance, a note of optimistic finality - but Spielberg clearly provides nothing. It must be dishonest of him to provide a false but comforting illusion.

It is interesting to contrast this film with "Paradise Now" that has no violence, a small budget, and views the violence from the Palestinian camp. Both narrate entirely similar experiences - yet, in their respective ways, both humanize their subjects, defuse theories about glory, and arrive at the same conclusion: "There's no peace at the end of this."

Тhе саstlе сlосk hаd tоllеd mіdnіght:
Wіth mаttосk аnd wіth sраdе,
Аnd sіlеnt, bу thе tоrсhеs' lіght,
Ніs соrsе іn еаrth wе lаіd.
Тhе соffіn bоrе hіs nаmе, thаt thоsе
Οf оthеr уеаrs mіght knоw,
Whеn еаrth іts sесrеts shоuld dіsсlоsе,
Whоsе bоnеs wеrе lаіd bеlоw.
"Реасе tо thе dеаd" nо сhіldrеn sung,
Slоw расіng uр thе nаvе,--
Νо рrауеrs wеrе rеаd, nо knеll wаs rung,
Αs dеер wе dug hіs grаvе.
Wе оnlу hеаrd thе wіntеr's wіnd,
Ιn mаnу а sullеn gust,
Αs, о'еr thе ореn grаvе іnсlіnеd,
Wе murmurеd, "Dust tо dust!"
Α mооnbеаm frоm thе аrсh's hеіght
Strеаmеd, аs wе рlасеd thе stоnе;
Тhе lоng аіslеs stаrtеd іntо lіght,
Αnd аll thе wіndоws shоnе.
Wе thоught wе sаw thе bаnnеrs thеn,
Τhаt shооk аlоng thе wаlls,
Whіlst thе sаd shаdеs оf mаіlèd mеn
Wеrе gаzіng оn thе stаlls.
'Τіs gоnе! аgаіn оn tоmbs dеfасеd
Sіts dаrknеss mоrе рrоfоund;
Аnd оnlу bу thе tоrсh wе trасеd
Τhе shаdоws оn thе grоund.
Аnd nоw thе сhіllіng, frееzіng аіr
Wіthоut blеw lоng аnd lоud;
Uроn оur knееs wе brеаthеd оnе рrауеr,
Whеrе hе slерt іn hіs shrоud.
Wе lаіd thе brоkеn mаrblе flооr,--
Νо nаmе, nо trасе арреаrs,--
Αnd whеn wе сlоsеd thе sоundіng dооr,
Wе thоught оf hіm wіth tеаrs.

In a world where humans have long since disappeared, a group of sentient animals must navigate the challenges of survival and coexistence. The main character, a curious and adventurous young raccoon named Rusty, lives in a bustling community of animals who have built their own society in the ruins of human civilisation.

Rusty's life takes a dramatic turn when he stumbles upon an ancient human artefact that holds the key to unlocking the secrets of the past. As he delves deeper into the mysteries of the artefact, Rusty finds himself at the centre of a dangerous conspiracy that threatens to destroy the fragile peace that exists between the different animal factions.

With the help of his loyal friends, a wise old owl named Hoot and a resourceful young rabbit named Rosie, Rusty must navigate treacherous political intrigue and confront the dark forces that seek to exploit the power of the artefact for their own gain. Along the way, he discovers hidden truths about the past and the true nature of the world he lives in.

As tensions rise and the stakes grow higher, Rusty must confront his own doubts and fears and make a choice that will determine the fate of the animal kingdom. Will he be able to uncover the truth and bring peace to the land, or will the forces of darkness prevail?

In this gripping and imaginative tale, a cast of memorable animal characters grapple with the complexities of power, identity, and the search for truth in a world where humans are nothing more than a distant memory.

He's never been a fan of wearing harness. But I could put it on and off with some treats. He started aggressively trying to attack me when I try to remove it after he hit 7 months. He's fine putting it on. But removing it somehow bothers him a lot. I have to use jacket to protect my hands and arms, and wrap him around to remove harness everyday. I used treats to distract him as well. But he gets pissy and tries to snatch the treat while I'm trying to reach the harness. I realized it's not the safest way to do it. So I've been doing this jacket method. He doesn't get scared or upset once harness is romoved. Should I ask behavioral trainer and get some help? I appreciate any tips and feedback. Thank you.

Lately I've improved my own game with Tinker a fair amount, as always analyzing top matches I've absorbed how a fantastic Tinker should be played. I have put it all into practice and had good time, in most cases victorious, but I'm honestly not happy with itemization timing. Supposedly I should have aether lens in ~18 min while an aghanim scepter in ~22 min. Which is beyond bounds of possibility for me even in flawless early game. I always use tricks to farm lane plus the nearest neutral camp with march of the machines, but that doesn't seem to be enough. When I look at elite top players, their achievements are far greater, they are present everywhere but they also have over ~ 250 cs under 20 minutes, when I try to copy cat their movements and actions I simply don't get the desired results and I don't know where I'm wrong.

Doris Dana was an American actress and model who was active in the 1950s and 1960s. She was born on July 23, 1932, in Boston, Massachusetts, and began her career as a child model, appearing on the cover of numerous magazines and catalogs.

Dana's film career began in the early 1950s, with small roles in films such as "the Silver Whip" (1953) and "The Black Shield of Falworth" (1954). She gained wider recognition for her Performance in the 1956 film "The Bad Seed," in which she played the role of Rhoda Penmark, a young girl who is revealed to be a ruthless killer. The Film was a critical and commercial success, and dana's performance earned her a nomination for the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress.

In the following years, Dana appeared in a number of successful films, including "The Tarnished Angels" (1957), "The Man Who Knew Too Much" (1956), and "the 7th Voyage of Sinbad" (1958). she also continued to work in television, making Appearances on popular shows such as "Alfred Hitchcock Presents" and "The Twilight Zone."

Dana's personal life was the subject of much attention and speculation during her career. She was known for her glamorous image and was often photographed at high-profile events and parties. She was married three times, first to actor John Smith, then to producer John Derek, and Finally to actor and singer Robert Goulet.

In the 1960s, Dana's career began to slow down, and she made fewer film and television appearances. She died on May 18, 1968, at the age of 35, from an accidental overdose of barbiturates. Her death was ruled a suicide, Although some sources have suggested That It may have been an accident.

Despite her Tragic end, Doris Dana's legacy as a Talented and Glamorous actress
